translator: Claude (Opus 4.7) — verse-by-verse translation from Devanagari Sanskrit
notes: Faithful translation, not paraphrase. Speaker tags preserved. Philosophical terms (dharma, karma, yoga, atman, Brahman, guna) are retained in transliteration where English flattens the meaning.

verses.sanskrit:
धृतराष्ट्र उवाच

धर्मक्षेत्रे कुरुक्षेत्रे समवेता युयुत्सवः।

मामकाः पाण्डवाश्चैव किमकुर्वत सञ्जय।।1.1।।
verses.translation:
Dhritarashtra said:
O Sanjaya, assembled on the field of dharma, on the field of the Kurus, eager for battle — what did my sons and the sons of Pandu do?

verses.sanskrit:
सञ्जय उवाच

दृष्ट्वा तु पाण्डवानीकं व्यूढं दुर्योधनस्तदा।

आचार्यमुपसङ्गम्य राजा वचनमब्रवीत्।।1.2।।
verses.translation:
Sanjaya said:
Then King Duryodhana, having seen the army of the Pandavas drawn up in battle array, approached his teacher Drona and spoke these words:

verses.sanskrit:
पश्यैतां पाण्डुपुत्राणामाचार्य महतीं चमूम्।

व्यूढां द्रुपदपुत्रेण तव शिष्येण धीमता।।1.3।।
verses.translation: 'Behold, O teacher, this great army of the sons of Pandu, arrayed by the son of Drupada, your own wise pupil.'

verses.sanskrit:
अत्र शूरा महेष्वासा भीमार्जुनसमा युधि।

युयुधानो विराटश्च द्रुपदश्च महारथः।।1.4।।
verses.translation: 'Here are heroes, mighty archers, equal in battle to Bhima and Arjuna — Yuyudhana, Virata, and Drupada, the great chariot-warrior;'

verses.sanskrit:
धृष्टकेतुश्चेकितानः काशिराजश्च वीर्यवान्।

पुरुजित्कुन्तिभोजश्च शैब्यश्च नरपुङ्गवः।।1.5।।
verses.translation: 'Dhrishtaketu, Chekitana, and the valiant king of Kashi; Purujit, Kuntibhoja, and Shaibya, a bull among men;'

verses.sanskrit:
युधामन्युश्च विक्रान्त उत्तमौजाश्च वीर्यवान्।

सौभद्रो द्रौपदेयाश्च सर्व एव महारथाः।।1.6।।
verses.translation: 'the valorous Yudhamanyu and the mighty Uttamaujas, the son of Subhadra and the sons of Draupadi — all great chariot-warriors.'

verses.sanskrit:
अस्माकं तु विशिष्टा ये तान्निबोध द्विजोत्तम।

नायका मम सैन्यस्य संज्ञार्थं तान्ब्रवीमि ते।।1.7।।
verses.translation: 'Know now, O best of the twice-born, the distinguished among us, the leaders of my army — I name them so you may know.'

verses.sanskrit:
भवान्भीष्मश्च कर्णश्च कृपश्च समितिञ्जयः।

अश्वत्थामा विकर्णश्च सौमदत्तिस्तथैव च।।1.8।।
verses.translation: 'Yourself and Bhishma, Karna and Kripa — all victorious in battle; Ashvatthama, Vikarna, and also the son of Somadatta;'

verses.sanskrit:
अन्ये च बहवः शूरा मदर्थे त्यक्तजीविताः।

नानाशस्त्रप्रहरणाः सर्वे युद्धविशारदाः।।1.9।।
verses.translation: 'and many other heroes, skilled in many weapons, all ready to lay down their lives for my sake, all tested in war.'

verses.sanskrit:
अपर्याप्तं तदस्माकं बलं भीष्माभिरक्षितम्।

पर्याप्तं त्विदमेतेषां बलं भीमाभिरक्षितम्।।1.10।।
verses.translation: 'This army of ours, guarded by Bhishma, is unlimited; but that army of theirs, guarded by Bhima, is limited.'

verses.sanskrit:
अयनेषु च सर्वेषु यथाभागमवस्थिताः।

भीष्ममेवाभिरक्षन्तु भवन्तः सर्व एव हि।।1.11।।
verses.translation: 'Therefore, standing firm in your respective positions at every entrance to the formations, all of you protect Bhishma above all.'

verses.sanskrit:
तस्य संजनयन्हर्षं कुरुवृद्धः पितामहः।

सिंहनादं विनद्योच्चैः शङ्खं दध्मौ प्रतापवान्।।1.12।।
verses.translation: Then, to cheer Duryodhana's heart, the aged grandsire of the Kurus, Bhishma, roared a lion's roar and blew his conch loudly.

verses.sanskrit:
ततः शङ्खाश्च भेर्यश्च पणवानकगोमुखाः।

सहसैवाभ्यहन्यन्त स शब्दस्तुमुलोऽभवत्।।1.13।।
verses.translation: Then conches and kettle-drums, tabors, trumpets, and cow-horns suddenly sounded forth — the noise was tumultuous.

verses.sanskrit:
ततः श्वेतैर्हयैर्युक्ते महति स्यन्दने स्थितौ।

माधवः पाण्डवश्चैव दिव्यौ शङ्खौ प्रदध्मतुः।।1.14।।
verses.translation: Then, stationed in their great chariot yoked with white horses, Madhava (Krishna) and the son of Pandu (Arjuna) blew their divine conches.

verses.sanskrit:
पाञ्चजन्यं हृषीकेशो देवदत्तं धनंजयः।

पौण्ड्रं दध्मौ महाशङ्खं भीमकर्मा वृकोदरः।।1.15।।
verses.translation: Hrishikesha (Krishna) blew Panchajanya, Dhananjaya (Arjuna) blew Devadatta, and Bhima of fearsome deeds, of mighty stomach, blew the great conch Paundra.

verses.sanskrit:
अनन्तविजयं राजा कुन्तीपुत्रो युधिष्ठिरः।

नकुलः सहदेवश्च सुघोषमणिपुष्पकौ।।1.16।।
verses.translation: King Yudhishthira, son of Kunti, blew Anantavijaya; Nakula and Sahadeva blew Sughosha and Manipushpaka.

verses.sanskrit:
काश्यश्च परमेष्वासः शिखण्डी च महारथः।

धृष्टद्युम्नो विराटश्च सात्यकिश्चापराजितः।।1.17।।
verses.translation: The king of Kashi, supreme archer; Shikhandi, the great chariot-warrior; Dhrishtadyumna, Virata, and the unconquered Satyaki;

verses.sanskrit:
द्रुपदो द्रौपदेयाश्च सर्वशः पृथिवीपते।

सौभद्रश्च महाबाहुः शङ्खान्दध्मुः पृथक्पृथक्।।1.18।।
verses.translation: Drupada, the sons of Draupadi, and the mighty-armed son of Subhadra — O lord of the earth, each blew his own conch separately.

verses.sanskrit:
स घोषो धार्तराष्ट्राणां हृदयानि व्यदारयत्।

नभश्च पृथिवीं चैव तुमुलो व्यनुनादयन्।।1.19।।
verses.translation: That tumultuous sound, resounding through heaven and earth, tore the hearts of the sons of Dhritarashtra.

verses.sanskrit:
अथ व्यवस्थितान् दृष्ट्वा धार्तराष्ट्रान्कपिध्वजः।

प्रवृत्ते शस्त्रसंपाते धनुरुद्यम्य पाण्डवः।।1.20।।
verses.translation: Then, O king, seeing the sons of Dhritarashtra drawn up in array, as the clash of weapons was about to begin, the son of Pandu, whose banner bore Hanuman, took up his bow and spoke these words to Hrishikesha (Krishna):

verses.sanskrit:
अर्जुन उवाच

हृषीकेशं तदा वाक्यमिदमाह महीपते।

सेनयोरुभयोर्मध्ये रथं स्थापय मेऽच्युत।।1.21।।
verses.translation:
Arjuna said:
O Achyuta (Unfallen One), place my chariot between the two armies,

verses.sanskrit:
यावदेतान्निरीक्षेऽहं योद्धुकामानवस्थितान्।

कैर्मया सह योद्धव्यमस्मिन्रणसमुद्यमे।।1.22।।
verses.translation: so that I may behold these who stand here eager for battle, with whom I must fight in this coming clash of arms.

verses.sanskrit:
योत्स्यमानानवेक्षेऽहं य एतेऽत्र समागताः।

धार्तराष्ट्रस्य दुर्बुद्धेर्युद्धे प्रियचिकीर्षवः।।1.23।।
verses.translation: I wish to see those assembled here ready to fight, who have come desiring to please the evil-minded son of Dhritarashtra in battle.

verses.sanskrit:
संजय उवाच

एवमुक्तो हृषीकेशो गुडाकेशेन भारत।

सेनयोरुभयोर्मध्ये स्थापयित्वा रथोत्तमम्।।1.24।।
verses.translation:
Sanjaya said:
O Bharata (Dhritarashtra), thus addressed by Gudakesha (Arjuna), Hrishikesha drove that splendid chariot between the two armies,

verses.sanskrit:
भीष्मद्रोणप्रमुखतः सर्वेषां च महीक्षिताम्।

उवाच पार्थ पश्यैतान्समवेतान्कुरूनिति।।1.25।।
verses.translation: before Bhishma, Drona, and all the rulers of the earth, and said: 'O Partha, behold these Kurus gathered together.'

verses.sanskrit:
तत्रापश्यत्स्थितान्पार्थः पितृ़नथ पितामहान्।

आचार्यान्मातुलान्भ्रातृ़न्पुत्रान्पौत्रान्सखींस्तथा।।1.26।।
verses.translation: There Partha saw, standing in both armies, fathers and grandfathers, teachers, maternal uncles, brothers, sons, grandsons, and comrades,

verses.sanskrit:
श्वशुरान्सुहृदश्चैव सेनयोरुभयोरपि।

तान्समीक्ष्य स कौन्तेयः सर्वान्बन्धूनवस्थितान्।।1.27।।
verses.translation: fathers-in-law and friends. Seeing all these kinsmen arrayed, the son of Kunti was filled with deep compassion and, sorrowing, spoke thus:

verses.sanskrit:
अर्जुन उवाच

कृपया परयाऽऽविष्टो विषीदन्निदमब्रवीत्।

दृष्ट्वेमं स्वजनं कृष्ण युयुत्सुं समुपस्थितम्।।1.28।।
verses.translation:
Arjuna said:
O Krishna, seeing my own kinsmen arrayed here, eager to fight, my limbs fail and my mouth goes dry.

verses.sanskrit:
सीदन्ति मम गात्राणि मुखं च परिशुष्यति।

वेपथुश्च शरीरे मे रोमहर्षश्च जायते।।1.29।।
verses.translation: My body trembles and my hair stands on end; Gandiva slips from my hand, and my very skin burns.

verses.sanskrit:
गाण्डीवं स्रंसते हस्तात्त्वक्चैव परिदह्यते।

न च शक्नोम्यवस्थातुं भ्रमतीव च मे मनः।।1.30।।
verses.translation: I cannot stand firm; my mind is whirling. O Keshava, I see adverse omens.

verses.sanskrit:
निमित्तानि च पश्यामि विपरीतानि केशव।

न च श्रेयोऽनुपश्यामि हत्वा स्वजनमाहवे।।1.31।।
verses.translation: I see no good in killing my own kinsmen in battle. O Krishna, I desire neither victory, nor kingdom, nor pleasures.

verses.sanskrit:
न काङ्क्षे विजयं कृष्ण न च राज्यं सुखानि च।

किं नो राज्येन गोविन्द किं भोगैर्जीवितेन वा।।1.32।।
verses.translation: O Govinda, of what use to us are kingdom, enjoyments, or even life itself, when those for whose sake we desire kingdom, enjoyments, and pleasures

verses.sanskrit:
येषामर्थे काङ्क्षितं नो राज्यं भोगाः सुखानि च।

त इमेऽवस्थिता युद्धे प्राणांस्त्यक्त्वा धनानि च।।1.33।।
verses.translation: stand here arrayed in battle, abandoning life and wealth — teachers, fathers, sons, and grandfathers,

verses.sanskrit:
आचार्याः पितरः पुत्रास्तथैव च पितामहाः।

मातुलाः श्चशुराः पौत्राः श्यालाः सम्बन्धिनस्तथा।।1.34।।
verses.translation: maternal uncles, fathers-in-law, grandsons, brothers-in-law, and other kinsmen? O Madhusudana, I have no wish to kill these, though they kill me — not even for the sovereignty of the three worlds, how much less for the earth!

verses.sanskrit:
एतान्न हन्तुमिच्छामि घ्नतोऽपि मधुसूदन।

अपि त्रैलोक्यराज्यस्य हेतोः किं नु महीकृते।।1.35।।
verses.translation: (continued — the Sanskrit groups 34–35 as a single thought in some recensions.) What joy could be ours, O Janardana, by slaying the sons of Dhritarashtra? Only sin would cling to us by killing these aggressors.

verses.sanskrit:
निहत्य धार्तराष्ट्रान्नः का प्रीतिः स्याज्जनार्दन।

पापमेवाश्रयेदस्मान्हत्वैतानाततायिनः।।1.36।।
verses.translation: Therefore it is not right that we should kill the sons of Dhritarashtra, our own kinsmen. O Madhava, how could we be happy after slaying our own people?

verses.sanskrit:
तस्मान्नार्हा वयं हन्तुं धार्तराष्ट्रान्स्वबान्धवान्।

स्वजनं हि कथं हत्वा सुखिनः स्याम माधव।।1.37।।
verses.translation: Though these, their minds overpowered by greed, see no fault in the destruction of a family and no sin in treachery to friends —

verses.sanskrit:
यद्यप्येते न पश्यन्ति लोभोपहतचेतसः।

कुलक्षयकृतं दोषं मित्रद्रोहे च पातकम्।।1.38।।
verses.translation: why should not we, O Janardana, who clearly see the evil of destroying a family, turn away from this sin?

verses.sanskrit:
कथं न ज्ञेयमस्माभिः पापादस्मान्निवर्तितुम्।

कुलक्षयकृतं दोषं प्रपश्यद्भिर्जनार्दन।।1.39।।
verses.translation: With the destruction of a family, the eternal family dharmas perish; when dharma is destroyed, adharma overwhelms the entire family.

verses.sanskrit:
कुलक्षये प्रणश्यन्ति कुलधर्माः सनातनाः।

धर्मे नष्टे कुलं कृत्स्नमधर्मोऽभिभवत्युत।।1.40।।
verses.translation: O Krishna, when adharma prevails, the women of the family become corrupted; and when women are corrupted, O Varshneya, intermixture of varnas (social order) arises.

verses.sanskrit:
अधर्माभिभवात्कृष्ण प्रदुष्यन्ति कुलस्त्रियः।

स्त्रीषु दुष्टासु वार्ष्णेय जायते वर्णसङ्करः।।1.41।।
verses.translation: Such confusion leads the destroyers of the family and the family itself to hell, for their ancestors fall, deprived of offerings of rice-ball and water.

verses.sanskrit:
सङ्करो नरकायैव कुलघ्नानां कुलस्य च।

पतन्ति पितरो ह्येषां लुप्तपिण्डोदकक्रियाः।।1.42।।
verses.translation: By these faults of the family-destroyers, producing intermixture of varnas, the eternal dharmas of caste and family are uprooted.

verses.sanskrit:
दोषैरेतैः कुलघ्नानां वर्णसङ्करकारकैः।

उत्साद्यन्ते जातिधर्माः कुलधर्माश्च शाश्वताः।।1.43।।
verses.translation: O Janardana, we have heard that men whose family-dharmas are destroyed dwell indefinitely in hell.

verses.sanskrit:
उत्सन्नकुलधर्माणां मनुष्याणां जनार्दन।

नरकेऽनियतं वासो भवतीत्यनुशुश्रुम।।1.44।।
verses.translation: Alas! We are resolved to commit a great sin, in that we are prepared to slay our own kinsmen through greed for the pleasures of a kingdom.

verses.sanskrit:
अहो बत महत्पापं कर्तुं व्यवसिता वयम्।

यद्राज्यसुखलोभेन हन्तुं स्वजनमुद्यताः।।1.45।।
verses.translation: It would be better for me if the sons of Dhritarashtra, weapons in hand, should slay me, unresisting and unarmed, in battle.

verses.sanskrit:
यदि मामप्रतीकारमशस्त्रं शस्त्रपाणयः।

धार्तराष्ट्रा रणे हन्युस्तन्मे क्षेमतरं भवेत्।।1.46।।
verses.translation:
Sanjaya said:
Having spoken thus on the battlefield, Arjuna cast aside his bow and arrows and sank down on the seat of his chariot, his mind overwhelmed with sorrow.

verses.sanskrit:
सञ्जय उवाच

एवमुक्त्वाऽर्जुनः संख्ये रथोपस्थ उपाविशत्।

विसृज्य सशरं चापं शोकसंविग्नमानसः।।1.47।।
verses.translation: (In recensions that number this verse separately:) Thus Arjuna, overwhelmed by grief, cast aside his bow and arrows and sat down on the chariot seat, his heart stricken with sorrow.

verses.sanskrit:
सञ्जय उवाच

तं तथा कृपयाऽविष्टमश्रुपूर्णाकुलेक्षणम्।

विषीदन्तमिदं वाक्यमुवाच मधुसूदनः।।2.1।।
verses.translation:
Sanjaya said:
To him, thus overcome with pity, his eyes filled with tears and downcast, his mind distressed, Madhusudana spoke these words:

verses.sanskrit:
श्री भगवानुवाच

कुतस्त्वा कश्मलमिदं विषमे समुपस्थितम्।

अनार्यजुष्टमस्वर्ग्यमकीर्तिकरमर्जुन।।2.2।।
verses.translation:
The Blessed Lord said:
Whence has this dejection come upon you in this hour of crisis — this unworthy weakness, not befitting an Arya, bringing neither heaven nor honor?

verses.sanskrit:
क्लैब्यं मा स्म गमः पार्थ नैतत्त्वय्युपपद्यते।

क्षुद्रं हृदयदौर्बल्यं त्यक्त्वोत्तिष्ठ परन्तप।।2.3।।
verses.translation: Yield not to this unmanliness, O Partha — it does not suit you. Cast off this petty weakness of heart and arise, O scorcher of foes.

verses.sanskrit:
अर्जुन उवाच

कथं भीष्ममहं संख्ये द्रोणं च मधुसूदन।

इषुभिः प्रतियोत्स्यामि पूजार्हावरिसूदन।।2.4।।
verses.translation:
Arjuna said:
O Madhusudana, how can I fight in battle against Bhishma and Drona with arrows, O slayer of enemies, when they are worthy of my worship?

verses.sanskrit:
गुरूनहत्वा हि महानुभावान्

श्रेयो भोक्तुं भैक्ष्यमपीह लोके।

हत्वार्थकामांस्तु गुरूनिहैव

भुञ्जीय भोगान् रुधिरप्रदिग्धान्।।2.5।।
verses.translation: Better to live on alms in this world than to slay these noble elders. For by killing them — though they seek personal gain — I would enjoy wealth and pleasures stained with their blood.

verses.sanskrit:
न चैतद्विद्मः कतरन्नो गरीयो

यद्वा जयेम यदि वा नो जयेयुः।

यानेव हत्वा न जिजीविषाम

स्तेऽवस्थिताः प्रमुखे धार्तराष्ट्राः।।2.6।।
verses.translation: Nor do we know which is better — that we should conquer them, or they us. Those very sons of Dhritarashtra stand facing us, after killing whom we would not wish to live.

verses.sanskrit:
कार्पण्यदोषोपहतस्वभावः

पृच्छामि त्वां धर्मसंमूढचेताः।

यच्छ्रेयः स्यान्निश्िचतं ब्रूहि तन्मे

शिष्यस्तेऽहं शाधि मां त्वां प्रपन्नम्।।2.7।।
verses.translation: My nature is overcome by the taint of faint-hearted pity; my mind is confused about dharma. I ask you — tell me decisively what is best. I am your disciple; instruct me, who have taken refuge in you.

verses.sanskrit:
न हि प्रपश्यामि ममापनुद्या

द्यच्छोकमुच्छोषणमिन्द्रियाणाम्।

अवाप्य भूमावसपत्नमृद्धम्

राज्यं सुराणामपि चाधिपत्यम्।।2.8।।
verses.translation: I see nothing that could dispel this grief that withers my senses — not even unrivaled, prosperous sovereignty on earth, nor lordship over the gods.

verses.sanskrit:
सञ्जय उवाच

एवमुक्त्वा हृषीकेशं गुडाकेशः परन्तप।

न योत्स्य इति गोविन्दमुक्त्वा तूष्णीं बभूव ह।।2.9।।
verses.translation:
Sanjaya said:
Having spoken thus to Hrishikesha, Gudakesha (Arjuna), the scorcher of foes, said 'I shall not fight' to Govinda, and fell silent.

verses.sanskrit:
तमुवाच हृषीकेशः प्रहसन्निव भारत।

सेनयोरुभयोर्मध्ये विषीदन्तमिदं वचः।।2.10।।
verses.translation: O Bharata (Dhritarashtra), to him grieving between the two armies, Hrishikesha, as if smiling, spoke these words:

verses.sanskrit:
श्री भगवानुवाच

अशोच्यानन्वशोचस्त्वं प्रज्ञावादांश्च भाषसे।

गतासूनगतासूंश्च नानुशोचन्ति पण्डिताः।।2.11।।
verses.translation:
The Blessed Lord said:
You grieve for those who should not be grieved for, and yet you speak words of wisdom. The wise grieve neither for the living nor for the dead.

verses.sanskrit:
न त्वेवाहं जातु नासं न त्वं नेमे जनाधिपाः।

न चैव न भविष्यामः सर्वे वयमतः परम्।।2.12।।
verses.translation: Never was there a time when I was not, nor you, nor these kings; nor shall any of us ever cease to be hereafter.

verses.sanskrit:
देहिनोऽस्मिन्यथा देहे कौमारं यौवनं जरा।

तथा देहान्तरप्राप्तिर्धीरस्तत्र न मुह्यति।।2.13।।
verses.translation: Just as in this body the embodied one passes through childhood, youth, and old age, so too does he pass into another body. The wise are not bewildered by this.

verses.sanskrit:
मात्रास्पर्शास्तु कौन्तेय शीतोष्णसुखदुःखदाः।

आगमापायिनोऽनित्यास्तांस्तितिक्षस्व भारत।।2.14।।
verses.translation: O son of Kunti, contacts with matter — which give rise to cold and heat, pleasure and pain — come and go; they are impermanent. Endure them, O Bharata.

verses.sanskrit:
यं हि न व्यथयन्त्येते पुरुषं पुरुषर्षभ।

समदुःखसुखं धीरं सोऽमृतत्वाय कल्पते।।2.15।।
verses.translation: The person whom these do not torment, O bull among men, steady in pleasure and pain, the wise — he is fit for immortality.

verses.sanskrit:
नासतो विद्यते भावो नाभावो विद्यते सतः।

उभयोरपि दृष्टोऽन्तस्त्वनयोस्तत्त्वदर्शिभिः।।2.16।।
verses.translation: Of the unreal there is no being; of the real there is no non-being. The truth of both is seen by the seers of reality.

verses.sanskrit:
अविनाशि तु तद्विद्धि येन सर्वमिदं ततम्।

विनाशमव्ययस्यास्य न कश्चित् कर्तुमर्हति।।2.17।।
verses.translation: Know that to be indestructible by which all this is pervaded. No one can bring about the destruction of the imperishable.

verses.sanskrit:
अन्तवन्त इमे देहा नित्यस्योक्ताः शरीरिणः।

अनाशिनोऽप्रमेयस्य तस्माद्युध्यस्व भारत।।2.18।।
verses.translation: These bodies, inhabited by the eternal, indestructible, immeasurable embodied one, are said to have an end. Therefore fight, O Bharata.

verses.sanskrit:
य एनं वेत्ति हन्तारं यश्चैनं मन्यते हतम्।

उभौ तौ न विजानीतो नायं हन्ति न हन्यते।।2.19।।
verses.translation: He who thinks this (atman) a slayer, and he who thinks it slain — both do not know. It neither slays nor is slain.

verses.sanskrit:
न जायते म्रियते वा कदाचि

न्नायं भूत्वा भविता वा न भूयः।

अजो नित्यः शाश्वतोऽयं पुराणो

न हन्यते हन्यमाने शरीरे।।2.20।।
verses.translation: It is never born and never dies; having once been, it never ceases to be. Unborn, eternal, everlasting, ancient — it is not slain when the body is slain.

verses.sanskrit:
वेदाविनाशिनं नित्यं य एनमजमव्ययम्।

कथं स पुरुषः पार्थ कं घातयति हन्ति कम्।।2.21।।
verses.translation: O Partha, how can that person who knows the atman to be indestructible, eternal, unborn, and imperishable, kill anyone, or cause anyone to kill?

verses.sanskrit:
वासांसि जीर्णानि यथा विहाय

नवानि गृह्णाति नरोऽपराणि।

तथा शरीराणि विहाय जीर्णा

न्यन्यानि संयाति नवानि देही।।2.22।।
verses.translation: As a person casts off worn-out garments and takes on new ones, so the embodied one casts off worn-out bodies and enters into new ones.

verses.sanskrit:
नैनं छिन्दन्ति शस्त्राणि नैनं दहति पावकः।

न चैनं क्लेदयन्त्यापो न शोषयति मारुतः।।2.23।।
verses.translation: Weapons do not cut this, fire does not burn this, water does not wet this, nor does the wind dry it.

verses.sanskrit:
अच्छेद्योऽयमदाह्योऽयमक्लेद्योऽशोष्य एव च।

नित्यः सर्वगतः स्थाणुरचलोऽयं सनातनः।।2.24।।
verses.translation: This cannot be cut, burned, wetted, or dried. It is eternal, all-pervading, stable, immovable, everlasting.

verses.sanskrit:
अव्यक्तोऽयमचिन्त्योऽयमविकार्योऽयमुच्यते।

तस्मादेवं विदित्वैनं नानुशोचितुमर्हसि।।2.25।।
verses.translation: This is said to be unmanifest, unthinkable, unchanging. Therefore, knowing it to be such, you ought not to grieve.

verses.sanskrit:
अथ चैनं नित्यजातं नित्यं वा मन्यसे मृतम्।

तथापि त्वं महाबाहो नैवं शोचितुमर्हसि।।2.26।।
verses.translation: And even if you think of it as constantly born and constantly dying, O mighty-armed one, you still ought not to grieve.

verses.sanskrit:
जातस्य हि ध्रुवो मृत्युर्ध्रुवं जन्म मृतस्य च।

तस्मादपरिहार्येऽर्थे न त्वं शोचितुमर्हसि।।2.27।।
verses.translation: For to the born, death is certain; and to the dead, birth is certain. Therefore, over the unavoidable, you ought not to grieve.

verses.sanskrit:
अव्यक्तादीनि भूतानि व्यक्तमध्यानि भारत।

अव्यक्तनिधनान्येव तत्र का परिदेवना।।2.28।।
verses.translation: Beings are unmanifest in their beginnings, manifest in the middle, and unmanifest again at their ends, O Bharata. What cause is there for grief?

verses.sanskrit:
आश्चर्यवत्पश्यति कश्चिदेन

माश्चर्यवद्वदति तथैव चान्यः।

आश्चर्यवच्चैनमन्यः श्रृणोति

श्रुत्वाप्येनं वेद न चैव कश्चित्।।2.29।।
verses.translation: One perceives the atman as a wonder; another speaks of it as a wonder; another hears of it as a wonder; and even having heard, no one knows it.

verses.sanskrit:
देही नित्यमवध्योऽयं देहे सर्वस्य भारत।

तस्मात्सर्वाणि भूतानि न त्वं शोचितुमर्हसि।।2.30।।
verses.translation: The embodied one in the body of each is eternal and indestructible, O Bharata. Therefore you should not grieve for any being.

verses.sanskrit:
स्वधर्ममपि चावेक्ष्य न विकम्पितुमर्हसि।

धर्म्याद्धि युद्धाछ्रेयोऽन्यत्क्षत्रियस्य न विद्यते।।2.31।।
verses.translation: And considering your own dharma, you should not waver. For a kshatriya, there is nothing higher than a righteous war.

verses.sanskrit:
यदृच्छया चोपपन्नं स्वर्गद्वारमपावृतम्।

सुखिनः क्षत्रियाः पार्थ लभन्ते युद्धमीदृशम्।।2.32।।
verses.translation: Fortunate indeed are the kshatriyas, O Partha, who are offered such a battle, come of its own accord — an open door to heaven.

verses.sanskrit:
अथ चैत्त्वमिमं धर्म्यं संग्रामं न करिष्यसि।

ततः स्वधर्मं कीर्तिं च हित्वा पापमवाप्स्यसि।।2.33।।
verses.translation: But if you will not fight this righteous war, then, abandoning your own dharma and your honor, you will incur sin.

verses.sanskrit:
अकीर्तिं चापि भूतानि कथयिष्यन्ति तेऽव्ययाम्।

संभावितस्य चाकीर्तिर्मरणादतिरिच्यते।।2.34।।
verses.translation: And the world will speak of your lasting dishonor, and to one who has been honored, dishonor is worse than death.

verses.sanskrit:
भयाद्रणादुपरतं मंस्यन्ते त्वां महारथाः।

येषां च त्वं बहुमतो भूत्वा यास्यसि लाघवम्।।2.35।।
verses.translation: The great chariot-warriors will think you have withdrawn from battle out of fear, and you will fall into contempt with those who once esteemed you highly.

verses.sanskrit:
अवाच्यवादांश्च बहून् वदिष्यन्ति तवाहिताः।

निन्दन्तस्तव सामर्थ्यं ततो दुःखतरं नु किम्।।2.36।।
verses.translation: Your enemies will speak many unspeakable words, mocking your strength. What could be more painful than that?

verses.sanskrit:
हतो वा प्राप्स्यसि स्वर्गं जित्वा वा भोक्ष्यसे महीम्।

तस्मादुत्तिष्ठ कौन्तेय युद्धाय कृतनिश्चयः।।2.37।।
verses.translation: Slain, you shall attain heaven; victorious, you shall enjoy the earth. Therefore arise, O son of Kunti, resolved to fight.

verses.sanskrit:
सुखदुःखे समे कृत्वा लाभालाभौ जयाजयौ।

ततो युद्धाय युज्यस्व नैवं पापमवाप्स्यसि।।2.38।।
verses.translation: Treating alike pleasure and pain, gain and loss, victory and defeat, gird yourself for battle. Thus you shall incur no sin.

verses.sanskrit:
एषा तेऽभिहिता सांख्ये बुद्धिर्योगे त्विमां श्रृणु।

बुद्ध्यायुक्तो यया पार्थ कर्मबन्धं प्रहास्यसि।।2.39।।
verses.translation: This wisdom has been declared to you in the Sankhya teaching; now hear it in the Yoga (of action), endowed with which, O Partha, you shall cast off the bondage of karma.

verses.sanskrit:
नेहाभिक्रमनाशोऽस्ति प्रत्यवायो न विद्यते।

स्वल्पमप्यस्य धर्मस्य त्रायते महतो भयात्।।2.40।।
verses.translation: In this (path) no effort is wasted, nor is any obstacle produced. Even a little of this dharma protects from great fear.

verses.sanskrit:
व्यवसायात्मिका बुद्धिरेकेह कुरुनन्दन।

बहुशाखा ह्यनन्ताश्च बुद्धयोऽव्यवसायिनाम्।।2.41।।
verses.translation: In this path, O joy of the Kurus, the resolute intellect is one-pointed; but the intellects of the irresolute are many-branched and endless.

verses.sanskrit:
यामिमां पुष्पितां वाचं प्रवदन्त्यविपश्चितः।

वेदवादरताः पार्थ नान्यदस्तीति वादिनः।।2.42।।
verses.translation: O Partha, the unwise who take delight in the flowery words of the Vedas, declaring 'there is nothing else,'

verses.sanskrit:
कामात्मानः स्वर्गपरा जन्मकर्मफलप्रदाम्।

क्रियाविशेषबहुलां भोगैश्वर्यगतिं प्रति।।2.43।।
verses.translation: full of desires, set on heaven, offer rites for the fruit of birth and action, laden with many specific ceremonies aimed at enjoyment and power —

verses.sanskrit:
भोगैश्वर्यप्रसक्तानां तयापहृतचेतसाम्।

व्यवसायात्मिका बुद्धिः समाधौ न विधीयते।।2.44।।
verses.translation: for those attached to enjoyment and power, whose minds are carried away by such talk, the resolute intellect, fixed in samadhi, is not granted.

verses.sanskrit:
त्रैगुण्यविषया वेदा निस्त्रैगुण्यो भवार्जुन।

निर्द्वन्द्वो नित्यसत्त्वस्थो निर्योगक्षेम आत्मवान्।।2.45।।
verses.translation: The Vedas deal with the three gunas; be free from the three gunas, O Arjuna, free from the pairs of opposites, ever established in purity (sattva), free from acquisition and preservation, self-possessed.

verses.sanskrit:
यावानर्थ उदपाने सर्वतः संप्लुतोदके।

तावान्सर्वेषु वेदेषु ब्राह्मणस्य विजानतः।।2.46।।
verses.translation: As much use as a well has when water is flooding everywhere, so much use have all the Vedas for a brahmana who knows.

verses.sanskrit:
कर्मण्येवाधिकारस्ते मा फलेषु कदाचन।

मा कर्मफलहेतुर्भूर्मा ते सङ्गोऽस्त्वकर्मणि।।2.47।।
verses.translation: You have the right to action alone, never to its fruits. Let not the fruits of action be your motive, nor let your attachment be to inaction.

verses.sanskrit:
योगस्थः कुरु कर्माणि सङ्गं त्यक्त्वा धनञ्जय।

सिद्ध्यसिद्ध्योः समो भूत्वा समत्वं योग उच्यते।।2.48।।
verses.translation: Established in yoga, perform actions, O Dhananjaya, abandoning attachment, indifferent to success and failure. This evenness of mind is called yoga.

verses.sanskrit:
दूरेण ह्यवरं कर्म बुद्धियोगाद्धनञ्जय।

बुद्धौ शरणमन्विच्छ कृपणाः फलहेतवः।।2.49।।
verses.translation: Action is far inferior to the yoga of wisdom, O Dhananjaya. Seek refuge in wisdom. Pitiable are those who act for fruits.

verses.sanskrit:
बुद्धियुक्तो जहातीह उभे सुकृतदुष्कृते।

तस्माद्योगाय युज्यस्व योगः कर्मसु कौशलम्।।2.50।।
verses.translation: He whose intellect is yoked (to wisdom) casts off here both good and evil deeds. Therefore apply yourself to yoga. Yoga is skill in action.

verses.sanskrit:
कर्मजं बुद्धियुक्ता हि फलं त्यक्त्वा मनीषिणः।

जन्मबन्धविनिर्मुक्ताः पदं गच्छन्त्यनामयम्।।2.51।।
verses.translation: For the wise, whose intellect is yoked, abandoning the fruit born of action, liberated from the bondage of birth, reach the abode beyond all ills.

verses.sanskrit:
यदा ते मोहकलिलं बुद्धिर्व्यतितरिष्यति।

तदा गन्तासि निर्वेदं श्रोतव्यस्य श्रुतस्य च।।2.52।।
verses.translation: When your intellect crosses beyond the thicket of delusion, then you shall become indifferent to what is to be heard and what has been heard.

verses.sanskrit:
श्रुतिविप्रतिपन्ना ते यदा स्थास्यति निश्चला।

समाधावचला बुद्धिस्तदा योगमवाप्स्यसि।।2.53।।
verses.translation: When your intellect, bewildered by scripture, stands immovable and steady in samadhi, then you shall attain yoga.

verses.sanskrit:
अर्जुन उवाच

स्थितप्रज्ञस्य का भाषा समाधिस्थस्य केशव।

स्थितधीः किं प्रभाषेत किमासीत व्रजेत किम्।।2.54।।
verses.translation:
Arjuna said:
O Keshava, what is the description of one of steady wisdom, established in samadhi? How does he speak, how sit, how walk?

verses.sanskrit:
श्री भगवानुवाच

प्रजहाति यदा कामान् सर्वान् पार्थ मनोगतान्।

आत्मन्येवात्मना तुष्टः स्थितप्रज्ञस्तदोच्यते।।2.55।।
verses.translation:
The Blessed Lord said:
When one casts off all desires of the mind, O Partha, content in the Self by the Self alone, he is said to be of steady wisdom.

verses.sanskrit:
दुःखेष्वनुद्विग्नमनाः सुखेषु विगतस्पृहः।

वीतरागभयक्रोधः स्थितधीर्मुनिरुच्यते।।2.56।।
verses.translation: He whose mind is not troubled in sorrows, who is without craving amid pleasures, from whom passion, fear, and anger have departed — he is called a sage of steady mind.

verses.sanskrit:
यः सर्वत्रानभिस्नेहस्तत्तत्प्राप्य शुभाशुभम्।

नाभिनन्दति न द्वेष्टि तस्य प्रज्ञा प्रतिष्ठिता।।2.57।।
verses.translation: He who is free from attachment on every side, who neither rejoices on obtaining good nor hates on obtaining evil — his wisdom is firmly established.

verses.sanskrit:
यदा संहरते चायं कूर्मोऽङ्गानीव सर्वशः।

इन्द्रियाणीन्द्रियार्थेभ्यस्तस्य प्रज्ञा प्रतिष्ठिता।।2.58।।
verses.translation: And when he withdraws his senses from the objects of sense, as a tortoise draws in its limbs, his wisdom is firmly established.

verses.sanskrit:
विषया विनिवर्तन्ते निराहारस्य देहिनः।

रसवर्जं रसोऽप्यस्य परं दृष्ट्वा निवर्तते।।2.59।।
verses.translation: Objects turn away from the abstinent man, but not the taste for them. Even the taste departs when he has seen the Supreme.

verses.sanskrit:
यततो ह्यपि कौन्तेय पुरुषस्य विपश्चितः।

इन्द्रियाणि प्रमाथीनि हरन्ति प्रसभं मनः।।2.60।।
verses.translation: O son of Kunti, the turbulent senses forcibly carry away the mind even of the wise person who is striving.

verses.sanskrit:
तानि सर्वाणि संयम्य युक्त आसीत मत्परः।

वशे हि यस्येन्द्रियाणि तस्य प्रज्ञा प्रतिष्ठिता।।2.61।।
verses.translation: Restraining all these, he should sit, yoked, intent on Me. For he whose senses are under control — his wisdom is firmly established.

verses.sanskrit:
ध्यायतो विषयान्पुंसः सङ्गस्तेषूपजायते।

सङ्गात् संजायते कामः कामात्क्रोधोऽभिजायते।।2.62।।
verses.translation: When a man dwells on objects, attachment to them is born; from attachment arises desire; from desire, anger.

verses.sanskrit:
क्रोधाद्भवति संमोहः संमोहात्स्मृतिविभ्रमः।

स्मृतिभ्रंशाद् बुद्धिनाशो बुद्धिनाशात्प्रणश्यति।।2.63।।
verses.translation: From anger arises delusion; from delusion, loss of memory; from loss of memory, destruction of the intellect; with destruction of the intellect, he perishes.

verses.sanskrit:
रागद्वेषवियुक्तैस्तु विषयानिन्द्रियैश्चरन्।

आत्मवश्यैर्विधेयात्मा प्रसादमधिगच्छति।।2.64।।
verses.translation: But the disciplined one, moving among objects with senses controlled, free from attachment and aversion, under the sway of the Self — attains tranquility.

verses.sanskrit:
प्रसादे सर्वदुःखानां हानिरस्योपजायते।

प्रसन्नचेतसो ह्याशु बुद्धिः पर्यवतिष्ठते।।2.65।।
verses.translation: In tranquility all sorrows are destroyed. For the intellect of the tranquil-minded soon becomes firmly established.

verses.sanskrit:
नास्ति बुद्धिरयुक्तस्य न चायुक्तस्य भावना।

न चाभावयतः शान्तिरशान्तस्य कुतः सुखम्।।2.66।।
verses.translation: The unyoked has no understanding, and the unyoked has no meditation. Without meditation there is no peace; and for one without peace, how can there be happiness?

verses.sanskrit:
इन्द्रियाणां हि चरतां यन्मनोऽनुविधीयते।

तदस्य हरति प्रज्ञां वायुर्नावमिवाम्भसि।।2.67।।
verses.translation: When the mind follows the wandering senses, it carries away the understanding, as the wind carries away a ship upon the waters.

verses.sanskrit:
तस्माद्यस्य महाबाहो निगृहीतानि सर्वशः।

इन्द्रियाणीन्द्रियार्थेभ्यस्तस्य प्रज्ञा प्रतिष्ठिता।।2.68।।
verses.translation: Therefore, O mighty-armed one, he whose senses are withdrawn on every side from their objects — his wisdom is firmly established.

verses.sanskrit:
या निशा सर्वभूतानां तस्यां जागर्ति संयमी।

यस्यां जाग्रति भूतानि सा निशा पश्यतो मुनेः।।2.69।।
verses.translation: What is night for all beings, in that the self-controlled is awake; where all beings are awake, that is night for the sage who sees.

verses.sanskrit:
आपूर्यमाणमचलप्रतिष्ठं

समुद्रमापः प्रविशन्ति यद्वत्।

तद्वत्कामा यं प्रविशन्ति सर्वे

स शान्तिमाप्नोति न कामकामी।।2.70।।
verses.translation: As waters enter the ocean which, ever being filled, remains unmoved, so he whom all desires enter attains peace — not he who craves for desires.

verses.sanskrit:
विहाय कामान्यः सर्वान्पुमांश्चरति निःस्पृहः।

निर्ममो निरहंकारः स शांतिमधिगच्छति।।2.71।।
verses.translation: The man who, abandoning all desires, moves about without longing, without the sense of 'I' and 'mine' — he attains peace.

verses.sanskrit:
एषा ब्राह्मी स्थितिः पार्थ नैनां प्राप्य विमुह्यति।

स्थित्वाऽस्यामन्तकालेऽपि ब्रह्मनिर्वाणमृच्छति।।2.72।।
verses.translation: This is the state of Brahman, O Partha; having attained it, one is no longer deluded. Abiding in it even at the hour of death, one attains the nirvana of Brahman.

verses.sanskrit:
अर्जुन उवाच

ज्यायसी चेत्कर्मणस्ते मता बुद्धिर्जनार्दन।

तत्किं कर्मणि घोरे मां नियोजयसि केशव।।3.1।।
verses.translation:
Arjuna said:
If you hold that wisdom is superior to action, O Janardana, why then, O Keshava, do you urge me to this terrible deed?

verses.sanskrit:
व्यामिश्रेणेव वाक्येन बुद्धिं मोहयसीव मे।

तदेकं वद निश्िचत्य येन श्रेयोऽहमाप्नुयाम्।।3.2।।
verses.translation: With seemingly conflicting words you confuse my understanding. Tell me decisively the one thing by which I may attain the highest good.

verses.sanskrit:
श्री भगवानुवाच

लोकेऽस्मिन्द्विविधा निष्ठा पुरा प्रोक्ता मयानघ।

ज्ञानयोगेन सांख्यानां कर्मयोगेन योगिनाम्।।3.3।।
verses.translation:
The Blessed Lord said:
O sinless one, in this world a twofold path was declared by Me of old: the yoga of knowledge for the contemplatives, and the yoga of action for the active.

verses.sanskrit:
न कर्मणामनारम्भान्नैष्कर्म्यं पुरुषोऽश्नुते।

न च संन्यसनादेव सिद्धिं समधिगच्छति।।3.4।।
verses.translation: Not by abstaining from action does a man attain freedom from action; nor by renunciation alone does he attain perfection.

verses.sanskrit:
न हि कश्िचत्क्षणमपि जातु तिष्ठत्यकर्मकृत्।

कार्यते ह्यवशः कर्म सर्वः प्रकृतिजैर्गुणैः।।3.5।।
verses.translation: No one can remain, even for a moment, without performing action; for everyone is driven to act, helpless, by the gunas born of nature.

verses.sanskrit:
कर्मेन्द्रियाणि संयम्य य आस्ते मनसा स्मरन्।

इन्द्रियार्थान्विमूढात्मा मिथ्याचारः स उच्यते।।3.6।।
verses.translation: He who, restraining the organs of action, sits with his mind dwelling on the objects of sense — that deluded soul is called a hypocrite.

verses.sanskrit:
यस्त्विन्द्रियाणि मनसा नियम्यारभतेऽर्जुन।

कर्मेन्द्रियैः कर्मयोगमसक्तः स विशिष्यते।।3.7।।
verses.translation: But he, O Arjuna, who, controlling the senses by the mind, engages the organs of action in Karma Yoga, unattached — he excels.

verses.sanskrit:
नियतं कुरु कर्म त्वं कर्म ज्यायो ह्यकर्मणः।

शरीरयात्रापि च ते न प्रसिद्ध्येदकर्मणः।।3.8।।
verses.translation: Do your allotted duty, for action is superior to inaction; even the bare maintenance of your body would not be possible without action.

verses.sanskrit:
यज्ञार्थात्कर्मणोऽन्यत्र लोकोऽयं कर्मबन्धनः।

तदर्थं कर्म कौन्तेय मुक्तसंगः समाचर।।3.9।।
verses.translation: This world is bound by action other than that performed as yajna (sacrifice). Therefore, O son of Kunti, perform action for that purpose, free from attachment.

verses.sanskrit:
सहयज्ञाः प्रजाः सृष्ट्वा पुरोवाच प्रजापतिः।

अनेन प्रसविष्यध्वमेष वोऽस्त्विष्टकामधुक्।।3.10।।
verses.translation: Having created mankind together with yajna in the beginning, the Lord of beings said: 'By this shall you multiply; let this be the cow of plenty that yields what you desire.'

verses.sanskrit:
देवान्भावयतानेन ते देवा भावयन्तु वः।

परस्परं भावयन्तः श्रेयः परमवाप्स्यथ।।3.11।।
verses.translation: 'By this you sustain the gods, and may the gods sustain you. Sustaining one another, you shall attain the highest good.'

verses.sanskrit:
इष्टान्भोगान्हि वो देवा दास्यन्ते यज्ञभाविताः।

तैर्दत्तानप्रदायैभ्यो यो भुङ्क्ते स्तेन एव सः।।3.12।।
verses.translation: 'For the gods, sustained by sacrifice, will give you the enjoyments you desire.' He who enjoys their gifts without offering in return is a thief.

verses.sanskrit:
यज्ञशिष्टाशिनः सन्तो मुच्यन्ते सर्वकिल्बिषैः।

भुञ्जते ते त्वघं पापा ये पचन्त्यात्मकारणात्।।3.13।।
verses.translation: The good, who eat the remnants of sacrifice, are freed from all sins; but the wicked, who cook only for themselves, eat sin.

verses.sanskrit:
अन्नाद्भवन्ति भूतानि पर्जन्यादन्नसम्भवः।

यज्ञाद्भवति पर्जन्यो यज्ञः कर्मसमुद्भवः।।3.14।।
verses.translation: Beings are born from food; food comes from rain; rain comes from sacrifice; sacrifice is born of action.

verses.sanskrit:
कर्म ब्रह्मोद्भवं विद्धि ब्रह्माक्षरसमुद्भवम्।

तस्मात्सर्वगतं ब्रह्म नित्यं यज्ञे प्रतिष्ठितम्।।3.15।।
verses.translation: Know action to spring from Brahman; Brahman from the Imperishable. Therefore the all-pervading Brahman is ever established in sacrifice.

verses.sanskrit:
एवं प्रवर्तितं चक्रं नानुवर्तयतीह यः।

अघायुरिन्द्रियारामो मोघं पार्थ स जीवति।।3.16।।
verses.translation: He who does not follow here the wheel thus set in motion, living in sin, rejoicing in the senses — he lives in vain, O Partha.

verses.sanskrit:
यस्त्वात्मरतिरेव स्यादात्मतृप्तश्च मानवः।

आत्मन्येव च सन्तुष्टस्तस्य कार्यं न विद्यते।।3.17।।
verses.translation: But the man who rejoices only in the Self, who is satisfied with the Self, who is content in the Self alone — for him there is nothing to be done.

verses.sanskrit:
नैव तस्य कृतेनार्थो नाकृतेनेह कश्चन।

न चास्य सर्वभूतेषु कश्िचदर्थव्यपाश्रयः।।3.18।।
verses.translation: For him no purpose is served by what is done, or by what is not done. Nor has he any need to depend on any being for any purpose.

verses.sanskrit:
तस्मादसक्तः सततं कार्यं कर्म समाचर।

असक्तो ह्याचरन्कर्म परमाप्नोति पूरुषः।।3.19।।
verses.translation: Therefore always perform without attachment the action that is your duty; for by performing action without attachment, man attains the Supreme.

verses.sanskrit:
कर्मणैव हि संसिद्धिमास्थिता जनकादयः।

लोकसंग्रहमेवापि संपश्यन्कर्तुमर्हसि।।3.20।।
verses.translation: By action alone Janaka and others attained perfection. Even with a view only to the welfare of the world (lokasamgraha), you should act.

verses.sanskrit:
यद्यदाचरति श्रेष्ठस्तत्तदेवेतरो जनः।

स यत्प्रमाणं कुरुते लोकस्तदनुवर्तते।।3.21।।
verses.translation: Whatever a great man does, others follow. Whatever standard he sets, the world pursues.

verses.sanskrit:
न मे पार्थास्ति कर्तव्यं त्रिषु लोकेषु किञ्चन।

नानवाप्तमवाप्तव्यं वर्त एव च कर्मणि।।3.22।।
verses.translation: O Partha, there is nothing in the three worlds that I have to do, nor anything unattained to be attained — yet I engage in action.

verses.sanskrit:
यदि ह्यहं न वर्तेयं जातु कर्मण्यतन्द्रितः।

मम वर्त्मानुवर्तन्ते मनुष्याः पार्थ सर्वशः।।3.23।।
verses.translation: For if I were not, unwearied, ever to engage in action, men would follow My path in every way, O Partha.

verses.sanskrit:
उत्सीदेयुरिमे लोका न कुर्यां कर्म चेदहम्।

सङ्करस्य च कर्ता स्यामुपहन्यामिमाः प्रजाः।।3.24।।
verses.translation: If I did not perform action, these worlds would perish; I would cause confusion and destroy these creatures.

verses.sanskrit:
सक्ताः कर्मण्यविद्वांसो यथा कुर्वन्ति भारत।

कुर्याद्विद्वांस्तथासक्तश्िचकीर्षुर्लोकसंग्रहम्।।3.25।।
verses.translation: As the ignorant act with attachment to action, O Bharata, so should the wise act without attachment, desiring the welfare of the world.

verses.sanskrit:
न बुद्धिभेदं जनयेदज्ञानां कर्मसङ्गिनाम्।

जोषयेत्सर्वकर्माणि विद्वान् युक्तः समाचरन्।।3.26।।
verses.translation: Let no wise one unsettle the minds of the ignorant who are attached to action. Rather, while himself acting yoked, he should inspire them to all actions.

verses.sanskrit:
प्रकृतेः क्रियमाणानि गुणैः कर्माणि सर्वशः।

अहङ्कारविमूढात्मा कर्ताऽहमिति मन्यते।।3.27।।
verses.translation: Actions are done in every way by the gunas of prakriti (nature). One whose self is deluded by egoism thinks, 'I am the doer.'

verses.sanskrit:
तत्त्ववित्तु महाबाहो गुणकर्मविभागयोः।

गुणा गुणेषु वर्तन्त इति मत्वा न सज्जते।।3.28।।
verses.translation: But he, O mighty-armed one, who knows the truth about the division of the gunas and action, understanding that gunas act upon gunas, is not attached.

verses.sanskrit:
प्रकृतेर्गुणसम्मूढाः सज्जन्ते गुणकर्मसु।

तानकृत्स्नविदो मन्दान्कृत्स्नविन्न विचालयेत्।।3.29।।
verses.translation: Those deluded by the gunas of prakriti are attached to the actions of the gunas. The one who knows the whole should not unsettle those of dull understanding who do not.

verses.sanskrit:
मयि सर्वाणि कर्माणि संन्यस्याध्यात्मचेतसा।

निराशीर्निर्ममो भूत्वा युध्यस्व विगतज्वरः।।3.30।।
verses.translation: Surrendering all actions to Me, with your mind fixed on the Self, free from hope and the sense of 'mine,' free from feverish excitement, fight.

verses.sanskrit:
ये मे मतमिदं नित्यमनुतिष्ठन्ति मानवाः।

श्रद्धावन्तोऽनसूयन्तो मुच्यन्ते तेऽपि कर्मभिः।।3.31।।
verses.translation: Those who, full of faith and without cavilling, constantly practice this teaching of Mine — they too are freed from actions.

verses.sanskrit:
ये त्वेतदभ्यसूयन्तो नानुतिष्ठन्ति मे मतम्।

सर्वज्ञानविमूढांस्तान्विद्धि नष्टानचेतसः।।3.32।।
verses.translation: But those who, carping at this teaching, do not practice it — know those senseless ones, deluded in all knowledge, to be lost.

verses.sanskrit:
सदृशं चेष्टते स्वस्याः प्रकृतेर्ज्ञानवानपि।

प्रकृतिं यान्ति भूतानि निग्रहः किं करिष्यति।।3.33।।
verses.translation: Even a wise man acts according to his own nature. Beings follow their nature. What will restraint accomplish?

verses.sanskrit:
इन्द्रियस्येन्द्रियस्यार्थे रागद्वेषौ व्यवस्थितौ।

तयोर्न वशमागच्छेत्तौ ह्यस्य परिपन्थिनौ।।3.34।।
verses.translation: Attachment and aversion are fixed in the sense in regard to its object. Let none come under their sway; these two are his enemies.

verses.sanskrit:
श्रेयान्स्वधर्मो विगुणः परधर्मात्स्वनुष्ठितात्।

स्वधर्मे निधनं श्रेयः परधर्मो भयावहः।।3.35।।
verses.translation: Better is one's own dharma, though imperfect, than another's well performed. Better is death in one's own dharma; another's dharma is fraught with fear.

verses.sanskrit:
अर्जुन उवाच

अथ केन प्रयुक्तोऽयं पापं चरति पूरुषः।

अनिच्छन्नपि वार्ष्णेय बलादिव नियोजितः।।3.36।।
verses.translation:
Arjuna said:
But then, O Varshneya, by what is a man impelled to commit sin, even against his will, as if driven by force?

verses.sanskrit:
श्री भगवानुवाच

काम एष क्रोध एष रजोगुणसमुद्भवः।

महाशनो महापाप्मा विद्ध्येनमिह वैरिणम्।।3.37।।
verses.translation:
The Blessed Lord said:
It is desire, it is anger, born of the guna of rajas — all-devouring, greatly sinful. Know this to be the enemy here.

verses.sanskrit:
धूमेनाव्रियते वह्निर्यथाऽऽदर्शो मलेन च।

यथोल्बेनावृतो गर्भस्तथा तेनेदमावृतम्।।3.38।।
verses.translation: As fire is covered by smoke, as a mirror by dust, as an embryo by the womb, so is this (knowledge) covered by that (desire).

verses.sanskrit:
आवृतं ज्ञानमेतेन ज्ञानिनो नित्यवैरिणा।

कामरूपेण कौन्तेय दुष्पूरेणानलेन च।।3.39।।
verses.translation: Knowledge is covered, O son of Kunti, by this constant enemy of the wise — desire, like an insatiable fire.

verses.sanskrit:
इन्द्रियाणि मनो बुद्धिरस्याधिष्ठानमुच्यते।

एतैर्विमोहयत्येष ज्ञानमावृत्य देहिनम्।।3.40।।
verses.translation: The senses, the mind, and the intellect are said to be its seat; through these it deludes the embodied one, veiling his knowledge.

verses.sanskrit:
तस्मात्त्वमिन्द्रियाण्यादौ नियम्य भरतर्षभ।

पाप्मानं प्रजहि ह्येनं ज्ञानविज्ञाननाशनम्।।3.41।।
verses.translation: Therefore, O bull among the Bharatas, controlling the senses first, slay this sinful destroyer of knowledge and realization.

verses.sanskrit:
इन्द्रियाणि पराण्याहुरिन्द्रियेभ्यः परं मनः।

मनसस्तु परा बुद्धिर्यो बुद्धेः परतस्तु सः।।3.42।।
verses.translation: The senses are said to be high; higher than the senses is the mind; higher than the mind is the intellect; and that which is higher than the intellect is He (the Self).

verses.sanskrit:
एवं बुद्धेः परं बुद्ध्वा संस्तभ्यात्मानमात्मना।

जहि शत्रुं महाबाहो कामरूपं दुरासदम्।।3.43।।
verses.translation: Thus, knowing Him who is beyond the intellect, steadying the self by the Self, slay, O mighty-armed one, the enemy in the form of desire, so hard to conquer.

chapters.chapter_title_english: The Yoga of Knowledge and the Renunciation of Action

verses.sanskrit:
श्री भगवानुवाच

इमं विवस्वते योगं प्रोक्तवानहमव्ययम्।

विवस्वान् मनवे प्राह मनुरिक्ष्वाकवेऽब्रवीत्।।4.1।।
verses.translation:
The Blessed Lord said:
This imperishable yoga I taught to Vivasvan (the sun-god); Vivasvan taught it to Manu; Manu told it to Ikshvaku.

verses.sanskrit:
एवं परम्पराप्राप्तमिमं राजर्षयो विदुः।

स कालेनेह महता योगो नष्टः परन्तप।।4.2।।
verses.translation: Thus handed down from one to another, the royal sages came to know it. But, O scorcher of foes, in the long course of time, that yoga was lost here.

verses.sanskrit:
स एवायं मया तेऽद्य योगः प्रोक्तः पुरातनः।

भक्तोऽसि मे सखा चेति रहस्यं ह्येतदुत्तमम्।।4.3।।
verses.translation: That same ancient yoga has been taught to you by Me today, for you are My devotee and friend. This is the supreme secret.

verses.sanskrit:
अर्जुन उवाच

अपरं भवतो जन्म परं जन्म विवस्वतः।

कथमेतद्विजानीयां त्वमादौ प्रोक्तवानिति।।4.4।।
verses.translation:
Arjuna said:
Your birth was later, and earlier the birth of Vivasvan. How then am I to understand that You declared this in the beginning?

verses.sanskrit:
श्री भगवानुवाच

बहूनि मे व्यतीतानि जन्मानि तव चार्जुन।

तान्यहं वेद सर्वाणि न त्वं वेत्थ परन्तप।।4.5।।
verses.translation:
The Blessed Lord said:
Many are the births I have passed through, and so have you, O Arjuna. I know them all; you do not, O scorcher of foes.

verses.sanskrit:
अजोऽपि सन्नव्ययात्मा भूतानामीश्वरोऽपि सन्।

प्रकृतिं स्वामधिष्ठाय संभवाम्यात्ममायया।।4.6।।
verses.translation: Though I am unborn, of imperishable Self, though I am the Lord of all beings, yet abiding in My own prakriti, I come into being by My own maya.

verses.sanskrit:
यदा यदा हि धर्मस्य ग्लानिर्भवति भारत।

अभ्युत्थानमधर्मस्य तदाऽऽत्मानं सृजाम्यहम्।।4.7।।
verses.translation: Whenever there is a decline of dharma, O Bharata, and a rise of adharma, then I bring Myself forth.

verses.sanskrit:
परित्राणाय साधूनां विनाशाय च दुष्कृताम्।

धर्मसंस्थापनार्थाय संभवामि युगे युगे।।4.8।।
verses.translation: For the protection of the good, for the destruction of the wicked, and for the establishment of dharma, I come into being age after age.

verses.sanskrit:
जन्म कर्म च मे दिव्यमेवं यो वेत्ति तत्त्वतः।

त्यक्त्वा देहं पुनर्जन्म नैति मामेति सोऽर्जुन।।4.9।।
verses.translation: He who truly knows My divine birth and action, on leaving the body, is not born again; he comes to Me, O Arjuna.

verses.sanskrit:
वीतरागभयक्रोधा मन्मया मामुपाश्रिताः।

बहवो ज्ञानतपसा पूता मद्भावमागताः।।4.10।।
verses.translation: Freed from attachment, fear, and anger, absorbed in Me, taking refuge in Me, purified by the austerity of knowledge, many have come to My state of being.

verses.sanskrit:
ये यथा मां प्रपद्यन्ते तांस्तथैव भजाम्यहम्।

मम वर्त्मानुवर्तन्ते मनुष्याः पार्थ सर्वशः।।4.11।।
verses.translation: In whatever way they approach Me, in that very way I reward them. O Partha, men follow My path in every way.

verses.sanskrit:
काङ्क्षन्तः कर्मणां सिद्धिं यजन्त इह देवताः।

क्षिप्रं हि मानुषे लोके सिद्धिर्भवति कर्मजा।।4.12।।
verses.translation: Those who desire the fruits of action sacrifice here to the gods; for in this world of men, success born of action comes quickly.

verses.sanskrit:
चातुर्वर्ण्यं मया सृष्टं गुणकर्मविभागशः।

तस्य कर्तारमपि मां विद्ध्यकर्तारमव्ययम्।।4.13।।
verses.translation: The four-fold order (chaturvarnya) was created by Me, according to the division of gunas and actions. Though I am its maker, know Me as the non-doer, imperishable.

verses.sanskrit:
न मां कर्माणि लिम्पन्ति न मे कर्मफले स्पृहा।

इति मां योऽभिजानाति कर्मभिर्न स बध्यते।।4.14।।
verses.translation: Actions do not stain Me; nor is there in Me any craving for the fruits of action. He who knows Me thus is not bound by actions.

verses.sanskrit:
एवं ज्ञात्वा कृतं कर्म पूर्वैरपि मुमुक्षुभिः।

कुरु कर्मैव तस्मात्त्वं पूर्वैः पूर्वतरं कृतम्।।4.15।।
verses.translation: Knowing thus, the ancient seekers of liberation also performed action. Therefore do you also perform action as did the ancients in earlier times.

verses.sanskrit:
किं कर्म किमकर्मेति कवयोऽप्यत्र मोहिताः।

तत्ते कर्म प्रवक्ष्यामि यज्ज्ञात्वा मोक्ष्यसेऽशुभात्।।4.16।।
verses.translation: What is action? What is inaction? Even the wise are confused about this. I shall declare to you that action, knowing which you shall be freed from the inauspicious.

verses.sanskrit:
कर्मणो ह्यपि बोद्धव्यं बोद्धव्यं च विकर्मणः।

अकर्मणश्च बोद्धव्यं गहना कर्मणो गतिः।।4.17।।
verses.translation: For one must understand the nature of action, and also the nature of wrong action, and the nature of inaction. The way of action is deep and hard to fathom.

verses.sanskrit:
कर्मण्यकर्म यः पश्येदकर्मणि च कर्म यः।

स बुद्धिमान् मनुष्येषु स युक्तः कृत्स्नकर्मकृत्।।4.18।।
verses.translation: He who sees inaction in action, and action in inaction — he is wise among men, he is yoked, a performer of all action.

verses.sanskrit:
यस्य सर्वे समारम्भाः कामसङ्कल्पवर्जिताः।

ज्ञानाग्निदग्धकर्माणं तमाहुः पण्डितं बुधाः।।4.19।।
verses.translation: He whose undertakings are all free from desire-based intention, whose actions are burned up in the fire of knowledge — him the wise call a pandit.

verses.sanskrit:
त्यक्त्वा कर्मफलासङ्गं नित्यतृप्तो निराश्रयः।

कर्मण्यभिप्रवृत्तोऽपि नैव किञ्चित्करोति सः।।4.20।।
verses.translation: Abandoning attachment to the fruits of action, ever content, depending on nothing, even when engaged in action, he does nothing at all.

verses.sanskrit:
निराशीर्यतचित्तात्मा त्यक्तसर्वपरिग्रहः।

शारीरं केवलं कर्म कुर्वन्नाप्नोति किल्बिषम्।।4.21।।
verses.translation: Without hope, with mind and self restrained, giving up all possessions, performing action by the body alone, he incurs no sin.

verses.sanskrit:
यदृच्छालाभसन्तुष्टो द्वन्द्वातीतो विमत्सरः।

समः सिद्धावसिद्धौ च कृत्वापि न निबध्यते।।4.22।।
verses.translation: Content with whatever comes unsought, beyond the pairs of opposites, free from envy, even-minded in success and failure — though he acts, he is not bound.

verses.sanskrit:
गतसङ्गस्य मुक्तस्य ज्ञानावस्थितचेतसः।

यज्ञायाचरतः कर्म समग्रं प्रविलीयते।।4.23।।
verses.translation: Of one who is liberated, whose mind is established in knowledge, who acts for yajna, all karma is dissolved away.

verses.sanskrit:
ब्रह्मार्पणं ब्रह्महविर्ब्रह्माग्नौ ब्रह्मणा हुतम्।

ब्रह्मैव तेन गन्तव्यं ब्रह्मकर्मसमाधिना।।4.24।।
verses.translation: The offering is Brahman; the oblation is Brahman; poured by Brahman into the fire of Brahman. Brahman alone is reached by him who sees Brahman in all action.

verses.sanskrit:
दैवमेवापरे यज्ञं योगिनः पर्युपासते।

ब्रह्माग्नावपरे यज्ञं यज्ञेनैवोपजुह्वति।।4.25।।
verses.translation: Some yogis offer sacrifice to the gods alone; others offer sacrifice itself as an oblation in the fire of Brahman.

verses.sanskrit:
श्रोत्रादीनीन्द्रियाण्यन्ये संयमाग्निषु जुह्वति।

शब्दादीन्विषयानन्य इन्द्रियाग्निषु जुह्वति।।4.26।।
verses.translation: Some offer the senses — hearing and the rest — into the fires of restraint; others offer the objects of sense — sound and the rest — into the fires of the senses.

verses.sanskrit:
सर्वाणीन्द्रियकर्माणि प्राणकर्माणि चापरे।

आत्मसंयमयोगाग्नौ जुह्वति ज्ञानदीपिते।।4.27।।
verses.translation: Some offer all the actions of the senses and the actions of the life-breaths into the fire of the yoga of self-restraint, kindled by knowledge.

verses.sanskrit:
द्रव्ययज्ञास्तपोयज्ञा योगयज्ञास्तथापरे।

स्वाध्यायज्ञानयज्ञाश्च यतयः संशितव्रताः।।4.28।।
verses.translation: Some perform sacrifice with wealth, some with austerity, some with yoga, and some with the study of scripture and knowledge — ascetics of strict vows.

verses.sanskrit:
अपाने जुह्वति प्राण प्राणेऽपानं तथाऽपरे।

प्राणापानगती रुद्ध्वा प्राणायामपरायणाः।।4.29।।
verses.translation: Others, devoted to breath-control, having regulated the flow of prana and apana, offer the prana in the apana, and the apana in the prana.

verses.sanskrit:
अपरे नियताहाराः प्राणान्प्राणेषु जुह्वति।

सर्वेऽप्येते यज्ञविदो यज्ञक्षपितकल्मषाः।।4.30।।
verses.translation: Others, restraining their food, offer the pranas in the pranas. All these are knowers of sacrifice, their sins destroyed by sacrifice.

verses.sanskrit:
यज्ञशिष्टामृतभुजो यान्ति ब्रह्म सनातनम्।

नायं लोकोऽस्त्ययज्ञस्य कुतो़ऽन्यः कुरुसत्तम।।4.31।।
verses.translation: Those who eat the nectar of the remnants of sacrifice go to the eternal Brahman. Not even this world is for the non-sacrificer — how then the other, O best of the Kurus?

verses.sanskrit:
एवं बहुविधा यज्ञा वितता ब्रह्मणो मुखे।

कर्मजान्विद्धि तान्सर्वानेवं ज्ञात्वा विमोक्ष्यसे।।4.32।।
verses.translation: Thus, many kinds of sacrifices are spread out at the mouth of Brahman. Know them all as born of action; knowing this, you shall be liberated.

verses.sanskrit:
श्रेयान्द्रव्यमयाद्यज्ञाज्ज्ञानयज्ञः परन्तप।

सर्वं कर्माखिलं पार्थ ज्ञाने परिसमाप्यते।।4.33।।
verses.translation: Better than the sacrifice of wealth is the sacrifice of knowledge, O scorcher of foes. All action in its entirety, O Partha, culminates in knowledge.

verses.sanskrit:
तद्विद्धि प्रणिपातेन परिप्रश्नेन सेवया।

उपदेक्ष्यन्ति ते ज्ञानं ज्ञानिनस्तत्त्वदर्शिनः।।4.34।।
verses.translation: Know that (truth) by prostration, by inquiry, and by service. The wise, the seers of the truth, will instruct you in knowledge.

verses.sanskrit:
यज्ज्ञात्वा न पुनर्मोहमेवं यास्यसि पाण्डव।

येन भूतान्यशेषेण द्रक्ष्यस्यात्मन्यथो मयि।।4.35।।
verses.translation: Having known this, O Pandava, you shall not again fall into delusion; by it you shall see all beings in your Self, and also in Me.

verses.sanskrit:
अपि चेदसि पापेभ्यः सर्वेभ्यः पापकृत्तमः।

सर्वं ज्ञानप्लवेनैव वृजिनं सन्तरिष्यसि।।4.36।।
verses.translation: Even if you were the most sinful of all sinners, with the boat of knowledge alone you shall cross over all evil.

verses.sanskrit:
यथैधांसि समिद्धोऽग्निर्भस्मसात्कुरुतेऽर्जुन।

ज्ञानाग्निः सर्वकर्माणि भस्मसात्कुरुते तथा।।4.37।।
verses.translation: As a kindled fire reduces firewood to ashes, O Arjuna, so does the fire of knowledge reduce all actions to ashes.

verses.sanskrit:
न हि ज्ञानेन सदृशं पवित्रमिह विद्यते।

तत्स्वयं योगसंसिद्धः कालेनात्मनि विन्दति।।4.38।।
verses.translation: Nothing here purifies like knowledge. He who is perfected in yoga finds it in himself, in time, by himself.

verses.sanskrit:
श्रद्धावाँल्लभते ज्ञानं तत्परः संयतेन्द्रियः।

ज्ञानं लब्ध्वा परां शान्तिमचिरेणाधिगच्छति।।4.39।।
verses.translation: The man of faith, devoted to it, with senses restrained, obtains knowledge; having obtained knowledge, he soon attains supreme peace.

verses.sanskrit:
अज्ञश्चाश्रद्दधानश्च संशयात्मा विनश्यति।

नायं लोकोऽस्ति न परो न सुखं संशयात्मनः।।4.40।।
verses.translation: But the ignorant, the faithless, the doubter, perishes. For the doubter there is neither this world, nor the other, nor happiness.

verses.sanskrit:
योगसंन्यस्तकर्माणं ज्ञानसंछिन्नसंशयम्।

आत्मवन्तं न कर्माणि निबध्नन्ति धनञ्जय।।4.41।।
verses.translation: O Dhananjaya, actions do not bind him who has renounced action through yoga, whose doubts have been sundered by knowledge, who is self-possessed.

verses.sanskrit:
तस्मादज्ञानसंभूतं हृत्स्थं ज्ञानासिनाऽऽत्मनः।

छित्त्वैनं संशयं योगमातिष्ठोत्तिष्ठ भारत।।4.42।।
verses.translation: Therefore, sundering with the sword of knowledge this doubt in your heart, born of ignorance, resort to yoga and arise, O Bharata.

chapters.chapter_title_english: The Yoga of Renunciation of Action

verses.sanskrit:
अर्जुन उवाच

संन्यासं कर्मणां कृष्ण पुनर्योगं च शंससि।

यच्छ्रेय एतयोरेकं तन्मे ब्रूहि सुनिश्िचतम्।।5.1।।
verses.translation:
Arjuna said:
O Krishna, You praise the renunciation of actions, and again You praise yoga (of action). Tell me decisively which one of these is the better.

verses.sanskrit:
श्री भगवानुवाच

संन्यासः कर्मयोगश्च निःश्रेयसकरावुभौ।

तयोस्तु कर्मसंन्यासात्कर्मयोगो विशिष्यते।।5.2।।
verses.translation:
The Blessed Lord said:
Renunciation and the yoga of action both lead to the highest bliss. But of the two, the yoga of action is superior to the renunciation of action.

verses.sanskrit:
ज्ञेयः स नित्यसंन्यासी यो न द्वेष्टि न काङ्क्षति।

निर्द्वन्द्वो हि महाबाहो सुखं बन्धात्प्रमुच्यते।।5.3।।
verses.translation: He is to be known as a perpetual sannyasin who neither hates nor desires; free from the pairs of opposites, O mighty-armed one, he is easily freed from bondage.

verses.sanskrit:
सांख्ययोगौ पृथग्बालाः प्रवदन्ति न पण्डिताः।

एकमप्यास्थितः सम्यगुभयोर्विन्दते फलम्।।5.4।।
verses.translation: Children, not the wise, speak of Sankhya and Yoga as different. He who is well established in even one, obtains the fruit of both.

verses.sanskrit:
यत्सांख्यैः प्राप्यते स्थानं तद्योगैरपि गम्यते।

एकं सांख्यं च योगं च यः पश्यति स पश्यति।।5.5।।
verses.translation: The state attained by the followers of Sankhya is also attained by the yogis. He who sees Sankhya and Yoga as one — he truly sees.

verses.sanskrit:
संन्यासस्तु महाबाहो दुःखमाप्तुमयोगतः।

योगयुक्तो मुनिर्ब्रह्म नचिरेणाधिगच्छति।।5.6।।
verses.translation: But renunciation, O mighty-armed one, is hard to attain without yoga. The sage yoked in yoga reaches Brahman in no long time.

verses.sanskrit:
योगयुक्तो विशुद्धात्मा विजितात्मा जितेन्द्रियः।

सर्वभूतात्मभूतात्मा कुर्वन्नपि न लिप्यते।।5.7।।
verses.translation: Yoked in yoga, with purified self, with body and senses conquered, his self becoming the Self of all beings, though acting, he is not tainted.

verses.sanskrit:
नैव किंचित्करोमीति युक्तो मन्येत तत्त्ववित्।

पश्यन् श्रृणवन्स्पृशञ्जिघ्रन्नश्नन्गच्छन्स्वपन् श्वसन्।।5.8।।
verses.translation: The knower of truth, yoked, thinks, 'I do nothing at all' — though seeing, hearing, touching, smelling, eating, walking, sleeping, breathing,

verses.sanskrit:
प्रलपन्विसृजन्गृह्णन्नुन्मिषन्निमिषन्नपि।

इन्द्रियाणीन्द्रियार्थेषु वर्तन्त इति धारयन्।।5.9।।
verses.translation: speaking, releasing, grasping, opening and closing the eyes — convinced that the senses move among the objects of sense.

verses.sanskrit:
ब्रह्मण्याधाय कर्माणि सङ्गं त्यक्त्वा करोति यः।

लिप्यते न स पापेन पद्मपत्रमिवाम्भसा।।5.10।।
verses.translation: He who, abandoning attachment, performs actions surrendering them to Brahman, is not tainted by sin, as a lotus-leaf is not wetted by water.

verses.sanskrit:
कायेन मनसा बुद्ध्या केवलैरिन्द्रियैरपि।

योगिनः कर्म कुर्वन्ति सङ्गं त्यक्त्वाऽऽत्मशुद्धये।।5.11।।
verses.translation: With the body, mind, intellect, and the senses alone, the yogis perform action, abandoning attachment, for the purification of the self.

verses.sanskrit:
युक्तः कर्मफलं त्यक्त्वा शान्तिमाप्नोति नैष्ठिकीम्।

अयुक्तः कामकारेण फले सक्तो निबध्यते।।5.12।।
verses.translation: The yoked one, abandoning the fruit of action, attains lasting peace. The unyoked one, attached to the fruit by desire, is bound.

verses.sanskrit:
सर्वकर्माणि मनसा संन्यस्यास्ते सुखं वशी।

नवद्वारे पुरे देही नैव कुर्वन्न कारयन्।।5.13।।
verses.translation: Mentally renouncing all actions, the embodied one, self-controlled, rests happily in the city of nine gates (the body), neither acting nor causing action.

verses.sanskrit:
न कर्तृत्वं न कर्माणि लोकस्य सृजति प्रभुः।

न कर्मफलसंयोगं स्वभावस्तु प्रवर्तते।।5.14।।
verses.translation: The Lord (of the body) creates neither agency nor actions for the world, nor the union of action with its fruit; it is nature that acts.

verses.sanskrit:
नादत्ते कस्यचित्पापं न चैव सुकृतं विभुः।

अज्ञानेनावृतं ज्ञानं तेन मुह्यन्ति जन्तवः।।5.15।।
verses.translation: The all-pervading One takes on neither the sin nor the merit of anyone. Knowledge is veiled by ignorance; thereby beings are deluded.

verses.sanskrit:
ज्ञानेन तु तदज्ञानं येषां नाशितमात्मनः।

तेषामादित्यवज्ज्ञानं प्रकाशयति तत्परम्।।5.16।।
verses.translation: But for those whose ignorance has been destroyed by the knowledge of the Self, that knowledge, like the sun, illumines the Supreme.

verses.sanskrit:
तद्बुद्धयस्तदात्मानस्तन्निष्ठास्तत्परायणाः।

गच्छन्त्यपुनरावृत्तिं ज्ञाननिर्धूतकल्मषाः।।5.17।।
verses.translation: Thinking of That, their self rooted in That, with That as their goal, devoted to That, they go whence there is no return, their sins wiped away by knowledge.

verses.sanskrit:
विद्याविनयसंपन्ने ब्राह्मणे गवि हस्तिनि।

शुनि चैव श्वपाके च पण्डिताः समदर्शिनः।।5.18।।
verses.translation: The wise look equally upon a learned and humble brahmin, a cow, an elephant, a dog, and an eater of dogs.

verses.sanskrit:
इहैव तैर्जितः सर्गो येषां साम्ये स्थितं मनः।

निर्दोषं हि समं ब्रह्म तस्माद्ब्रह्मणि ते स्थिताः।।5.19।।
verses.translation: Even here in this world, birth is conquered by those whose minds are fixed in evenness. Brahman is spotless and equal; therefore they are established in Brahman.

verses.sanskrit:
न प्रहृष्येत्प्रियं प्राप्य नोद्विजेत्प्राप्य चाप्रियम्।

स्थिरबुद्धिरसम्मूढो ब्रह्मविद्ब्रह्मणि स्थितः।।5.20।।
verses.translation: The knower of Brahman, established in Brahman, with steady intellect and unbewildered, rejoices not on gaining the pleasant, nor grieves on meeting the unpleasant.

verses.sanskrit:
बाह्यस्पर्शेष्वसक्तात्मा विन्दत्यात्मनि यत्सुखम्।

स ब्रह्मयोगयुक्तात्मा सुखमक्षयमश्नुते।।5.21।।
verses.translation: With the self unattached to external contacts, he finds the joy that is in the Self. His self yoked in the yoga of Brahman, he enjoys imperishable happiness.

verses.sanskrit:
ये हि संस्पर्शजा भोगा दुःखयोनय एव ते।

आद्यन्तवन्तः कौन्तेय न तेषु रमते बुधः।।5.22।।
verses.translation: For the enjoyments born of contact are indeed wombs of pain, they have a beginning and an end. The wise, O son of Kunti, do not delight in them.

verses.sanskrit:
शक्नोतीहैव यः सोढुं प्राक्शरीरविमोक्षणात्।

कामक्रोधोद्भवं वेगं स युक्तः स सुखी नरः।।5.23।।
verses.translation: He who, even here, before release from the body, can withstand the impulse born of desire and anger — he is yoked, he is a happy man.

verses.sanskrit:
योऽन्तःसुखोऽन्तरारामस्तथान्तर्ज्योतिरेव यः।

स योगी ब्रह्मनिर्वाणं ब्रह्मभूतोऽधिगच्छति।।5.24।।
verses.translation: He whose happiness is within, whose delight is within, whose light is within — that yogi, becoming Brahman, attains the nirvana of Brahman.

verses.sanskrit:
लभन्ते ब्रह्मनिर्वाणमृषयः क्षीणकल्मषाः।

छिन्नद्वैधा यतात्मानः सर्वभूतहिते रताः।।5.25।।
verses.translation: The rishis whose sins are destroyed, whose doubts are dispelled, whose selves are disciplined, who delight in the welfare of all beings, attain the nirvana of Brahman.

verses.sanskrit:
कामक्रोधवियुक्तानां यतीनां यतचेतसाम्।

अभितो ब्रह्मनिर्वाणं वर्तते विदितात्मनाम्।।5.26।।
verses.translation: For those ascetics freed from desire and anger, who have subdued their minds, who know the Self, the nirvana of Brahman exists all around.

verses.sanskrit:
स्पर्शान्कृत्वा बहिर्बाह्यांश्चक्षुश्चैवान्तरे भ्रुवोः।

प्राणापानौ समौ कृत्वा नासाभ्यन्तरचारिणौ।।5.27।।
verses.translation: Shutting out external contacts, fixing the gaze between the eyebrows, equalizing the outgoing and incoming breaths moving within the nostrils,

verses.sanskrit:
यतेन्द्रियमनोबुद्धिर्मुनिर्मोक्षपरायणः।

विगतेच्छाभयक्रोधो यः सदा मुक्त एव सः।।5.28।।
verses.translation: with the senses, mind, and intellect controlled, the sage devoted to liberation, free from desire, fear, and anger — he is indeed ever liberated.

verses.sanskrit:
भोक्तारं यज्ञतपसां सर्वलोकमहेश्वरम्।

सुहृदं सर्वभूतानां ज्ञात्वा मां शान्तिमृच्छति।।5.29।।
verses.translation: Knowing Me as the enjoyer of sacrifices and austerities, the great Lord of all the worlds, the friend of all beings — he attains peace.

verses.sanskrit:
श्री भगवानुवाच

अनाश्रितः कर्मफलं कार्यं कर्म करोति यः।

स संन्यासी च योगी च न निरग्निर्न चाक्रियः।।6.1।।
verses.translation:
The Blessed Lord said:
He who performs his prescribed duty without depending on its fruit — he is a sannyasin, he is a yogi; not he who is without (sacrificial) fire, nor without action.

verses.sanskrit:
यं संन्यासमिति प्राहुर्योगं तं विद्धि पाण्डव।

न ह्यसंन्यस्तसङ्कल्पो योगी भवति कश्चन।।6.2।।
verses.translation: Know that what is called renunciation to be yoga, O Pandava. For no one becomes a yogi without renouncing the intention (toward fruit).

verses.sanskrit:
आरुरुक्षोर्मुनेर्योगं कर्म कारणमुच्यते।

योगारूढस्य तस्यैव शमः कारणमुच्यते।।6.3।।
verses.translation: For the sage wishing to ascend to yoga, action is said to be the means. For one who has ascended to yoga, quietude is said to be the means.

verses.sanskrit:
यदा हि नेन्द्रियार्थेषु न कर्मस्वनुषज्जते।

सर्वसङ्कल्पसंन्यासी योगारूढस्तदोच्यते।।6.4।।
verses.translation: When one is attached neither to objects of sense nor to actions, renouncing all intentions, then he is said to have ascended to yoga.

verses.sanskrit:
उद्धरेदात्मनाऽऽत्मानं नात्मानमवसादयेत्।

आत्मैव ह्यात्मनो बन्धुरात्मैव रिपुरात्मनः।।6.5।।
verses.translation: Let a man raise himself by his own Self; let him not degrade himself. The Self alone is the friend of the self, and the Self alone is the enemy of the self.

verses.sanskrit:
बन्धुरात्माऽऽत्मनस्तस्य येनात्मैवात्मना जितः।

अनात्मनस्तु शत्रुत्वे वर्तेतात्मैव शत्रुवत्।।6.6।।
verses.translation: The Self is the friend of the self for him who has conquered himself by the Self; but to him whose self is unconquered, his own Self acts with hostility, as an enemy.

verses.sanskrit:
जितात्मनः प्रशान्तस्य परमात्मा समाहितः।

शीतोष्णसुखदुःखेषु तथा मानापमानयोः।।6.7।।
verses.translation: The self-controlled, the peaceful one, is steady in the Supreme Self, in cold and heat, pleasure and pain, honor and dishonor.

verses.sanskrit:
ज्ञानविज्ञानतृप्तात्मा कूटस्थो विजितेन्द्रियः।

युक्त इत्युच्यते योगी समलोष्टाश्मकाञ्चनः।।6.8।।
verses.translation: The yogi, content with knowledge and realization, unshaken, his senses conquered, to whom a clod, a stone, and gold are equal — he is said to be yoked.

verses.sanskrit:
सुहृन्मित्रार्युदासीनमध्यस्थद्वेष्यबन्धुषु।

साधुष्वपि च पापेषु समबुद्धिर्विशिष्यते।।6.9।।
verses.translation: He who regards with equal mind well-wishers, friends, foes, neutrals, mediators, the hateful, relatives, the righteous, and the sinful — he excels.

verses.sanskrit:
योगी युञ्जीत सततमात्मानं रहसि स्थितः।

एकाकी यतचित्तात्मा निराशीरपरिग्रहः।।6.10।।
verses.translation: Let the yogi constantly concentrate his mind, staying alone in a secret place, with mind and body restrained, without possessions, free from longing.

verses.sanskrit:
शुचौ देशे प्रतिष्ठाप्य स्थिरमासनमात्मनः।

नात्युच्छ्रितं नातिनीचं चैलाजिनकुशोत्तरम्।।6.11।।
verses.translation: Establishing for himself a firm seat in a clean place, neither too high nor too low, covered with cloth, a deer-skin, and kusha grass, one upon the other,

verses.sanskrit:
तत्रैकाग्रं मनः कृत्वा यतचित्तेन्द्रियक्रियः।

उपविश्यासने युञ्ज्याद्योगमात्मविशुद्धये।।6.12।।
verses.translation: sitting there on that seat, making the mind one-pointed, restraining the actions of the senses and the mind, let him practice yoga for the purification of the self.

verses.sanskrit:
समं कायशिरोग्रीवं धारयन्नचलं स्थिरः।

संप्रेक्ष्य नासिकाग्रं स्वं दिशश्चानवलोकयन्।।6.13।।
verses.translation: Holding the body, head, and neck erect and still, gazing at the tip of his own nose, not looking in any direction,

verses.sanskrit:
प्रशान्तात्मा विगतभीर्ब्रह्मचारिव्रते स्थितः।

मनः संयम्य मच्चित्तो युक्त आसीत मत्परः।।6.14।।
verses.translation: with serene self, fearless, firm in the vow of celibacy, mind restrained, his thoughts fixed on Me, let him sit, yoked, intent on Me as the supreme.

verses.sanskrit:
युञ्जन्नेवं सदाऽऽत्मानं योगी नियतमानसः।

शान्तिं निर्वाणपरमां मत्संस्थामधिगच्छति।।6.15।।
verses.translation: Ever yoking himself thus, the yogi of disciplined mind attains the peace abiding in Me, which ends in nirvana.

verses.sanskrit:
नात्यश्नतस्तु योगोऽस्ति न चैकान्तमनश्नतः।

न चातिस्वप्नशीलस्य जाग्रतो नैव चार्जुन।।6.16।।
verses.translation: Yoga is not for him who eats too much, nor for him who eats nothing at all; not for him who sleeps too much, nor for him who keeps awake, O Arjuna.

verses.sanskrit:
युक्ताहारविहारस्य युक्तचेष्टस्य कर्मसु।

युक्तस्वप्नावबोधस्य योगो भवति दुःखहा।।6.17।।
verses.translation: For him whose food and recreation are measured, whose effort in actions is measured, whose sleep and waking are measured — yoga is the destroyer of sorrow.

verses.sanskrit:
यदा विनियतं चित्तमात्मन्येवावतिष्ठते।

निःस्पृहः सर्वकामेभ्यो युक्त इत्युच्यते तदा।।6.18।।
verses.translation: When the disciplined mind is established in the Self alone, free from longing for any desire — then is one said to be yoked.

verses.sanskrit:
यथा दीपो निवातस्थो नेङ्गते सोपमा स्मृता।

योगिनो यतचित्तस्य युञ्जतो योगमात्मनः।।6.19।।
verses.translation: As a lamp in a windless place does not flicker — this simile is used for the yoked mind of the yogi practicing concentration on the Self.

verses.sanskrit:
यत्रोपरमते चित्तं निरुद्धं योगसेवया।

यत्र चैवात्मनाऽऽत्मानं पश्यन्नात्मनि तुष्यति।।6.20।।
verses.translation: That state in which the mind ceases, restrained by the practice of yoga, in which, seeing the Self by the Self, one is satisfied in the Self;

verses.sanskrit:
सुखमात्यन्तिकं यत्तद्बुद्धिग्राह्यमतीन्द्रियम्।

वेत्ति यत्र न चैवायं स्थितश्चलति तत्त्वतः।।6.21।।
verses.translation: that in which he knows that infinite bliss, grasped by the intellect, beyond the senses, and wherein, established, he never wanders from the truth;

verses.sanskrit:
यं लब्ध्वा चापरं लाभं मन्यते नाधिकं ततः।

यस्मिन्स्थितो न दुःखेन गुरुणापि विचाल्यते।।6.22।।
verses.translation: that, which having gained, he thinks there is no greater gain; established in which, he is not shaken even by heavy sorrow;

verses.sanskrit:
तं विद्याद् दुःखसंयोगवियोगं योगसंज्ञितम्।

स निश्चयेन योक्तव्यो योगोऽनिर्विण्णचेतसा।।6.23।।
verses.translation: that severance from the union with pain should be known as yoga. This yoga is to be practiced with resolute heart and without despondency.

verses.sanskrit:
सङ्कल्पप्रभवान्कामांस्त्यक्त्वा सर्वानशेषतः।

मनसैवेन्द्रियग्रामं विनियम्य समन्ततः।।6.24।।
verses.translation: Abandoning, without reserve, all desires born of intention, restraining the whole host of senses with the mind on every side,

verses.sanskrit:
शनैः शनैरुपरमेद् बुद्ध्या धृतिगृहीतया।

आत्मसंस्थं मनः कृत्वा न किञ्चिदपि चिन्तयेत्।।6.25।।
verses.translation: let him gradually become quiet by the intellect held firm. With the mind fixed on the Self, let him not think of anything.

verses.sanskrit:
यतो यतो निश्चरति मनश्चञ्चलमस्थिरम्।

ततस्ततो नियम्यैतदात्मन्येव वशं नयेत्।।6.26।।
verses.translation: Whatever makes the wavering and unsteady mind wander away — from that let him restrain it, bring it back under the control of the Self alone.

verses.sanskrit:
प्रशान्तमनसं ह्येनं योगिनं सुखमुत्तमम्।

उपैति शान्तरजसं ब्रह्मभूतमकल्मषम्।।6.27।।
verses.translation: Supreme bliss indeed comes to the yogi whose mind is perfectly peaceful, whose passion is stilled, who has become Brahman, free from stain.

verses.sanskrit:
युञ्जन्नेवं सदाऽऽत्मानं योगी विगतकल्मषः।

सुखेन ब्रह्मसंस्पर्शमत्यन्तं सुखमश्नुते।।6.28।।
verses.translation: Thus constantly yoking the self, the yogi, his sins cast off, easily attains the infinite bliss of contact with Brahman.

verses.sanskrit:
सर्वभूतस्थमात्मानं सर्वभूतानि चात्मनि।

ईक्षते योगयुक्तात्मा सर्वत्र समदर्शनः।।6.29।।
verses.translation: He whose self is yoked in yoga, who sees the same everywhere, sees the Self abiding in all beings, and all beings in the Self.

verses.sanskrit:
यो मां पश्यति सर्वत्र सर्वं च मयि पश्यति।

तस्याहं न प्रणश्यामि स च मे न प्रणश्यति।।6.30।।
verses.translation: He who sees Me everywhere and sees all in Me — for him I am not lost, and he is not lost to Me.

verses.sanskrit:
सर्वभूतस्थितं यो मां भजत्येकत्वमास्थितः।

सर्वथा वर्तमानोऽपि स योगी मयि वर्तते।।6.31।।
verses.translation: He who, established in oneness, worships Me as abiding in all beings — that yogi, however he dwells, dwells in Me.

verses.sanskrit:
आत्मौपम्येन सर्वत्र समं पश्यति योऽर्जुन।

सुखं वा यदि वा दुःखं सः योगी परमो मतः।।6.32।।
verses.translation: He who, by the analogy with his own self, O Arjuna, sees equality everywhere, whether in pleasure or in pain — he is regarded as the highest yogi.

verses.sanskrit:
अर्जुन उवाच

योऽयं योगस्त्वया प्रोक्तः साम्येन मधुसूदन।

एतस्याहं न पश्यामि चञ्चलत्वात् स्थितिं स्थिराम्।।6.33।।
verses.translation:
Arjuna said:
O Madhusudana, this yoga of evenness which You have declared — I do not see its firm abiding, because of the restlessness of the mind.

verses.sanskrit:
चञ्चलं हि मनः कृष्ण प्रमाथि बलवद्दृढम्।

तस्याहं निग्रहं मन्ये वायोरिव सुदुष्करम्।।6.34।।
verses.translation: The mind is indeed restless, O Krishna, turbulent, strong, and obstinate. To control it, I think, is as hard as to control the wind.

verses.sanskrit:
श्री भगवानुवाच

असंशयं महाबाहो मनो दुर्निग्रहं चलं।

अभ्यासेन तु कौन्तेय वैराग्येण च गृह्यते।।6.35।।
verses.translation:
The Blessed Lord said:
Undoubtedly, O mighty-armed one, the mind is hard to restrain and restless. But by practice (abhyasa), O son of Kunti, and by dispassion (vairagya), it is brought under control.

verses.sanskrit:
असंयतात्मना योगो दुष्प्राप इति मे मतिः।

वश्यात्मना तु यतता शक्योऽवाप्तुमुपायतः।।6.36।।
verses.translation: Yoga is hard to attain, I agree, for one whose self is unsubdued. But by him who strives, his self subdued, it can be attained by means.

verses.sanskrit:
अर्जुन उवाच

अयतिः श्रद्धयोपेतो योगाच्चलितमानसः।

अप्राप्य योगसंसिद्धिं कां गतिं कृष्ण गच्छति।।6.37।।
verses.translation:
Arjuna said:
He who strives with faith, but whose mind wanders away from yoga, and who does not attain perfection in yoga — what path, O Krishna, does he tread?

verses.sanskrit:
कच्चिन्नोभयविभ्रष्टश्छिन्नाभ्रमिव नश्यति।

अप्रतिष्ठो महाबाहो विमूढो ब्रह्मणः पथि।।6.38।।
verses.translation: Fallen from both, does he not perish like a rent cloud, O mighty-armed one, without support, deluded on the path of Brahman?

verses.sanskrit:
एतन्मे संशयं कृष्ण छेत्तुमर्हस्यशेषतः।

त्वदन्यः संशयस्यास्य छेत्ता न ह्युपपद्यते।।6.39।।
verses.translation: O Krishna, please dispel completely this doubt of mine. None other than You can be found to dispel this doubt.

verses.sanskrit:
श्री भगवानुवाच

पार्थ नैवेह नामुत्र विनाशस्तस्य विद्यते।

नहि कल्याणकृत्कश्िचद्दुर्गतिं तात गच्छति।।6.40।।
verses.translation:
The Blessed Lord said:
O Partha, neither in this world nor in the next does he perish. None who does good, My son, ever comes to grief.

verses.sanskrit:
प्राप्य पुण्यकृतां लोकानुषित्वा शाश्वतीः समाः।

शुचीनां श्रीमतां गेहे योगभ्रष्टोऽभिजायते।।6.41।।
verses.translation: Having reached the worlds of the righteous and dwelt there for countless years, he who has fallen from yoga is born in the home of the pure and the prosperous.

verses.sanskrit:
अथवा योगिनामेव कुले भवति धीमताम्।

एतद्धि दुर्लभतरं लोके जन्म यदीदृशम्।।6.42।।
verses.translation: Or he may even be born into a family of wise yogis. Such a birth as this is very hard to attain in the world.

verses.sanskrit:
तत्र तं बुद्धिसंयोगं लभते पौर्वदेहिकम्।

यतते च ततो भूयः संसिद्धौ कुरुनन्दन।।6.43।।
verses.translation: There he regains the understanding acquired in his former body and strives once again, O joy of the Kurus, for perfection.

verses.sanskrit:
पूर्वाभ्यासेन तेनैव ह्रियते ह्यवशोऽपि सः।

जिज्ञासुरपि योगस्य शब्दब्रह्मातिवर्तते।।6.44।।
verses.translation: By that very former practice he is carried on, even against his will. One who merely inquires about yoga surpasses the one who follows only the Word of the Vedas.

verses.sanskrit:
प्रयत्नाद्यतमानस्तु योगी संशुद्धकिल्बिषः।

अनेकजन्मसंसिद्धस्ततो याति परां गतिम्।।6.45।।
verses.translation: But the yogi, striving with diligence, purified of his sins, perfected through many births, then attains the supreme goal.

verses.sanskrit:
तपस्विभ्योऽधिको योगी ज्ञानिभ्योऽपि मतोऽधिकः।

कर्मिभ्यश्चाधिको योगी तस्माद्योगी भवार्जुन।।6.46।।
verses.translation: The yogi is greater than the ascetics, greater even than those of knowledge, greater than the ritualists. Therefore be a yogi, O Arjuna.

verses.sanskrit:
योगिनामपि सर्वेषां मद्गतेनान्तरात्मना।

श्रद्धावान्भजते यो मां स मे युक्ततमो मतः।।6.47।।
verses.translation: And of all yogis, he who, full of faith, worships Me with his inmost self absorbed in Me — him I consider most deeply yoked.

chapters.chapter_title_english: The Yoga of Knowledge and Realization

verses.sanskrit:
श्री भगवानुवाच

मय्यासक्तमनाः पार्थ योगं युञ्जन्मदाश्रयः।

असंशयं समग्रं मां यथा ज्ञास्यसि तच्छृणु।।7.1।।
verses.translation:
The Blessed Lord said:
O Partha, with mind fixed on Me, taking refuge in Me, practicing yoga, hear how you shall know Me fully, without doubt.

verses.sanskrit:
ज्ञानं तेऽहं सविज्ञानमिदं वक्ष्याम्यशेषतः।

यज्ज्ञात्वा नेह भूयोऽन्यज्ज्ञातव्यमवशिष्यते।।7.2।।
verses.translation: I shall declare to you in full this knowledge together with realization, knowing which nothing further remains to be known here.

verses.sanskrit:
मनुष्याणां सहस्रेषु कश्िचद्यतति सिद्धये।

यततामपि सिद्धानां कश्िचन्मां वेत्ति तत्त्वतः।।7.3।।
verses.translation: Among thousands of men, scarcely one strives for perfection; even among those who strive and are perfected, scarcely one knows Me in truth.

verses.sanskrit:
भूमिरापोऽनलो वायुः खं मनो बुद्धिरेव च।

अहङ्कार इतीयं मे भिन्ना प्रकृतिरष्टधा।।7.4।।
verses.translation: Earth, water, fire, air, ether, mind, intellect, and the sense of 'I' — thus is My prakriti divided eightfold.

verses.sanskrit:
अपरेयमितस्त्वन्यां प्रकृतिं विद्धि मे पराम्।

जीवभूतां महाबाहो ययेदं धार्यते जगत्।।7.5।।
verses.translation: This is the lower. But know, O mighty-armed one, My other, higher prakriti — the life-principle (jiva) by which this universe is upheld.

verses.sanskrit:
एतद्योनीनि भूतानि सर्वाणीत्युपधारय।

अहं कृत्स्नस्य जगतः प्रभवः प्रलयस्तथा।।7.6।।
verses.translation: Know that all beings have these two as their source. I am the origin of the entire universe, and also its dissolution.

verses.sanskrit:
मत्तः परतरं नान्यत्किञ्चिदस्ति धनञ्जय।

मयि सर्वमिदं प्रोतं सूत्रे मणिगणा इव।।7.7।।
verses.translation: There is nothing whatever higher than Me, O Dhananjaya. All this is strung on Me like rows of jewels on a thread.

verses.sanskrit:
रसोऽहमप्सु कौन्तेय प्रभास्मि शशिसूर्ययोः।

प्रणवः सर्ववेदेषु शब्दः खे पौरुषं नृषु।।7.8।।
verses.translation: O son of Kunti, I am the taste in water, the light in the moon and the sun, the sacred syllable Om in all the Vedas, the sound in space, manliness in men.

verses.sanskrit:
पुण्यो गन्धः पृथिव्यां च तेजश्चास्मि विभावसौ।

जीवनं सर्वभूतेषु तपश्चास्मि तपस्विषु।।7.9।।
verses.translation: I am the sweet fragrance of the earth, the brilliance in fire; the life in all beings, the austerity in ascetics.

verses.sanskrit:
बीजं मां सर्वभूतानां विद्धि पार्थ सनातनम्।

बुद्धिर्बुद्धिमतामस्मि तेजस्तेजस्विनामहम्।।7.10।।
verses.translation: Know Me, O Partha, as the eternal seed of all beings. I am the intelligence of the intelligent, the splendor of the splendid.

verses.sanskrit:
बलं बलवतां चाहं कामरागविवर्जितम्।

धर्माविरुद्धो भूतेषु कामोऽस्मि भरतर्षभ।।7.11।।
verses.translation: I am the strength of the strong, devoid of desire and passion. In beings, O bull of the Bharatas, I am desire that is not contrary to dharma.

verses.sanskrit:
ये चैव सात्त्विका भावा राजसास्तामसाश्च ये।

मत्त एवेति तान्विद्धि नत्वहं तेषु ते मयि।।7.12।।
verses.translation: Whatever states of being there are, of sattva, rajas, and tamas — know them to come from Me alone. But I am not in them; they are in Me.

verses.sanskrit:
त्रिभिर्गुणमयैर्भावैरेभिः सर्वमिदं जगत्।

मोहितं नाभिजानाति मामेभ्यः परमव्ययम्।।7.13।।
verses.translation: Deluded by these three states of the gunas, this whole world does not recognize Me, who am beyond them, imperishable.

verses.sanskrit:
दैवी ह्येषा गुणमयी मम माया दुरत्यया।

मामेव ये प्रपद्यन्ते मायामेतां तरन्ति ते।।7.14।।
verses.translation: This divine maya of Mine, consisting of the gunas, is hard to cross over. They who take refuge in Me alone cross over this maya.

verses.sanskrit:
न मां दुष्कृतिनो मूढाः प्रपद्यन्ते नराधमाः।

माययापहृतज्ञाना आसुरं भावमाश्रिताः।।7.15।।
verses.translation: The evildoers, the deluded, the lowest of men, do not take refuge in Me; their knowledge is carried away by maya; they cling to demonic nature.

verses.sanskrit:
चतुर्विधा भजन्ते मां जनाः सुकृतिनोऽर्जुन।

आर्तो जिज्ञासुरर्थार्थी ज्ञानी च भरतर्षभ।।7.16।।
verses.translation: Four kinds of virtuous men worship Me, O Arjuna: the distressed, the seeker of knowledge, the seeker of wealth, and the wise, O bull of the Bharatas.

verses.sanskrit:
तेषां ज्ञानी नित्ययुक्त एकभक्ितर्विशिष्यते।

प्रियो हि ज्ञानिनोऽत्यर्थमहं स च मम प्रियः।।7.17।।
