verses.translation: Of them, the wise man, ever yoked, devoted to the One, excels. To the wise I am exceedingly dear, and he is dear to Me.

verses.sanskrit:
उदाराः सर्व एवैते ज्ञानी त्वात्मैव मे मतम्।

आस्थितः स हि युक्तात्मा मामेवानुत्तमां गतिम्।।7.18।।
verses.translation: All these are noble; but the wise I hold to be My very Self. For he, with steady mind, is established in Me alone, the supreme goal.

verses.sanskrit:
बहूनां जन्मनामन्ते ज्ञानवान्मां प्रपद्यते।

वासुदेवः सर्वमिति स महात्मा सुदुर्लभः।।7.19।।
verses.translation: At the end of many births, the man of knowledge takes refuge in Me, seeing 'Vasudeva is all this.' Such a great soul is very hard to find.

verses.sanskrit:
कामैस्तैस्तैर्हृतज्ञानाः प्रपद्यन्तेऽन्यदेवताः।

तं तं नियममास्थाय प्रकृत्या नियताः स्वया।।7.20।।
verses.translation: Those whose knowledge is carried away by this and that desire resort to other deities, adopting various practices, constrained by their own nature.

verses.sanskrit:
यो यो यां यां तनुं भक्तः श्रद्धयार्चितुमिच्छति।

तस्य तस्याचलां श्रद्धां तामेव विदधाम्यहम्।।7.21।।
verses.translation: Whatever form any devotee with faith desires to worship — that same faith I make steady in him.

verses.sanskrit:
स तया श्रद्धया युक्तस्तस्याराधनमीहते।

लभते च ततः कामान्मयैव विहितान् हि तान्।।7.22।।
verses.translation: Endowed with that faith, he strives for the grace of that deity; and from that he obtains those desired fruits — ordained by Me alone.

verses.sanskrit:
अन्तवत्तु फलं तेषां तद्भवत्यल्पमेधसाम्।

देवान्देवयजो यान्ति मद्भक्ता यान्ति मामपि।।7.23।।
verses.translation: But the fruit that accrues to them, men of small understanding, is finite. Worshippers of the gods go to the gods; My devotees come to Me.

verses.sanskrit:
अव्यक्तं व्यक्ितमापन्नं मन्यन्ते मामबुद्धयः।

परं भावमजानन्तो ममाव्ययमनुत्तमम्।।7.24।।
verses.translation: The foolish regard Me, the unmanifest, as having come into manifestation, not knowing My higher, imperishable, and supreme nature.

verses.sanskrit:
नाहं प्रकाशः सर्वस्य योगमायासमावृतः।

मूढोऽयं नाभिजानाति लोको मामजमव्ययम्।।7.25।।
verses.translation: I am not manifest to all, veiled as I am by My yoga-maya. This deluded world does not know Me, the unborn, the imperishable.

verses.sanskrit:
वेदाहं समतीतानि वर्तमानानि चार्जुन।

भविष्याणि च भूतानि मां तु वेद न कश्चन।।7.26।।
verses.translation: O Arjuna, I know the beings of the past, present, and future; but no one knows Me.

verses.sanskrit:
इच्छाद्वेषसमुत्थेन द्वन्द्वमोहेन भारत।

सर्वभूतानि संमोहं सर्गे यान्ति परन्तप।।7.27।।
verses.translation: Deluded by the pairs of opposites that arise from desire and aversion, O Bharata, all beings in creation are bewildered, O scorcher of foes.

verses.sanskrit:
येषां त्वन्तगतं पापं जनानां पुण्यकर्मणाम्।

ते द्वन्द्वमोहनिर्मुक्ता भजन्ते मां दृढव्रताः।।7.28।।
verses.translation: But those of virtuous action, whose sin has come to an end, freed from the delusion of the pairs of opposites, worship Me with firm vows.

verses.sanskrit:
जरामरणमोक्षाय मामाश्रित्य यतन्ति ये।

ते ब्रह्म तद्विदुः कृत्स्नमध्यात्मं कर्म चाखिलम्।।7.29।।
verses.translation: Those who strive for liberation from old age and death, taking refuge in Me — they know Brahman entire, the Adhyatma (the Self), and all action.

verses.sanskrit:
साधिभूताधिदैवं मां साधियज्ञं च ये विदुः।

प्रयाणकालेऽपि च मां ते विदुर्युक्तचेतसः।।7.30।।
verses.translation: Those who know Me as the Adhibhuta (principle of beings), the Adhidaiva (of gods), and the Adhiyajna (of sacrifices), with yoked mind, know Me even at the hour of death.

chapters.chapter_title_english: The Yoga of the Imperishable Brahman

verses.sanskrit:
अर्जुन उवाच

किं तद्ब्रह्म किमध्यात्मं किं कर्म पुरुषोत्तम।

अधिभूतं च किं प्रोक्तमधिदैवं किमुच्यते।।8.1।।
verses.translation:
Arjuna said:
What is that Brahman? What is the Adhyatma? What is action, O best of men? What is said to be the Adhibhuta? And what is called the Adhidaiva?

verses.sanskrit:
अधियज्ञः कथं कोऽत्र देहेऽस्मिन्मधुसूदन।

प्रयाणकाले च कथं ज्ञेयोऽसि नियतात्मभिः।।8.2।।
verses.translation: What is the Adhiyajna here, and how, in this body, O Madhusudana? And how, at the hour of death, are You to be known by the self-controlled?

verses.sanskrit:
श्री भगवानुवाच

अक्षरं ब्रह्म परमं स्वभावोऽध्यात्ममुच्यते।

भूतभावोद्भवकरो विसर्गः कर्मसंज्ञितः।।8.3।।
verses.translation:
The Blessed Lord said:
The Imperishable is the supreme Brahman; its nature is called Adhyatma; the offering that causes the origination of beings is called karma.

verses.sanskrit:
अधिभूतं क्षरो भावः पुरुषश्चाधिदैवतम्।

अधियज्ञोऽहमेवात्र देहे देहभृतां वर।।8.4।।
verses.translation: The Adhibhuta is the perishable nature; the Adhidaiva is the Purusha (cosmic Person); the Adhiyajna here in the body is I Myself, O best of the embodied.

verses.sanskrit:
अन्तकाले च मामेव स्मरन्मुक्त्वा कलेवरम्।

यः प्रयाति स मद्भावं याति नास्त्यत्र संशयः।।8.5।।
verses.translation: He who, at the hour of death, remembering Me alone, leaves the body and departs, attains My state of being — of this there is no doubt.

verses.sanskrit:
यं यं वापि स्मरन्भावं त्यजत्यन्ते कलेवरम्।

तं तमेवैति कौन्तेय सदा तद्भावभावितः।।8.6।।
verses.translation: Whatever state of being one remembers at the last moment when leaving the body — to that very one he goes, O son of Kunti, ever reflected upon.

verses.sanskrit:
तस्मात्सर्वेषु कालेषु मामनुस्मर युध्य च।

मय्यर्पितमनोबुद्धिर्मामेवैष्यस्यसंशयम्।।8.7।।
verses.translation: Therefore at all times remember Me and fight. With mind and intellect fixed on Me, you shall surely come to Me.

verses.sanskrit:
अभ्यासयोगयुक्तेन चेतसा नान्यगामिना।

परमं पुरुषं दिव्यं याति पार्थानुचिन्तयन्।।8.8।।
verses.translation: He whose mind, yoked by the practice of yoga, does not wander to anything else, reaches the supreme Divine Purusha, O Partha, meditating upon Him.

verses.sanskrit:
कविं पुराणमनुशासितार

मणोरणीयांसमनुस्मरेद्यः।

सर्वस्य धातारमचिन्त्यरूप

मादित्यवर्णं तमसः परस्तात्।।8.9।।
verses.translation: He who meditates on the omniscient, the ancient, the ruler, smaller than the smallest, the sustainer of all, of inconceivable form, sun-colored, beyond darkness,

verses.sanskrit:
प्रयाणकाले मनसाऽचलेन

भक्त्या युक्तो योगबलेन चैव।

भ्रुवोर्मध्ये प्राणमावेश्य सम्यक्

स तं परं पुरुषमुपैति दिव्यम्।।8.10।।
verses.translation: at the hour of death, with a steady mind, yoked by devotion and by the power of yoga, drawing the life-breath fully between the eyebrows — he reaches the Supreme Divine Purusha.

verses.sanskrit:
यदक्षरं वेदविदो वदन्ति

विशन्ति यद्यतयो वीतरागाः।

यदिच्छन्तो ब्रह्मचर्यं चरन्ति

तत्ते पदं संग्रहेण प्रवक्ष्ये।।8.11।।
verses.translation: That which the knowers of the Vedas call the Imperishable, that which the self-controlled ascetics, free from passion, enter; desiring which they practice celibacy — that goal I shall briefly declare.

verses.sanskrit:
सर्वद्वाराणि संयम्य मनो हृदि निरुध्य च।

मूर्ध्न्याधायात्मनः प्राणमास्थितो योगधारणाम्।।8.12।।
verses.translation: Closing all the gates of the body, confining the mind in the heart, fixing the life-breath in the head, established in yogic concentration,

verses.sanskrit:
ओमित्येकाक्षरं ब्रह्म व्याहरन्मामनुस्मरन्।

यः प्रयाति त्यजन्देहं स याति परमां गतिम्।।8.13।।
verses.translation: uttering the one-syllabled Om, which is Brahman, remembering Me, he who departs, giving up the body, attains the supreme goal.

verses.sanskrit:
अनन्यचेताः सततं यो मां स्मरति नित्यशः।

तस्याहं सुलभः पार्थ नित्ययुक्तस्य योगिनः।।8.14।।
verses.translation: He who always remembers Me without thinking of anything else — for that ever-yoked yogi, I am easy to reach, O Partha.

verses.sanskrit:
मामुपेत्य पुनर्जन्म दुःखालयमशाश्वतम्।

नाप्नुवन्ति महात्मानः संसिद्धिं परमां गताः।।8.15।।
verses.translation: The great souls who have come to Me, having attained the highest perfection, are not reborn in this impermanent abode of suffering.

verses.sanskrit:
आब्रह्मभुवनाल्लोकाः पुनरावर्तिनोऽर्जुन।

मामुपेत्य तु कौन्तेय पुनर्जन्म न विद्यते।।8.16।।
verses.translation: From the world of Brahma (the creator) downwards, all worlds are subject to return, O Arjuna. But on attaining Me, O son of Kunti, there is no rebirth.

verses.sanskrit:
सहस्रयुगपर्यन्तमहर्यद्ब्रह्मणो विदुः।

रात्रिं युगसहस्रान्तां तेऽहोरात्रविदो जनाः।।8.17।।
verses.translation: Those who know that the day of Brahma lasts a thousand yugas, and that his night also extends to a thousand yugas — they are the knowers of day and night.

verses.sanskrit:
अव्यक्ताद्व्यक्तयः सर्वाः प्रभवन्त्यहरागमे।

रात्र्यागमे प्रलीयन्ते तत्रैवाव्यक्तसंज्ञके।।8.18।।
verses.translation: At the coming of day, all manifestations issue forth from the unmanifest; at the coming of night, they dissolve into that very same unmanifest.

verses.sanskrit:
भूतग्रामः स एवायं भूत्वा भूत्वा प्रलीयते।

रात्र्यागमेऽवशः पार्थ प्रभवत्यहरागमे।।8.19।।
verses.translation: This same multitude of beings, coming forth again and again, helplessly dissolves at the coming of night, O Partha, and comes forth at the coming of day.

verses.sanskrit:
परस्तस्मात्तु भावोऽन्योऽव्यक्तोऽव्यक्तात्सनातनः।

यः स सर्वेषु भूतेषु नश्यत्सु न विनश्यति।।8.20।।
verses.translation: But beyond this unmanifest is another unmanifest, eternal Being, which does not perish when all beings perish.

verses.sanskrit:
अव्यक्तोऽक्षर इत्युक्तस्तमाहुः परमां गतिम्।

यं प्राप्य न निवर्तन्ते तद्धाम परमं मम।।8.21।।
verses.translation: That unmanifest, called the Imperishable, is declared to be the supreme goal. Attaining which they do not return — that is My supreme abode.

verses.sanskrit:
पुरुषः स परः पार्थ भक्त्या लभ्यस्त्वनन्यया।

यस्यान्तःस्थानि भूतानि येन सर्वमिदं ततम्।।8.22।।
verses.translation: O Partha, that Supreme Purusha, in whom all beings abide and by whom all this is pervaded, is attainable by undivided devotion alone.

verses.sanskrit:
यत्र काले त्वनावृत्तिमावृत्तिं चैव योगिनः।

प्रयाता यान्ति तं कालं वक्ष्यामि भरतर्षभ।।8.23।।
verses.translation: Now I shall declare the times at which yogis depart, O bull of the Bharatas, to return or not to return.

verses.sanskrit:
अग्निर्ज्योतिरहः शुक्लः षण्मासा उत्तरायणम्।

तत्र प्रयाता गच्छन्ति ब्रह्म ब्रह्मविदो जनाः।।8.24।।
verses.translation: Fire, light, the day, the bright fortnight, the six months of the northern course — departing then, the knowers of Brahman go to Brahman.

verses.sanskrit:
धूमो रात्रिस्तथा कृष्णः षण्मासा दक्षिणायनम्।

तत्र चान्द्रमसं ज्योतिर्योगी प्राप्य निवर्तते।।8.25।।
verses.translation: Smoke, night, the dark fortnight, the six months of the southern course — the yogi attaining by these the moon's light returns.

verses.sanskrit:
शुक्लकृष्णे गती ह्येते जगतः शाश्वते मते।

एकया यात्यनावृत्तिमन्ययाऽऽवर्तते पुनः।।8.26।।
verses.translation: These two paths of the world — the bright and the dark — are considered eternal. By one, one goes to non-return; by the other, one returns again.

verses.sanskrit:
नैते सृती पार्थ जानन्योगी मुह्यति कश्चन।

तस्मात्सर्वेषु कालेषु योगयुक्तो भवार्जुन।।8.27।।
verses.translation: Knowing these two paths, O Partha, no yogi is deluded. Therefore, at all times, be yoked in yoga, O Arjuna.

verses.sanskrit:
वेदेषु यज्ञेषु तपःसु चैव

दानेषु यत्पुण्यफलं प्रदिष्टम्।

अत्येति तत्सर्वमिदं विदित्वा

योगी परं स्थानमुपैति चाद्यम्।।8.28।।
verses.translation: Whatever fruit of merit is declared for the study of the Vedas, for sacrifices, austerities, and gifts — the yogi, knowing this, transcends all these and attains the supreme, primal abode.

chapters.chapter_title_english: The Yoga of Royal Knowledge and Royal Secret

verses.sanskrit:
श्री भगवानुवाच

इदं तु ते गुह्यतमं प्रवक्ष्याम्यनसूयवे।

ज्ञानं विज्ञानसहितं यज्ज्ञात्वा मोक्ष्यसेऽशुभात्।।9.1।।
verses.translation:
The Blessed Lord said:
To you, who do not cavil, I shall declare this most profound secret — knowledge combined with realization — knowing which you shall be freed from evil.

verses.sanskrit:
राजविद्या राजगुह्यं पवित्रमिदमुत्तमम्।

प्रत्यक्षावगमं धर्म्यं सुसुखं कर्तुमव्ययम्।।9.2।।
verses.translation: This is the king of sciences, the king of secrets, the supreme purifier; it is realizable by direct experience, accords with dharma, easy to practice, imperishable.

verses.sanskrit:
अश्रद्दधानाः पुरुषा धर्मस्यास्य परन्तप।

अप्राप्य मां निवर्तन्ते मृत्युसंसारवर्त्मनि।।9.3।।
verses.translation: Men who have no faith in this dharma, O scorcher of foes, fail to reach Me, and return to the path of the cycle of death.

verses.sanskrit:
मया ततमिदं सर्वं जगदव्यक्तमूर्तिना।

मत्स्थानि सर्वभूतानि न चाहं तेष्ववस्थितः।।9.4।।
verses.translation: By Me, in My unmanifest form, all this world is pervaded. All beings abide in Me, but I do not abide in them.

verses.sanskrit:
न च मत्स्थानि भूतानि पश्य मे योगमैश्वरम्।

भूतभृन्न च भूतस्थो ममात्मा भूतभावनः।।9.5।।
verses.translation: And yet beings do not abide in Me — behold My divine yoga! Sustaining beings and not abiding in them, My Self is the cause of beings.

verses.sanskrit:
यथाऽऽकाशस्थितो नित्यं वायुः सर्वत्रगो महान्।

तथा सर्वाणि भूतानि मत्स्थानीत्युपधारय।।9.6।।
verses.translation: As the mighty wind, moving everywhere, ever abides in the ether, so all beings abide in Me — know this.

verses.sanskrit:
सर्वभूतानि कौन्तेय प्रकृतिं यान्ति मामिकाम्।

कल्पक्षये पुनस्तानि कल्पादौ विसृजाम्यहम्।।9.7।।
verses.translation: All beings, O son of Kunti, enter into My prakriti at the end of a kalpa; at the beginning of the next kalpa, I send them forth again.

verses.sanskrit:
प्रकृतिं स्वामवष्टभ्य विसृजामि पुनः पुनः।

भूतग्राममिमं कृत्स्नमवशं प्रकृतेर्वशात्।।9.8।।
verses.translation: Taking hold of My own prakriti, I send forth again and again this entire multitude of beings, helpless under the sway of nature.

verses.sanskrit:
न च मां तानि कर्माणि निबध्नन्ति धनञ्जय।

उदासीनवदासीनमसक्तं तेषु कर्मसु।।9.9।।
verses.translation: But these actions do not bind Me, O Dhananjaya, seated as if indifferent, unattached to those actions.

verses.sanskrit:
मयाऽध्यक्षेण प्रकृतिः सूयते सचराचरम्।

हेतुनाऽनेन कौन्तेय जगद्विपरिवर्तते।।9.10।।
verses.translation: With Me as overseer, prakriti produces the moving and the unmoving. For this reason, O son of Kunti, the world revolves.

verses.sanskrit:
अवजानन्ति मां मूढा मानुषीं तनुमाश्रितम्।

परं भावमजानन्तो मम भूतमहेश्वरम्।।9.11।।
verses.translation: Fools disregard Me when I have taken a human body, not knowing My higher state as the great Lord of beings.

verses.sanskrit:
मोघाशा मोघकर्माणो मोघज्ञाना विचेतसः।

राक्षसीमासुरीं चैव प्रकृतिं मोहिनीं श्रिताः।।9.12।।
verses.translation: Of futile hopes, futile actions, futile knowledge, without discernment, they are filled with the deluding nature of rakshasas and asuras.

verses.sanskrit:
महात्मानस्तु मां पार्थ दैवीं प्रकृतिमाश्रिताः।

भजन्त्यनन्यमनसो ज्ञात्वा भूतादिमव्ययम्।।9.13।।
verses.translation: But the great souls, O Partha, partaking of the divine nature, worship Me with undistracted mind, knowing Me as the imperishable source of beings.

verses.sanskrit:
सततं कीर्तयन्तो मां यतन्तश्च दृढव्रताः।

नमस्यन्तश्च मां भक्त्या नित्ययुक्ता उपासते।।9.14।।
verses.translation: Always glorifying Me, striving, firm in vows, bowing down to Me with devotion, ever-yoked, they worship Me.

verses.sanskrit:
ज्ञानयज्ञेन चाप्यन्ये यजन्तो मामुपासते।

एकत्वेन पृथक्त्वेन बहुधा विश्वतोमुखम्।।9.15।।
verses.translation: Others, sacrificing by the yajna of knowledge, worship Me as the One, as distinct, as in many forms, facing everywhere.

verses.sanskrit:
अहं क्रतुरहं यज्ञः स्वधाऽहमहमौषधम्।

मंत्रोऽहमहमेवाज्यमहमग्निरहं हुतम्।।9.16।।
verses.translation: I am the ritual, I am the sacrifice, I am the ancestral offering, I am the herb; I am the mantra, I am the clarified butter, I am the fire, I am the oblation.

verses.sanskrit:
पिताऽहमस्य जगतो माता धाता पितामहः।

वेद्यं पवित्रमोंकार ऋक् साम यजुरेव च।।9.17।।
verses.translation: I am the father of this world, the mother, the sustainer, the grandsire, the knowable, the purifier, the sacred syllable Om, and also the Rik, the Sama, and the Yajus.

verses.sanskrit:
गतिर्भर्ता प्रभुः साक्षी निवासः शरणं सुहृत्।

प्रभवः प्रलयः स्थानं निधानं बीजमव्ययम्।।9.18।।
verses.translation: I am the goal, the sustainer, the master, the witness, the abode, the shelter, the friend; the origin, the dissolution, the ground, the treasure-house, the imperishable seed.

verses.sanskrit:
तपाम्यहमहं वर्षं निगृह्णाम्युत्सृजामि च।

अमृतं चैव मृत्युश्च सदसच्चाहमर्जुन।।9.19।।
verses.translation: I give heat; I hold back and pour forth the rain. I am immortality as well as death; I am both being and non-being, O Arjuna.

verses.sanskrit:
त्रैविद्या मां सोमपाः पूतपापा

यज्ञैरिष्ट्वा स्वर्गतिं प्रार्थयन्ते।

ते पुण्यमासाद्य सुरेन्द्रलोक

मश्नन्ति दिव्यान्दिवि देवभोगान्।।9.20।।
verses.translation: The knowers of the three Vedas, the soma-drinkers, their sins cleansed, worshipping Me by sacrifices, pray for the way to heaven; reaching the pure world of the lord of gods, they taste in heaven the divine pleasures of the gods.

verses.sanskrit:
ते तं भुक्त्वा स्वर्गलोकं विशालं

क्षीणे पुण्ये मर्त्यलोकं विशन्ति।

एव त्रयीधर्ममनुप्रपन्ना

गतागतं कामकामा लभन्ते।।9.21।।
verses.translation: Having enjoyed that vast heavenly world, on the exhaustion of their merit, they enter the mortal world. Thus, following the dharma of the three Vedas, desiring enjoyments, they obtain only what goes and returns.

verses.sanskrit:
अनन्याश्चिन्तयन्तो मां ये जनाः पर्युपासते।

तेषां नित्याभियुक्तानां योगक्षेमं वहाम्यहम्।।9.22।।
verses.translation: For those who, thinking of no other, worship Me as ever-united, I provide what they lack and preserve what they have.

verses.sanskrit:
येऽप्यन्यदेवता भक्ता यजन्ते श्रद्धयाऽन्विताः।

तेऽपि मामेव कौन्तेय यजन्त्यविधिपूर्वकम्।।9.23।।
verses.translation: Even those devotees of other gods who worship them with faith — they too, O son of Kunti, worship Me alone, though not according to the rule.

verses.sanskrit:
अहं हि सर्वयज्ञानां भोक्ता च प्रभुरेव च।

न तु मामभिजानन्ति तत्त्वेनातश्च्यवन्ति ते।।9.24।।
verses.translation: For I alone am the enjoyer and Lord of all sacrifices. But they do not recognize Me in truth, and so they fall.

verses.sanskrit:
यान्ति देवव्रता देवान् पितृ़न्यान्ति पितृव्रताः।

भूतानि यान्ति भूतेज्या यान्ति मद्याजिनोऽपि माम्।।9.25।।
verses.translation: Worshippers of the gods go to the gods; to the ancestors go the ancestor-worshippers; to the bhutas go the worshippers of bhutas; and My worshippers come to Me.

verses.sanskrit:
पत्रं पुष्पं फलं तोयं यो मे भक्त्या प्रयच्छति।

तदहं भक्त्युपहृतमश्नामि प्रयतात्मनः।।9.26।।
verses.translation: Whoever offers Me with devotion a leaf, a flower, a fruit, water — that, offered with devotion by the pure-minded, I accept.

verses.sanskrit:
यत्करोषि यदश्नासि यज्जुहोषि ददासि यत्।

यत्तपस्यसि कौन्तेय तत्कुरुष्व मदर्पणम्।।9.27।।
verses.translation: Whatever you do, whatever you eat, whatever you offer in sacrifice, whatever you give, whatever austerity you practice — do that, O son of Kunti, as an offering to Me.

verses.sanskrit:
शुभाशुभफलैरेवं मोक्ष्यसे कर्मबन्धनैः।

संन्यासयोगयुक्तात्मा विमुक्तो मामुपैष्यसि।।9.28।।
verses.translation: Thus you shall be freed from the bonds of action, from its good and evil fruits. With your self yoked in the yoga of renunciation, liberated, you shall come to Me.

verses.sanskrit:
समोऽहं सर्वभूतेषु न मे द्वेष्योऽस्ति न प्रियः।

ये भजन्ति तु मां भक्त्या मयि ते तेषु चाप्यहम्।।9.29।।
verses.translation: I am the same in all beings; none is hateful nor dear to Me. But those who worship Me with devotion — they are in Me, and I am in them.

verses.sanskrit:
अपि चेत्सुदुराचारो भजते मामनन्यभाक्।

साधुरेव स मन्तव्यः सम्यग्व्यवसितो हि सः।।9.30।।
verses.translation: Even if the most evildoer worships Me with undivided devotion, he is to be regarded as righteous, for he has resolved rightly.

verses.sanskrit:
क्षिप्रं भवति धर्मात्मा शश्वच्छान्तिं निगच्छति।

कौन्तेय प्रतिजानीहि न मे भक्तः प्रणश्यति।।9.31।।
verses.translation: Quickly he becomes righteous in soul and attains lasting peace. O son of Kunti, know for certain that My devotee never perishes.

verses.sanskrit:
मां हि पार्थ व्यपाश्रित्य येऽपि स्युः पापयोनयः।

स्त्रियो वैश्यास्तथा शूद्रास्तेऽपि यान्ति परां गतिम्।।9.32।।
verses.translation: Even those who are of sinful birth, O Partha — women, vaishyas, and shudras — taking refuge in Me, attain the supreme goal.

verses.sanskrit:
किं पुनर्ब्राह्मणाः पुण्या भक्ता राजर्षयस्तथा।

अनित्यमसुखं लोकमिमं प्राप्य भजस्व माम्।।9.33।।
verses.translation: How much more, then, holy brahmanas and devoted royal sages! Having come to this impermanent and sorrowful world, worship Me.

verses.sanskrit:
मन्मना भव मद्भक्तो मद्याजी मां नमस्कुरु।

मामेवैष्यसि युक्त्वैवमात्मानं मत्परायणः।।9.34।।
verses.translation: Fix your mind on Me, be devoted to Me, sacrifice to Me, bow down to Me. Yoking yourself thus, with Me as your supreme goal, to Me you shall come.

chapters.chapter_title_english: The Yoga of Divine Manifestations (Vibhuti Yoga)

verses.sanskrit:
श्री भगवानुवाच

भूय एव महाबाहो श्रृणु मे परमं वचः।

यत्तेऽहं प्रीयमाणाय वक्ष्यामि हितकाम्यया।।10.1।।
verses.translation:
The Blessed Lord said:
Again, O mighty-armed one, hear My supreme word, which I shall speak to you, who are dear to Me, desiring your welfare.

verses.sanskrit:
न मे विदुः सुरगणाः प्रभवं न महर्षयः।

अहमादिर्हि देवानां महर्षीणां च सर्वशः।।10.2।।
verses.translation: Neither the hosts of gods nor the great sages know My origin; for I am the source, in every way, of the gods and the great rishis.

verses.sanskrit:
यो मामजमनादिं च वेत्ति लोकमहेश्वरम्।

असम्मूढः स मर्त्येषु सर्वपापैः प्रमुच्यते।।10.3।।
verses.translation: He who knows Me, unborn, without beginning, the great Lord of the worlds — he, undeluded among men, is freed from all sins.

verses.sanskrit:
बुद्धिर्ज्ञानमसंमोहः क्षमा सत्यं दमः शमः।

सुखं दुःखं भवोऽभावो भयं चाभयमेव च।।10.4।।
verses.translation: Discrimination, knowledge, freedom from delusion, patience, truthfulness, self-control, calmness, pleasure, pain, existence, non-existence, fear, and also fearlessness;

verses.sanskrit:
अहिंसा समता तुष्टिस्तपो दानं यशोऽयशः।

भवन्ति भावा भूतानां मत्त एव पृथग्विधाः।।10.5।।
verses.translation: non-injury, equanimity, contentment, austerity, charity, fame, infamy — these various dispositions of beings arise from Me alone.

verses.sanskrit:
महर्षयः सप्त पूर्वे चत्वारो मनवस्तथा।

मद्भावा मानसा जाता येषां लोक इमाः प्रजाः।।10.6।।
verses.translation: The seven great sages and the four ancient (Manus), partaking of My nature, were born of My mind. From them have come these creatures in the world.

verses.sanskrit:
एतां विभूतिं योगं च मम यो वेत्ति तत्त्वतः।

सोऽविकम्पेन योगेन युज्यते नात्र संशयः।।10.7।।
verses.translation: He who knows in truth this glory and yoga of Mine — he is yoked in unshakable yoga. Of this there is no doubt.

verses.sanskrit:
अहं सर्वस्य प्रभवो मत्तः सर्वं प्रवर्तते।

इति मत्वा भजन्ते मां बुधा भावसमन्विताः।।10.8।।
verses.translation: I am the source of all; from Me everything proceeds. Knowing this, the wise, full of devotion, worship Me.

verses.sanskrit:
मच्चित्ता मद्गतप्राणा बोधयन्तः परस्परम्।

कथयन्तश्च मां नित्यं तुष्यन्ति च रमन्ति च।।10.9।।
verses.translation: With their thoughts fixed on Me, with their life surrendered to Me, enlightening one another, ever speaking of Me, they are satisfied and rejoice.

verses.sanskrit:
तेषां सततयुक्तानां भजतां प्रीतिपूर्वकम्।

ददामि बुद्धियोगं तं येन मामुपयान्ति ते।।10.10।।
verses.translation: To them, ever yoked, worshipping Me with love, I give the yoga of discrimination by which they come to Me.

verses.sanskrit:
तेषामेवानुकम्पार्थमहमज्ञानजं तमः।

नाशयाम्यात्मभावस्थो ज्ञानदीपेन भास्वता।।10.11।।
verses.translation: Out of compassion for them, I, dwelling in their own self, destroy the darkness born of ignorance with the shining lamp of knowledge.

verses.sanskrit:
अर्जुन उवाच

परं ब्रह्म परं धाम पवित्रं परमं भवान्।

पुरुषं शाश्वतं दिव्यमादिदेवमजं विभुम्।।10.12।।
verses.translation:
Arjuna said:
You are the supreme Brahman, the supreme abode, the supreme purifier — the eternal divine Person, the primal God, unborn, all-pervading.

verses.sanskrit:
आहुस्त्वामृषयः सर्वे देवर्षिर्नारदस्तथा।

असितो देवलो व्यासः स्वयं चैव ब्रवीषि मे।।10.13।।
verses.translation: All the sages say this of You, and so too the divine sage Narada, Asita, Devala, and Vyasa; and You Yourself tell me so.

verses.sanskrit:
सर्वमेतदृतं मन्ये यन्मां वदसि केशव।

न हि ते भगवन् व्यक्ितं विदुर्देवा न दानवाः।।10.14।।
verses.translation: I hold all this that You have told me to be true, O Keshava. Neither the gods nor the demons, O Lord, know Your manifestation.

verses.sanskrit:
स्वयमेवात्मनाऽत्मानं वेत्थ त्वं पुरुषोत्तम।

भूतभावन भूतेश देवदेव जगत्पते।।10.15।।
verses.translation: You alone know Yourself by Yourself, O supreme Person, source of beings, Lord of beings, God of gods, Lord of the world.

verses.sanskrit:
वक्तुमर्हस्यशेषेण दिव्या ह्यात्मविभूतयः।

याभिर्विभूतिभिर्लोकानिमांस्त्वं व्याप्य तिष्ठसि।।10.16।।
verses.translation: Please declare in full Your divine vibhutis, by which vibhutis You pervade these worlds and abide.

verses.sanskrit:
कथं विद्यामहं योगिंस्त्वां सदा परिचिन्तयन्।

केषु केषु च भावेषु चिन्त्योऽसि भगवन्मया।।10.17।।
verses.translation: How shall I know You, O Yogin, by constant meditation? In what various aspects are You, O Lord, to be meditated upon by me?

verses.sanskrit:
विस्तरेणात्मनो योगं विभूतिं च जनार्दन।

भूयः कथय तृप्तिर्हि श्रृण्वतो नास्ति मेऽमृतम्।।10.18।।
verses.translation: O Janardana, tell me again in full of Your yoga and Your vibhutis; for I am never satiated hearing Your nectar-like words.

verses.sanskrit:
श्री भगवानुवाच

हन्त ते कथयिष्यामि दिव्या ह्यात्मविभूतयः।

प्राधान्यतः कुरुश्रेष्ठ नास्त्यन्तो विस्तरस्य मे।।10.19।।
verses.translation:
The Blessed Lord said:
Well, I shall declare to you My divine vibhutis — only the principal ones, O best of the Kurus; for there is no end to the detail of My manifestation.

verses.sanskrit:
अहमात्मा गुडाकेश सर्वभूताशयस्थितः।

अहमादिश्च मध्यं च भूतानामन्त एव च।।10.20।।
verses.translation: I am the Self, O Gudakesha, seated in the hearts of all beings. I am the beginning, the middle, and also the end of all beings.

verses.sanskrit:
आदित्यानामहं विष्णुर्ज्योतिषां रविरंशुमान्।

मरीचिर्मरुतामस्मि नक्षत्राणामहं शशी।।10.21।।
verses.translation: Of the Adityas I am Vishnu; of lights, the radiant sun; of the Maruts I am Marichi; of stars (nakshatras), I am the moon.

verses.sanskrit:
वेदानां सामवेदोऽस्मि देवानामस्मि वासवः।

इन्द्रियाणां मनश्चास्मि भूतानामस्मि चेतना।।10.22।।
verses.translation: Of the Vedas I am the Sama; of the gods, I am Indra; of the senses I am the mind; in living beings I am consciousness.

verses.sanskrit:
रुद्राणां शङ्करश्चास्मि वित्तेशो यक्षरक्षसाम्।

वसूनां पावकश्चास्मि मेरुः शिखरिणामहम्।।10.23।।
verses.translation: Of the Rudras I am Shankara; of yakshas and rakshasas, I am Kubera; of the Vasus I am Agni; of mountains, I am Meru.

verses.sanskrit:
पुरोधसां च मुख्यं मां विद्धि पार्थ बृहस्पतिम्।

सेनानीनामहं स्कन्दः सरसामस्मि सागरः।।10.24।।
verses.translation: Know Me, O Partha, the chief of household priests, as Brihaspati. Of generals I am Skanda; of bodies of water, I am the ocean.

verses.sanskrit:
महर्षीणां भृगुरहं गिरामस्म्येकमक्षरम्।

यज्ञानां जपयज्ञोऽस्मि स्थावराणां हिमालयः।।10.25।।
verses.translation: Of the great rishis I am Bhrigu; of utterances, I am the single syllable Om. Of sacrifices I am the japa (silent repetition); of the immovable, the Himalayas.

verses.sanskrit:
अश्वत्थः सर्ववृक्षाणां देवर्षीणां च नारदः।

गन्धर्वाणां चित्ररथः सिद्धानां कपिलो मुनिः।।10.26।।
verses.translation: Of all trees I am the Ashvattha; of the divine rishis, Narada; of the gandharvas, Chitraratha; of the perfected, the sage Kapila.

verses.sanskrit:
उच्चैःश्रवसमश्वानां विद्धि माममृतोद्भवम्।

ऐरावतं गजेन्द्राणां नराणां च नराधिपम्।।10.27।।
verses.translation: Of horses know Me to be Ucchaisravas, born from the churning for nectar; of lordly elephants, Airavata; of men, the king.

verses.sanskrit:
आयुधानामहं वज्रं धेनूनामस्मि कामधुक्।

प्रजनश्चास्मि कन्दर्पः सर्पाणामस्मि वासुकिः।।10.28।।
verses.translation: Of weapons I am the thunderbolt; of cows, Kamadhenu; of begetters I am Kandarpa (the god of love); of serpents, I am Vasuki.

verses.sanskrit:
अनन्तश्चास्मि नागानां वरुणो यादसामहम्।

पितृ़णामर्यमा चास्मि यमः संयमतामहम्।।10.29।।
verses.translation: Of nagas I am Ananta; of water-beings, Varuna; of the ancestors, Aryaman; among rulers, I am Yama.

verses.sanskrit:
प्रह्लादश्चास्मि दैत्यानां कालः कलयतामहम्।

मृगाणां च मृगेन्द्रोऽहं वैनतेयश्च पक्षिणाम्।।10.30।।
verses.translation: Of the daityas I am Prahlada; of reckoners, Time; of beasts, the king of beasts (lion); of birds, the son of Vinata (Garuda).

verses.sanskrit:
पवनः पवतामस्मि रामः शस्त्रभृतामहम्।

झषाणां मकरश्चास्मि स्रोतसामस्मि जाह्नवी।।10.31।।
verses.translation: Of purifiers I am the wind; of warriors, Rama; of fishes I am the makara; of rivers, I am the Ganga.

verses.sanskrit:
सर्गाणामादिरन्तश्च मध्यं चैवाहमर्जुन।

अध्यात्मविद्या विद्यानां वादः प्रवदतामहम्।।10.32।।
verses.translation: Of creations I am the beginning, the end, and also the middle, O Arjuna. Of sciences I am the knowledge of the Self; in disputants, I am the debate.

verses.sanskrit:
अक्षराणामकारोऽस्मि द्वन्द्वः सामासिकस्य च।

अहमेवाक्षयः कालो धाताऽहं विश्वतोमुखः।।10.33।।
verses.translation: Of letters I am the letter 'A'; of compounds, I am the dvandva (pair). I alone am imperishable Time; I am the dispenser facing all directions.

verses.sanskrit:
मृत्युः सर्वहरश्चाहमुद्भवश्च भविष्यताम्।

कीर्तिः श्रीर्वाक्च नारीणां स्मृतिर्मेधा धृतिः क्षमा।।10.34।।
verses.translation: I am all-devouring Death, and the origin of what is yet to be; of feminine qualities: fame, fortune, speech, memory, intelligence, constancy, forbearance.

verses.sanskrit:
बृहत्साम तथा साम्नां गायत्री छन्दसामहम्।

मासानां मार्गशीर्षोऽहमृतूनां कुसुमाकरः।।10.35।।
verses.translation: Of hymns likewise I am the Brihat-saman; of meters, I am Gayatri; of months I am Margashirsha; of seasons, the flowering spring.

verses.sanskrit:
द्यूतं छलयतामस्मि तेजस्तेजस्विनामहम्।

जयोऽस्मि व्यवसायोऽस्मि सत्त्वं सत्त्ववतामहम्।।10.36।।
verses.translation: I am the gambling of the cheats, the splendor of the splendid; I am victory, I am determination, I am the goodness of the good.

verses.sanskrit:
वृष्णीनां वासुदेवोऽस्मि पाण्डवानां धनंजयः।

मुनीनामप्यहं व्यासः कवीनामुशना कविः।।10.37।।
verses.translation: Of the Vrishnis I am Vasudeva; of the Pandavas, Dhananjaya; of sages I am Vyasa; among poets, Ushanas the poet.

verses.sanskrit:
दण्डो दमयतामस्मि नीतिरस्मि जिगीषताम्।

मौनं चैवास्मि गुह्यानां ज्ञानं ज्ञानवतामहम्।।10.38।।
verses.translation: Of those who chastise, I am the rod; of those who seek victory, I am statecraft. Of secrets I am silence; I am the knowledge of the knowers.

verses.sanskrit:
यच्चापि सर्वभूतानां बीजं तदहमर्जुन।

न तदस्ति विना यत्स्यान्मया भूतं चराचरम्।।10.39।।
verses.translation: Whatever is the seed of all beings, that am I, O Arjuna. There is no being, moving or unmoving, that could exist without Me.

verses.sanskrit:
नान्तोऽस्ति मम दिव्यानां विभूतीनां परंतप।

एष तूद्देशतः प्रोक्तो विभूतेर्विस्तरो मया।।10.40।।
verses.translation: There is no end to My divine vibhutis, O scorcher of foes. This is only a brief statement by Me, an instance of the expanse of My glory.

verses.sanskrit:
यद्यद्विभूतिमत्सत्त्वं श्रीमदूर्जितमेव वा।

तत्तदेवावगच्छ त्वं मम तेजोंऽशसंभवम्।।10.41।।
verses.translation: Whatever being there is that is glorious, prosperous, or mighty — know that every such one has sprung from a fragment of My splendor.

verses.sanskrit:
अथवा बहुनैतेन किं ज्ञातेन तवार्जुन।

विष्टभ्याहमिदं कृत्स्नमेकांशेन स्थितो जगत्।।10.42।।
verses.translation: But what use is it for you to know all this in detail, O Arjuna? I stand supporting this entire universe with a single fragment of Myself.

chapters.chapter_title_english: The Yoga of the Vision of the Universal Form

verses.sanskrit:
अर्जुन उवाच

मदनुग्रहाय परमं गुह्यमध्यात्मसंज्ञितम्।

यत्त्वयोक्तं वचस्तेन मोहोऽयं विगतो मम।।11.1।।
verses.translation:
Arjuna said:
As a favor to me, You have spoken of the supreme secret known as the Self; by those words of Yours, my delusion is dispelled.

verses.sanskrit:
भवाप्ययौ हि भूतानां श्रुतौ विस्तरशो मया।

त्वत्तः कमलपत्राक्ष माहात्म्यमपि चाव्ययम्।।11.2।।
verses.translation: O lotus-eyed one, I have heard from You in detail of the origin and dissolution of beings, and also of Your imperishable majesty.

verses.sanskrit:
एवमेतद्यथात्थ त्वमात्मानं परमेश्वर।

द्रष्टुमिच्छामि ते रूपमैश्वरं पुरुषोत्तम।।11.3।।
verses.translation: So it is, O supreme Lord, just as You have declared Yourself. I desire to see Your Ishvara form, O supreme Person.

verses.sanskrit:
मन्यसे यदि तच्छक्यं मया द्रष्टुमिति प्रभो।

योगेश्वर ततो मे त्वं दर्शयाऽत्मानमव्ययम्।।11.4।।
verses.translation: If You think, O Lord, that it can be seen by me, then, O Lord of yoga, show me Your imperishable Self.

verses.sanskrit:
श्री भगवानुवाच

पश्य मे पार्थ रूपाणि शतशोऽथ सहस्रशः।

नानाविधानि दिव्यानि नानावर्णाकृतीनि च।।11.5।।
verses.translation:
The Blessed Lord said:
Behold, O Partha, My forms, by hundreds and thousands, of various kinds, divine, of many colors and shapes.

verses.sanskrit:
पश्यादित्यान्वसून्रुद्रानश्िवनौ मरुतस्तथा।

बहून्यदृष्टपूर्वाणि पश्याऽश्चर्याणि भारत।।11.6।।
verses.translation: Behold the Adityas, the Vasus, the Rudras, the two Ashvins, and the Maruts. Behold, O Bharata, many wonders never seen before.

verses.sanskrit:
इहैकस्थं जगत्कृत्स्नं पश्याद्य सचराचरम्।

मम देहे गुडाकेश यच्चान्यद्द्रष्टुमिच्छसि।।11.7।।
verses.translation: Here today behold the whole universe, moving and unmoving, united in My body, O Gudakesha, and whatever else you wish to see.

verses.sanskrit:
न तु मां शक्यसे द्रष्टुमनेनैव स्वचक्षुषा।

दिव्यं ददामि ते चक्षुः पश्य मे योगमैश्वरम्।।11.8।।
verses.translation: But you cannot see Me with this, your own eye. I give you a divine eye — behold My sovereign yoga!

verses.sanskrit:
सञ्जय उवाच

एवमुक्त्वा ततो राजन्महायोगेश्वरो हरिः।

दर्शयामास पार्थाय परमं रूपमैश्वरम्।।11.9।।
verses.translation:
Sanjaya said:
Having spoken thus, O king, the great Lord of yoga, Hari, revealed to Partha His supreme Ishvara form:

verses.sanskrit:
अनेकवक्त्रनयनमनेकाद्भुतदर्शनम्।

अनेकदिव्याभरणं दिव्यानेकोद्यतायुधम्।।11.10।।
verses.translation: with many mouths and eyes, with many wondrous sights, with many divine ornaments, with many divine weapons uplifted;

verses.sanskrit:
दिव्यमाल्याम्बरधरं दिव्यगन्धानुलेपनम्।

सर्वाश्चर्यमयं देवमनन्तं विश्वतोमुखम्।।11.11।।
verses.translation: wearing divine garlands and robes, anointed with divine perfumes, all-wonderful, effulgent, infinite, with faces on every side.

verses.sanskrit:
दिवि सूर्यसहस्रस्य भवेद्युगपदुत्थिता।

यदि भाः सदृशी सा स्याद्भासस्तस्य महात्मनः।।11.12।।
verses.translation: If the splendor of a thousand suns were to blaze forth all at once in the sky, it might resemble the splendor of that Great Soul.

verses.sanskrit:
तत्रैकस्थं जगत्कृत्स्नं प्रविभक्तमनेकधा।

अपश्यद्देवदेवस्य शरीरे पाण्डवस्तदा।।11.13।।
verses.translation: There Pandava saw the entire universe, in its manifold divisions, gathered together in one, in the body of that God of gods.

verses.sanskrit:
ततः स विस्मयाविष्टो हृष्टरोमा धनञ्जयः।

प्रणम्य शिरसा देवं कृताञ्जलिरभाषत।।11.14।।
verses.translation: Then, filled with astonishment, his hair standing on end, Dhananjaya, bowing down his head before the Lord, with folded hands, said:

verses.sanskrit:
अर्जुन उवाच

पश्यामि देवांस्तव देव देहे

सर्वांस्तथा भूतविशेषसङ्घान्।

ब्रह्माणमीशं कमलासनस्थ

मृषींश्च सर्वानुरगांश्च दिव्यान्।।11.15।।
verses.translation:
Arjuna said:
O God, I see all the gods in Your body, and all the hosts of beings — Brahma the Lord seated on the lotus-seat, all the rishis, and the divine serpents.

verses.sanskrit:
अनेकबाहूदरवक्त्रनेत्रं

पश्यामि त्वां सर्वतोऽनन्तरूपम्।

नान्तं न मध्यं न पुनस्तवादिं

पश्यामि विश्वेश्वर विश्वरूप।।11.16।।
verses.translation: With many arms, bellies, mouths, and eyes, I behold You, of infinite form on every side. I see neither Your end, nor middle, nor beginning, O Lord of the universe, O Form universal.

verses.sanskrit:
किरीटिनं गदिनं चक्रिणं च

तेजोराशिं सर्वतोदीप्तिमन्तम्।

पश्यामि त्वां दुर्निरीक्ष्यं समन्ता

द्दीप्तानलार्कद्युतिमप्रमेयम्।।11.17।।
verses.translation: I see You with crown, club, and discus — a mass of splendor blazing on all sides, hard to see on every side, with the brilliance of blazing fire and sun, immeasurable.

verses.sanskrit:
त्वमक्षरं परमं वेदितव्यं

त्वमस्य विश्वस्य परं निधानम्।

त्वमव्ययः शाश्वतधर्मगोप्ता

सनातनस्त्वं पुरुषो मतो मे।।11.18।।
verses.translation: You are the Imperishable, the supreme to be known; You are the final resting-place of this universe. You are the imperishable guardian of the eternal dharma; You, I hold, are the primal Person.

verses.sanskrit:
अनादिमध्यान्तमनन्तवीर्य

मनन्तबाहुं शशिसूर्यनेत्रम्।

पश्यामि त्वां दीप्तहुताशवक्त्रम्

स्वतेजसा विश्वमिदं तपन्तम्।।11.19।।
verses.translation: Without beginning, middle, or end, of infinite power, of numberless arms, with the sun and moon for eyes, with blazing fire for mouth, scorching this universe with Your radiance — I see You.

verses.sanskrit:
द्यावापृथिव्योरिदमन्तरं हि

व्याप्तं त्वयैकेन दिशश्च सर्वाः।

दृष्ट्वाऽद्भुतं रूपमुग्रं तवेदं

लोकत्रयं प्रव्यथितं महात्मन्।।11.20।।
verses.translation: This space between heaven and earth and all the directions are pervaded by You alone. Seeing this wondrous, terrible form of Yours, the three worlds tremble, O Great Soul.

verses.sanskrit:
अमी हि त्वां सुरसङ्घाः विशन्ति

केचिद्भीताः प्राञ्जलयो गृणन्ति।

स्वस्तीत्युक्त्वा महर्षिसिद्धसङ्घाः

स्तुवन्ति त्वां स्तुतिभिः पुष्कलाभिः।।11.21।।
verses.translation: Into You enter the hosts of gods. Some, in fear, with folded hands, praise You. The great rishis and the perfected, crying 'May it be well!', glorify You with abundant hymns.

verses.sanskrit:
रुद्रादित्या वसवो ये च साध्या

विश्वेऽश्िवनौ मरुतश्चोष्मपाश्च।

गन्धर्वयक्षासुरसिद्धसङ्घा

वीक्षन्ते त्वां विस्मिताश्चैव सर्वे।।11.22।।
verses.translation: The Rudras, Adityas, Vasus, and Sadhyas, the Vishvedevas, the two Ashvins, the Maruts, the ancestors, the hosts of gandharvas, yakshas, asuras, and siddhas — they all gaze upon You in wonder.

verses.sanskrit:
रूपं महत्ते बहुवक्त्रनेत्रं

महाबाहो बहुबाहूरुपादम्।

बहूदरं बहुदंष्ट्राकरालं

दृष्ट्वा लोकाः प्रव्यथितास्तथाऽहम्।।11.23।।
verses.translation: Seeing Your great form, of many mouths and eyes, O mighty-armed one, of many arms, thighs, and feet, of many bellies, of many fearsome tusks — the worlds tremble, and so do I.

verses.sanskrit:
नभःस्पृशं दीप्तमनेकवर्णं

व्यात्ताननं दीप्तविशालनेत्रम्।

दृष्ट्वा हि त्वां प्रव्यथितान्तरात्मा

धृतिं न विन्दामि शमं च विष्णो।।11.24।।
verses.translation: Seeing You touching the sky, blazing with many colors, with gaping mouths and great, flaming eyes, my inmost soul trembles; I find neither firmness nor peace, O Vishnu.

verses.sanskrit:
दंष्ट्राकरालानि च ते मुखानि

दृष्ट्वैव कालानलसन्निभानि।

दिशो न जाने न लभे च शर्म

प्रसीद देवेश जगन्निवास।।11.25।।
verses.translation: Seeing Your mouths terrible with tusks, like the fires of cosmic dissolution, I know not the directions, nor do I find any refuge. Be gracious, O Lord of gods, O Abode of the worlds.

verses.sanskrit:
अमी च त्वां धृतराष्ट्रस्य पुत्राः

सर्वे सहैवावनिपालसङ्घैः।

भीष्मो द्रोणः सूतपुत्रस्तथाऽसौ

सहास्मदीयैरपि योधमुख्यैः।।11.26।।
verses.translation: Into You rush all the sons of Dhritarashtra, together with the hosts of kings; Bhishma, Drona, and the charioteer's son (Karna) as well, and also our chief warriors,

verses.sanskrit:
वक्त्राणि ते त्वरमाणा विशन्ति

दंष्ट्राकरालानि भयानकानि।

केचिद्विलग्ना दशनान्तरेषु

संदृश्यन्ते चूर्णितैरुत्तमाङ्गैः।।11.27।।
verses.translation: rush hastening into Your mouths, fearsome with tusks, terrifying to see. Some, clinging in the gaps between the tusks, are seen with their heads crushed to powder.

verses.sanskrit:
यथा नदीनां बहवोऽम्बुवेगाः

समुद्रमेवाभिमुखाः द्रवन्ति।

तथा तवामी नरलोकवीरा

विशन्ति वक्त्राण्यभिविज्वलन्ति।।11.28।।
verses.translation: As the many torrents of rivers rush toward the ocean alone, so these heroes of the world of men enter Your flaming mouths.

verses.sanskrit:
यथा प्रदीप्तं ज्वलनं पतङ्गा

विशन्ति नाशाय समृद्धवेगाः।

तथैव नाशाय विशन्ति लोका

स्तवापि वक्त्राणि समृद्धवेगाः।।11.29।।
verses.translation: As moths, with quickened speed, enter a blazing fire for their destruction, so do these creatures enter Your mouths with quickened speed for their destruction.

verses.sanskrit:
लेलिह्यसे ग्रसमानः समन्ता

ल्लोकान्समग्रान्वदनैर्ज्वलद्भिः।

तेजोभिरापूर्य जगत्समग्रं

भासस्तवोग्राः प्रतपन्ति विष्णो।।11.30।।
verses.translation: Swallowing all the worlds on every side with Your flaming mouths, You lick them up. Filling the whole universe with Your radiance, Your fierce rays burn, O Vishnu.

verses.sanskrit:
आख्याहि मे को भवानुग्ररूपो

नमोऽस्तु ते देववर प्रसीद।

विज्ञातुमिच्छामि भवन्तमाद्यं

न हि प्रजानामि तव प्रवृत्तिम्।।11.31।।
verses.translation: Tell me who You are, O fearsome-formed one — I bow to You, O best of gods; be gracious. I wish to know You, the primal one, for I understand not Your purpose.

verses.sanskrit:
श्री भगवानुवाच

कालोऽस्मि लोकक्षयकृत्प्रवृद्धो

लोकान्समाहर्तुमिह प्रवृत्तः।

ऋतेऽपि त्वां न भविष्यन्ति सर्वे

येऽवस्थिताः प्रत्यनीकेषु योधाः।।11.32।।
verses.translation:
The Blessed Lord said:
I am Time, mighty destroyer of the worlds, set to destroy the worlds. Even without you, none of these warriors arrayed in the opposing armies shall live.

verses.sanskrit:
तस्मात्त्वमुत्तिष्ठ यशो लभस्व

जित्वा शत्रून् भुङ्क्ष्व राज्यं समृद्धम्।

मयैवैते निहताः पूर्वमेव

निमित्तमात्रं भव सव्यसाचिन्।।11.33।।
verses.translation: Therefore arise; win fame. Defeat your enemies and enjoy a prosperous kingdom. By Me these have already been slain; be you merely the outward cause, O left-handed archer.

verses.sanskrit:
द्रोणं च भीष्मं च जयद्रथं च

कर्णं तथाऽन्यानपि योधवीरान्।

मया हतांस्त्वं जहि मा व्यथिष्ठा

युध्यस्व जेतासि रणे सपत्नान्।।11.34।।
verses.translation: Drona, Bhishma, Jayadratha, Karna, and the other warrior-heroes — they are slain by Me. Do you slay them! Do not tremble. Fight! You shall conquer your enemies in battle.

verses.sanskrit:
सञ्जय उवाच

एतच्छ्रुत्वा वचनं केशवस्य

कृताञ्जलिर्वेपमानः किरीटी।

नमस्कृत्वा भूय एवाह कृष्णं

सगद्गदं भीतभीतः प्रणम्य।।11.35।।
verses.translation:
Sanjaya said:
Having heard this speech of Keshava, Kiriti (Arjuna), trembling, hands folded, bowing down, spoke again to Krishna, stammering, overcome with fear, prostrating himself:

verses.sanskrit:
अर्जुन उवाच

स्थाने हृषीकेश तव प्रकीर्त्या

जगत् प्रहृष्यत्यनुरज्यते च।

रक्षांसि भीतानि दिशो द्रवन्ति

सर्वे नमस्यन्ति च सिद्धसङ्घाः।।11.36।।
verses.translation:
Arjuna said:
Rightly, O Hrishikesha, the world delights and rejoices in Your praise; the rakshasas, afraid, flee in all directions; all the hosts of the perfected bow down to You.

verses.sanskrit:
कस्माच्च ते न नमेरन्महात्मन्

गरीयसे ब्रह्मणोऽप्यादिकर्त्रे।

अनन्त देवेश जगन्निवास

त्वमक्षरं सदसत्तत्परं यत्।।11.37।।
verses.translation: And why should they not bow down to You, O Great Soul — greater than Brahma, the first creator? O infinite Lord of gods, refuge of the worlds, You are the Imperishable, being and non-being and what is beyond.

verses.sanskrit:
त्वमादिदेवः पुरुषः पुराण

स्त्वमस्य विश्वस्य परं निधानम्।

वेत्तासि वेद्यं च परं च धाम

त्वया ततं विश्वमनन्तरूप।।11.38।।
verses.translation: You are the primal Deity, the ancient Person; You are the supreme resting-place of this universe. You are the knower and the to-be-known, the supreme goal. By You is this universe pervaded, O Form of infinite forms.

verses.sanskrit:
वायुर्यमोऽग्निर्वरुणः शशाङ्कः

प्रजापतिस्त्वं प्रपितामहश्च।

नमो नमस्तेऽस्तु सहस्रकृत्वः

पुनश्च भूयोऽपि नमो नमस्ते।।11.39।।
verses.translation: You are Vayu, Yama, Agni, Varuna, the Moon, Prajapati, the great grandsire. Salutation to You a thousand times; again and again, salutation, salutation to You!

verses.sanskrit:
नमः पुरस्तादथ पृष्ठतस्ते

नमोऽस्तु ते सर्वत एव सर्व।

अनन्तवीर्यामितविक्रमस्त्वं

सर्वं समाप्नोषि ततोऽसि सर्वः।।11.40।।
verses.translation: Salutation to You from the front and from behind; salutation to You on every side, O All; infinite in valor, of immeasurable might, You pervade all — therefore You are all.

verses.sanskrit:
सखेति मत्वा प्रसभं यदुक्तं

हे कृष्ण हे यादव हे सखेति।

अजानता महिमानं तवेदं

मया प्रमादात्प्रणयेन वापि।।11.41।।
verses.translation: Whatever I said rashly, whether in madness or in love, addressing You as 'O Krishna,' 'O Yadava,' 'O friend' — thinking You a friend, not knowing this Your greatness,

verses.sanskrit:
यच्चावहासार्थमसत्कृतोऽसि

विहारशय्यासनभोजनेषु।

एकोऽथवाप्यच्युत तत्समक्षं

तत्क्षामये त्वामहमप्रमेयम्।।11.42।।
verses.translation: and whatever disrespect I showed You in jest, while playing, resting, sitting, or at meals, alone or in the presence of others — O Unshaken One, I beg forgiveness from You, the immeasurable.

verses.sanskrit:
पितासि लोकस्य चराचरस्य

त्वमस्य पूज्यश्च गुरुर्गरीयान्।

न त्वत्समोऽस्त्यभ्यधिकः कुतोऽन्यो

लोकत्रयेऽप्यप्रतिमप्रभाव।।11.43।।
verses.translation: You are the father of the world, of the moving and unmoving; its object of worship, its most venerable teacher. There is none equal to You — how could there be one greater? — in the three worlds, O incomparable Power.

verses.sanskrit:
तस्मात्प्रणम्य प्रणिधाय कायं

प्रसादये त्वामहमीशमीड्यम्।

पितेव पुत्रस्य सखेव सख्युः

प्रियः प्रियायार्हसि देव सोढुम्।।11.44।।
verses.translation: Therefore, prostrating myself and bowing down my body, I beg Your grace, O adorable Lord. As father to son, as friend to friend, as beloved to beloved — please bear with me, O God.

verses.sanskrit:
अदृष्टपूर्वं हृषितोऽस्मि दृष्ट्वा

भयेन च प्रव्यथितं मनो मे।

तदेव मे दर्शय देव रूपं

प्रसीद देवेश जगन्निवास।।11.45।।
verses.translation: Seeing what was never seen before, I am filled with joy; yet my mind is shaken with fear. Show me, O God, that other form. Be gracious, O Lord of gods, O Abode of the worlds.

verses.sanskrit:
किरीटिनं गदिनं चक्रहस्त

मिच्छामि त्वां द्रष्टुमहं तथैव।

तेनैव रूपेण चतुर्भुजेन

सहस्रबाहो भव विश्वमूर्ते।।11.46।।
verses.translation: I wish to see You even as before, with crown, club, and discus in hand. Assume that four-armed form, O thousand-armed one, O Form universal.

verses.sanskrit:
श्री भगवानुवाच

मया प्रसन्नेन तवार्जुनेदं

रूपं परं दर्शितमात्मयोगात्।

तेजोमयं विश्वमनन्तमाद्यं

यन्मे त्वदन्येन न दृष्टपूर्वम्।।11.47।।
verses.translation:
The Blessed Lord said:
By My grace, O Arjuna, this supreme form has been shown to you, by My own yogic power — luminous, universal, infinite, primal — which none but you has ever seen.

verses.sanskrit:
न वेदयज्ञाध्ययनैर्न दानै

र्न च क्रियाभिर्न तपोभिरुग्रैः।

एवंरूपः शक्य अहं नृलोके

द्रष्टुं त्वदन्येन कुरुप्रवीर।।11.48।।
verses.translation: Not by study of the Vedas, nor by sacrifices, nor by gifts, nor by rituals, nor by severe austerities, can I be seen in such a form in the world of men by any other than you, O hero of the Kurus.

verses.sanskrit:
मा ते व्यथा मा च विमूढभावो

दृष्ट्वा रूपं घोरमीदृङ्ममेदम्।

व्यपेतभीः प्रीतमनाः पुनस्त्वं

तदेव मे रूपमिदं प्रपश्य।।11.49।।
verses.translation: Be not afraid, nor bewildered, on beholding this terrible form of Mine. Freed from fear, with gladdened heart, behold again this other form of Mine.

verses.sanskrit:
सञ्जय उवाच

इत्यर्जुनं वासुदेवस्तथोक्त्वा

स्वकं रूपं दर्शयामास भूयः।

आश्वासयामास च भीतमेनं

भूत्वा पुनः सौम्यवपुर्महात्मा।।11.50।।
verses.translation:
Sanjaya said:
Having spoken thus to Arjuna, Vasudeva showed him again His own form; and the Great Soul, assuming once more His gentle form, consoled the frightened one.

verses.sanskrit:
अर्जुन उवाच

दृष्ट्वेदं मानुषं रूपं तवसौम्यं जनार्दन।

इदानीमस्मि संवृत्तः सचेताः प्रकृतिं गतः।।11.51।।
verses.translation:
Arjuna said:
Seeing this Your gentle human form, O Janardana, my thoughts are now composed; I am restored to my own nature.

verses.sanskrit:
श्री भगवानुवाच

सुदुर्दर्शमिदं रूपं दृष्टवानसि यन्मम।

देवा अप्यस्य रूपस्य नित्यं दर्शनकाङ्क्षिणः।।11.52।।
verses.translation:
The Blessed Lord said:
This form of Mine that you have seen is very hard to behold. Even the gods are ever eager to behold this form.

verses.sanskrit:
नाहं वेदैर्न तपसा न दानेन न चेज्यया।

शक्य एवंविधो द्रष्टुं दृष्टवानसि मां यथा।।11.53।।
verses.translation: Not by the Vedas, nor by austerities, nor by gifts, nor by sacrifice, am I to be seen in the form in which you have seen Me.

verses.sanskrit:
भक्त्या त्वनन्यया शक्यमहमेवंविधोऽर्जुन।

ज्ञातुं दृष्टुं च तत्त्वेन प्रवेष्टुं च परंतप।।11.54।।
verses.translation: But by undivided devotion, O Arjuna, I can thus be known, truly seen, and entered into, O scorcher of foes.

verses.sanskrit:
मत्कर्मकृन्मत्परमो मद्भक्तः सङ्गवर्जितः।

निर्वैरः सर्वभूतेषु यः स मामेति पाण्डव।।11.55।।
verses.translation: He who does work for Me, who holds Me as his supreme goal, who is devoted to Me, free from attachment, without enmity toward any being — he comes to Me, O Pandava.

verses.sanskrit: अर्जुन उवाचएवं सततयुक्ता ये भक्तास्त्वां पर्युपासते।येचाप्यक्षरमव्यक्तं तेषां के योगवित्तमाः।।12.1।।
verses.translation:
Arjuna said:
Those devotees who, ever-yoked, worship You, and those who worship the imperishable Unmanifest — of these, who are the better knowers of yoga?

verses.sanskrit: श्री भगवानुवाचमय्यावेश्य मनो ये मां नित्ययुक्ता उपासते।श्रद्धया परयोपेतास्ते मे युक्ततमा मताः।।12.2।।
verses.translation:
The Blessed Lord said:
Those who, fixing their mind on Me, ever-yoked, worship Me endowed with supreme faith — them I regard as the most deeply yoked.

verses.sanskrit: ये त्वक्षरमनिर्देश्यमव्यक्तं पर्युपासते।सर्वत्रगमचिन्त्यं च कूटस्थमचलं ध्रुवम्।।12.3।।
verses.translation: But those who worship the Imperishable — the Indefinable, the Unmanifest, the Omnipresent, the Unthinkable, the Unchanging, the Immovable, the Constant —

verses.sanskrit: संनियम्येन्द्रियग्रामं सर्वत्र समबुद्धयः।ते प्राप्नुवन्ति मामेव सर्वभूतहिते रताः।।12.4।।
verses.translation: controlling the senses, of even-minded everywhere, delighting in the welfare of all beings — they too attain Me alone.

verses.sanskrit:
क्लेशोऽधिकतरस्तेषामव्यक्तासक्तचेतसाम्।

अव्यक्ता हि गतिर्दुःखं देहवद्भिरवाप्यते।।12.5।।
verses.translation: The trouble is greater for those whose minds are fixed on the unmanifest; for the goal of the unmanifest is attained with difficulty by the embodied.

verses.sanskrit: ये तु सर्वाणि कर्माणि मयि संन्यस्य मत्पराः।अनन्येनैव योगेन मां ध्यायन्त उपासते।।12.6।।
verses.translation: But those who, surrendering all actions to Me, intent on Me, worship Me, meditating on Me with undivided yoga —

verses.sanskrit: तेषामहं समुद्धर्ता मृत्युसंसारसागरात्।भवामि नचिरात्पार्थ मय्यावेशितचेतसाम्।।12.7।।
verses.translation: of those whose mind has entered into Me, I am soon the redeemer from the ocean of the mortal cycle, O Partha.

verses.sanskrit: मय्येव मन आधत्स्व मयि बुद्धिं निवेशय।निवसिष्यसि मय्येव अत ऊर्ध्वं न संशयः।।12.8।।
verses.translation: Fix your mind on Me alone; let your intellect dwell in Me. Thereafter, without doubt, you shall dwell in Me alone.

verses.sanskrit: अथ चित्तं समाधातुं न शक्नोषि मयि स्थिरम्।अभ्यासयोगेन ततो मामिच्छाप्तुं धनञ्जय।।12.9।।
verses.translation: But if you cannot fix your mind steadily on Me, then seek to reach Me through the practice of yoga, O Dhananjaya.

verses.sanskrit: अभ्यासेऽप्यसमर्थोऽसि मत्कर्मपरमो भव।मदर्थमपि कर्माणि कुर्वन् सिद्धिमवाप्स्यसि।।12.10।।
verses.translation: If you are incapable even of practice, devote yourself to working for Me. Even performing actions for My sake, you shall attain perfection.

verses.sanskrit: अथैतदप्यशक्तोऽसि कर्तुं मद्योगमाश्रितः।सर्वकर्मफलत्यागं ततः कुरु यतात्मवान्।।12.11।।
verses.translation: If you are unable to do even this, then taking refuge in yoga in Me, give up the fruits of all action, self-controlled.

verses.sanskrit: श्रेयो हि ज्ञानमभ्यासाज्ज्ञानाद्ध्यानं विशिष्यते।ध्यानात्कर्मफलत्यागस्त्यागाच्छान्तिरनन्तरम्।।12.12।।
verses.translation: Knowledge is indeed superior to practice; meditation is superior to knowledge; renunciation of the fruit of action to meditation; from such renunciation, peace immediately follows.

verses.sanskrit: अद्वेष्टा सर्वभूतानां मैत्रः करुण एव च।निर्ममो निरहङ्कारः समदुःखसुखः क्षमी।।12.13।।
verses.translation: He who hates no creature, who is friendly and compassionate to all, free from the sense of 'mine' and 'I,' even-minded in pleasure and pain, forgiving;

verses.sanskrit: सन्तुष्टः सततं योगी यतात्मा दृढनिश्चयः।मय्यर्पितमनोबुद्धिर्यो मद्भक्तः स मे प्रियः।।12.14।।
verses.translation: ever content, yoked, self-controlled, of firm resolve, with mind and intellect offered to Me — he, My devotee, is dear to Me.

verses.sanskrit: यस्मान्नोद्विजते लोको लोकान्नोद्विजते च यः।हर्षामर्षभयोद्वेगैर्मुक्तो यः स च मे प्रियः।।12.15।।
verses.translation: He by whom the world is not disturbed, and who is not disturbed by the world, free from delight, anger, fear, and agitation — he too is dear to Me.

verses.sanskrit: अनपेक्षः शुचिर्दक्ष उदासीनो गतव्यथः।सर्वारम्भपरित्यागी यो मद्भक्तः स मे प्रियः।।12.16।।
verses.translation: He who is not dependent, pure, skillful, unconcerned, untroubled, renouncing all undertakings — he, My devotee, is dear to Me.

verses.sanskrit: यो न हृष्यति न द्वेष्टि न शोचति न काङ्क्षति।शुभाशुभपरित्यागी भक्ितमान्यः स मे प्रियः।।12.17।।
verses.translation: He who neither rejoices nor hates, neither grieves nor desires, renouncing both good and evil, full of devotion — he is dear to Me.

verses.sanskrit: समः शत्रौ च मित्रे च तथा मानापमानयोः।शीतोष्णसुखदुःखेषु समः सङ्गविवर्जितः।।12.18।।
verses.translation: The same to foe and friend, the same in honor and dishonor, the same in cold and heat, in pleasure and pain, free from attachment;

verses.sanskrit: तुल्यनिन्दास्तुतिर्मौनी सन्तुष्टो येनकेनचित्।अनिकेतः स्थिरमतिर्भक्ितमान्मे प्रियो नरः।।12.19।।
verses.translation: to whom blame and praise are equal, silent, content with anything, homeless, of steady mind, full of devotion — that man is dear to Me.

verses.sanskrit: ये तु धर्म्यामृतमिदं यथोक्तं पर्युपासते।श्रद्दधाना मत्परमा भक्तास्तेऽतीव मे प्रियाः।।12.20।।
verses.translation: But those who follow this nectar of dharma as declared, with faith, holding Me as the supreme goal — the devotees, they are exceedingly dear to Me.

chapters.chapter_title_english: The Yoga of the Distinction between the Field and the Knower of the Field

verses.sanskrit:
अर्जुन उवाच

प्रकृतिं पुरुषं चैव क्षेत्रं क्षेत्रज्ञमेव च।

एतद्वेदितुमिच्छामि ज्ञानं ज्ञेयं च केशव।।13.1।।
verses.translation:
Arjuna said:
Prakriti and Purusha, the field (kshetra) and the knower of the field (kshetrajna), knowledge and that which is to be known — these I desire to know, O Keshava.

verses.sanskrit: श्री भगवानुवाचइदं शरीरं कौन्तेय क्षेत्रमित्यभिधीयते।एतद्यो वेत्ति तं प्राहुः क्षेत्रज्ञ इति तद्विदः।।13.2।।
verses.translation:
The Blessed Lord said:
This body, O son of Kunti, is called the field; him who knows this, the knowers of truth call the knower of the field.

verses.sanskrit:
क्षेत्रज्ञं चापि मां विद्धि सर्वक्षेत्रेषु भारत।

क्षेत्रक्षेत्रज्ञयोर्ज्ञानं यत्तज्ज्ञानं मतं मम।।13.3।।
verses.translation: Know Me also, O Bharata, as the knower of the field in every field. The knowledge of the field and its knower — that I hold to be true knowledge.

verses.sanskrit: तत्क्षेत्रं यच्च यादृक् च यद्विकारि यतश्च यत्।स च यो यत्प्रभावश्च तत्समासेन मे श्रृणु।।13.4।।
verses.translation: What this field is, of what nature, of what modifications, whence it is, who He (its knower) is and what His powers — hear from Me briefly.

verses.sanskrit: ऋषिभिर्बहुधा गीतं छन्दोभिर्विविधैः पृथक्।ब्रह्मसूत्रपदैश्चैव हेतुमद्भिर्विनिश्िचतैः।।13.5।।
verses.translation: This has been sung in many ways by the rishis, distinctly in various hymns, and in the well-reasoned, decisive verses of the Brahma-sutras.

verses.sanskrit: महाभूतान्यहङ्कारो बुद्धिरव्यक्तमेव च।इन्द्रियाणि दशैकं च पञ्च चेन्द्रियगोचराः।।13.6।।
verses.translation: The great elements, egoism, intellect, the unmanifest (prakriti), the ten senses and the one (mind), and the five fields of the senses;

verses.sanskrit: इच्छा द्वेषः सुखं दुःखं सङ्घातश्चेतनाधृतिः।एतत्क्षेत्रं समासेन सविकारमुदाहृतम्।।13.7।।
verses.translation: desire, aversion, pleasure, pain, the aggregate (body), intelligence, steadfastness — this, in brief, is described as the field with its modifications.

verses.sanskrit: अमानित्वमदम्भित्वमहिंसा क्षान्तिरार्जवम्।आचार्योपासनं शौचं स्थैर्यमात्मविनिग्रहः।।13.8।।
verses.translation: Absence of pride, absence of hypocrisy, non-injury, forbearance, uprightness, service to the teacher, purity, steadiness, self-control;

verses.sanskrit: इन्द्रियार्थेषु वैराग्यमनहङ्कार एव च।जन्ममृत्युजराव्याधिदुःखदोषानुदर्शनम्।।13.9।।
verses.translation: dispassion toward the objects of the senses, and absence of egoism; insight into the evils of birth, death, old age, sickness, and pain;

verses.sanskrit: असक्ितरनभिष्वङ्गः पुत्रदारगृहादिषु।नित्यं च समचित्तत्वमिष्टानिष्टोपपत्तिषु।।13.10।।
verses.translation: non-attachment, non-identification with son, wife, home, and the rest; and constant evenness of mind in the happening of the desirable and the undesirable;

verses.sanskrit: मयि चानन्ययोगेन भक्ितरव्यभिचारिणी।विविक्तदेशसेवित्वमरतिर्जनसंसदि।।13.11।।
verses.translation: and unswerving devotion to Me by the yoga of non-separation; resort to solitary places, distaste for the company of men;

verses.sanskrit: अध्यात्मज्ञाननित्यत्वं तत्त्वज्ञानार्थदर्शनम्।एतज्ज्ञानमिति प्रोक्तमज्ञानं यदतोन्यथा।।13.12।।
verses.translation: constancy in the knowledge of the Self, and insight into the end of the knowledge of truth — this is declared to be knowledge; what is opposed to it is ignorance.

verses.sanskrit: ज्ञेयं यत्तत्प्रवक्ष्यामि यज्ज्ञात्वाऽमृतमश्नुते।अनादिमत्परं ब्रह्म न सत्तन्नासदुच्यते।।13.13।।
verses.translation: I shall declare that which is to be known, by knowing which one attains immortality — the beginningless supreme Brahman, which is said to be neither being nor non-being.

verses.sanskrit: सर्वतः पाणिपादं तत्सर्वतोऽक्षिशिरोमुखम्।सर्वतः श्रुतिमल्लोके सर्वमावृत्य तिष्ठति।।13.14।।
verses.translation: With hands and feet everywhere, with eyes, heads, and faces everywhere, with ears on all sides — it stands enveloping everything in the world.

verses.sanskrit: सर्वेन्द्रियगुणाभासं सर्वेन्द्रियविवर्जितम्।असक्तं सर्वभृच्चैव निर्गुणं गुणभोक्तृ च।।13.15।।
verses.translation: Appearing to have the qualities of all the senses, yet free from all senses; unattached, yet supporting all; free from the gunas, yet the experiencer of the gunas.

verses.sanskrit: बहिरन्तश्च भूतानामचरं चरमेव च।सूक्ष्मत्वात्तदविज्ञेयं दूरस्थं चान्तिके च तत्।।13.16।।
verses.translation: Within and without all beings; the unmoving and also the moving; because of its subtlety, it is incomprehensible; it is far and yet near.

verses.sanskrit: अविभक्तं च भूतेषु विभक्तमिव च स्थितम्।भूतभर्तृ च तज्ज्ञेयं ग्रसिष्णु प्रभविष्णु च।।13.17।।
verses.translation: Undivided, yet It seems to stand divided in beings; It is to be known as the supporter of beings — their devourer and their creator.

verses.sanskrit: ज्योतिषामपि तज्ज्योतिस्तमसः परमुच्यते।ज्ञानं ज्ञेयं ज्ञानगम्यं हृदि सर्वस्य विष्ठितम्।।13.18।।
verses.translation: The Light of lights, It is said to be beyond darkness; It is Knowledge, the Knowable, the Goal of knowledge — seated in the hearts of all.

verses.sanskrit: इति क्षेत्रं तथा ज्ञानं ज्ञेयं चोक्तं समासतः।मद्भक्त एतद्विज्ञाय मद्भावायोपपद्यते।।13.19।।
verses.translation: Thus the field, knowledge, and the knowable have been briefly declared. My devotee, knowing this, attains to My state of being.

verses.sanskrit: प्रकृतिं पुरुषं चैव विद्ध्यनादी उभावपि।विकारांश्च गुणांश्चैव विद्धि प्रकृतिसंभवान्।।13.20।।
verses.translation: Know both Prakriti and Purusha to be without beginning. Know also that modifications and the gunas are born of Prakriti.

verses.sanskrit: कार्यकारणकर्तृत्वे हेतुः प्रकृतिरुच्यते।पुरुषः सुखदुःखानां भोक्तृत्वे हेतुरुच्यते।।13.21।।
verses.translation: Prakriti is said to be the cause in the production of cause and effect (bodily activities); Purusha is said to be the cause in the experience of pleasure and pain.

verses.sanskrit: पुरुषः प्रकृतिस्थो हि भुङ्क्ते प्रकृतिजान्गुणान्।कारणं गुणसङ्गोऽस्य सदसद्योनिजन्मसु।।13.22।।
verses.translation: For Purusha, seated in Prakriti, experiences the gunas born of Prakriti. Attachment to the gunas is the cause of his birth in good and evil wombs.

verses.sanskrit: उपद्रष्टाऽनुमन्ता च भर्ता भोक्ता महेश्वरः।परमात्मेति चाप्युक्तो देहेऽस्मिन्पुरुषः परः।।13.23।।
verses.translation: The supreme Purusha in this body is also called the spectator, the permitter, the sustainer, the experiencer, the great Lord, the supreme Self.

verses.sanskrit: य एवं वेत्ति पुरुषं प्रकृतिं च गुणैःसह।सर्वथा वर्तमानोऽपि न स भूयोऽभिजायते।।13.24।।
verses.translation: He who thus knows Purusha and Prakriti together with the gunas, however he may be engaged, is not born again.

verses.sanskrit: ध्यानेनात्मनि पश्यन्ति केचिदात्मानमात्मना।अन्ये सांख्येन योगेन कर्मयोगेन चापरे।।13.25।।
verses.translation: Some by meditation behold the Self in themselves by the self; others by the yoga of Sankhya, and others by the yoga of action.

verses.sanskrit: अन्ये त्वेवमजानन्तः श्रुत्वाऽन्येभ्य उपासते।तेऽपि चातितरन्त्येव मृत्युं श्रुतिपरायणाः।।13.26।।
verses.translation: Yet others, not knowing, worship as they have heard from others; they too cross beyond death, clinging to what they have heard.

verses.sanskrit: यावत्सञ्जायते किञ्चित्सत्त्वं स्थावरजङ्गमम्।क्षेत्रक्षेत्रज्ञसंयोगात्तद्विद्धि भरतर्षभ।।13.27।।
verses.translation: Whatever being is born, moving or unmoving, know that, O best of the Bharatas, to arise from the union of the field and the knower of the field.

verses.sanskrit: समं सर्वेषु भूतेषु तिष्ठन्तं परमेश्वरम्।विनश्यत्स्वविनश्यन्तं यः पश्यति स पश्यति।।13.28।।
verses.translation: He who sees the supreme Lord dwelling equally in all beings, imperishable among the perishing — he truly sees.

verses.sanskrit: समं पश्यन्हि सर्वत्र समवस्थितमीश्वरम्।न हिनस्त्यात्मनाऽऽत्मानं ततो याति परां गतिम्।।13.29।।
verses.translation: For seeing the same Lord established everywhere, he does not destroy the Self by the self, and therefore attains the supreme goal.

verses.sanskrit: प्रकृत्यैव च कर्माणि क्रियमाणानि सर्वशः।यः पश्यति तथाऽऽत्मानमकर्तारं स पश्यति।।13.30।।
verses.translation: He who sees that all action is performed by Prakriti alone, and that the Self is therefore a non-doer — he truly sees.

verses.sanskrit: यदा भूतपृथग्भावमेकस्थमनुपश्यति।तत एव च विस्तारं ब्रह्म सम्पद्यते तदा।।13.31।।
verses.translation: When he sees the diverse states of beings as abiding in the One, and their expansion from That alone — then he attains Brahman.

verses.sanskrit: अनादित्वान्निर्गुणत्वात्परमात्मायमव्ययः।शरीरस्थोऽपि कौन्तेय न करोति न लिप्यते।।13.32।।
verses.translation: Because of being without beginning and without qualities, this imperishable supreme Self, O son of Kunti, though dwelling in the body, neither acts nor is tainted.

verses.sanskrit: यथा सर्वगतं सौक्ष्म्यादाकाशं नोपलिप्यते।सर्वत्रावस्थितो देहे तथाऽऽत्मा नोपलिप्यते।।13.33।।
verses.translation: As the all-pervading ether, because of its subtlety, is not tainted, so the Self, seated everywhere in the body, is not tainted.

verses.sanskrit: यथा प्रकाशयत्येकः कृत्स्नं लोकमिमं रविः।क्षेत्रं क्षेत्री तथा कृत्स्नं प्रकाशयति भारत।।13.34।।
verses.translation: As the one sun illumines this whole world, so the Lord of the field (the Self) illumines the entire field, O Bharata.

verses.sanskrit: क्षेत्रक्षेत्रज्ञयोरेवमन्तरं ज्ञानचक्षुषा।भूतप्रकृतिमोक्षं च ये विदुर्यान्ति ते परम्।।13.35।।
verses.translation: Those who thus perceive, by the eye of knowledge, the distinction between the field and the knower of the field, and the liberation of beings from Prakriti — they attain the Supreme.

chapters.chapter_title_english: The Yoga of the Division of the Three Gunas

verses.sanskrit: श्री भगवानुवाचपरं भूयः प्रवक्ष्यामि ज्ञानानां ज्ञानमुत्तमम्।यज्ज्ञात्वा मुनयः सर्वे परां सिद्धिमितो गताः।।14.1।।
verses.translation:
The Blessed Lord said:
Again I shall declare the supreme knowledge, the best of all knowledge, knowing which all sages have passed from this world to supreme perfection.

verses.sanskrit: इदं ज्ञानमुपाश्रित्य मम साधर्म्यमागताः।सर्गेऽपि नोपजायन्ते प्रलये न व्यथन्ति च।।14.2।।
verses.translation: Resorting to this knowledge, having come to identity with Me, they are not born at the creation, nor troubled at the dissolution.

verses.sanskrit: मम योनिर्महद्ब्रह्म तस्मिन् गर्भं दधाम्यहम्।संभवः सर्वभूतानां ततो भवति भारत।।14.3।।
verses.translation: My womb is the great Brahman (prakriti); in it I place the seed. From that, O Bharata, is the birth of all beings.

verses.sanskrit: सर्वयोनिषु कौन्तेय मूर्तयः सम्भवन्ति याः।तासां ब्रह्म महद्योनिरहं बीजप्रदः पिता।।14.4।।
verses.translation: Whatever forms come forth from all wombs, O son of Kunti, of these, the great Brahman is the womb; I am the seed-giving father.

verses.sanskrit: सत्त्वं रजस्तम इति गुणाः प्रकृतिसंभवाः।निबध्नन्ति महाबाहो देहे देहिनमव्ययम्।।14.5।।
verses.translation: Sattva, rajas, and tamas — these gunas, born of Prakriti, O mighty-armed one, bind in the body the imperishable embodied one.

verses.sanskrit: तत्र सत्त्वं निर्मलत्वात्प्रकाशकमनामयम्।सुखसङ्गेन बध्नाति ज्ञानसङ्गेन चानघ।।14.6।।
verses.translation: Among these, sattva, because of its purity, is illuminating and free from disease; it binds, O sinless one, by attachment to happiness and by attachment to knowledge.

verses.sanskrit: रजो रागात्मकं विद्धि तृष्णासङ्गसमुद्भवम्।तन्निबध्नाति कौन्तेय कर्मसङ्गेन देहिनम्।।14.7।।
verses.translation: Know rajas to be of the nature of passion, the source of thirst and attachment. It binds, O son of Kunti, the embodied by attachment to action.

verses.sanskrit: तमस्त्वज्ञानजं विद्धि मोहनं सर्वदेहिनाम्।प्रमादालस्यनिद्राभिस्तन्निबध्नाति भारत।।14.8।।
verses.translation: Know tamas to be born of ignorance, the deluder of all embodied beings. It binds, O Bharata, through heedlessness, indolence, and sleep.

verses.sanskrit: सत्त्वं सुखे सञ्जयति रजः कर्मणि भारत।ज्ञानमावृत्य तु तमः प्रमादे सञ्जयत्युत।।14.9।।
verses.translation: Sattva attaches to happiness, rajas to action, O Bharata; but tamas, veiling knowledge, attaches to heedlessness.

verses.sanskrit: रजस्तमश्चाभिभूय सत्त्वं भवति भारत।रजः सत्त्वं तमश्चैव तमः सत्त्वं रजस्तथा।।14.10।।
verses.translation: Sattva arises overcoming rajas and tamas, O Bharata; rajas, overcoming sattva and tamas; and tamas, overcoming sattva and rajas.

verses.sanskrit: सर्वद्वारेषु देहेऽस्मिन्प्रकाश उपजायते।ज्ञानं यदा तदा विद्याद्विवृद्धं सत्त्वमित्युत।।14.11।।
verses.translation: When the light of knowledge streams forth at all the gates of this body, then it should be known that sattva is predominant.

verses.sanskrit: लोभः प्रवृत्तिरारम्भः कर्मणामशमः स्पृहा।रजस्येतानि जायन्ते विवृद्धे भरतर्षभ।।14.12।।
verses.translation: Greed, activity, undertaking of actions, restlessness, longing — these are born when rajas is predominant, O bull of the Bharatas.

verses.sanskrit: अप्रकाशोऽप्रवृत्तिश्च प्रमादो मोह एव च।तमस्येतानि जायन्ते विवृद्धे कुरुनन्दन।।14.13।।
verses.translation: Darkness, inactivity, heedlessness, and delusion — these arise when tamas is predominant, O joy of the Kurus.

verses.sanskrit: यदा सत्त्वे प्रवृद्धे तु प्रलयं याति देहभृत्।तदोत्तमविदां लोकानमलान्प्रतिपद्यते।।14.14।।
verses.translation: If the embodied one goes to dissolution when sattva prevails, then he attains the pure worlds of those who know the Supreme.

verses.sanskrit: रजसि प्रलयं गत्वा कर्मसङ्गिषु जायते।तथा प्रलीनस्तमसि मूढयोनिषु जायते।।14.15।।
verses.translation: Going to dissolution in rajas, he is born among those attached to action; and dissolving in tamas, he is born in the wombs of the deluded.

verses.sanskrit: कर्मणः सुकृतस्याहुः सात्त्विकं निर्मलं फलम्।रजसस्तु फलं दुःखमज्ञानं तमसः फलम्।।14.16।।
verses.translation: The fruit of good action is said to be sattvic and pure; the fruit of rajas is pain; the fruit of tamas is ignorance.

verses.sanskrit: सत्त्वात्सञ्जायते ज्ञानं रजसो लोभ एव च।प्रमादमोहौ तमसो भवतोऽज्ञानमेव च।।14.17।।
verses.translation: From sattva arises knowledge; from rajas, greed; from tamas arise heedlessness, delusion, and ignorance.

verses.sanskrit: ऊर्ध्वं गच्छन्ति सत्त्वस्था मध्ये तिष्ठन्ति राजसाः।जघन्यगुणवृत्तिस्था अधो गच्छन्ति तामसाः।।14.18।।
verses.translation: Those established in sattva go upwards; those in rajas remain in the middle; those in tamas, abiding in the function of the lowest guna, go downwards.

verses.sanskrit: नान्यं गुणेभ्यः कर्तारं यदा द्रष्टानुपश्यति।गुणेभ्यश्च परं वेत्ति मद्भावं सोऽधिगच्छति।।14.19।।
verses.translation: When the seer perceives no agent other than the gunas, and knows that which is higher than the gunas — he attains to My state of being.

verses.sanskrit: गुणानेतानतीत्य त्रीन्देही देहसमुद्भवान्।जन्ममृत्युजरादुःखैर्विमुक्तोऽमृतमश्नुते।।14.20।।
verses.translation: Transcending these three gunas, the source of the body, the embodied one, freed from birth, death, old age, and pain, attains immortality.

verses.sanskrit: अर्जुन उवाचकैर्लिंगैस्त्रीन्गुणानेतानतीतो भवति प्रभो।किमाचारः कथं चैतांस्त्रीन्गुणानतिवर्तते।।14.21।।
verses.translation:
Arjuna said:
By what marks, O Lord, is he known who has transcended these three gunas? What is his conduct, and how does he pass beyond these three gunas?

verses.sanskrit: श्री भगवानुवाचप्रकाशं च प्रवृत्तिं च मोहमेव च पाण्डव।न द्वेष्टि सम्प्रवृत्तानि न निवृत्तानि काङ्क्षति।।14.22।।
verses.translation:
The Blessed Lord said:
O Pandava, he who does not hate illumination, activity, or delusion when present, nor longs for them when absent;

verses.sanskrit: उदासीनवदासीनो गुणैर्यो न विचाल्यते।गुणा वर्तन्त इत्येव योऽवतिष्ठति नेङ्गते।।14.23।।
verses.translation: he who, seated as if indifferent, is not shaken by the gunas; who, thinking 'the gunas act,' stands firm and does not waver;

verses.sanskrit: समदुःखसुखः स्वस्थः समलोष्टाश्मकाञ्चनः।तुल्यप्रियाप्रियो धीरस्तुल्यनिन्दात्मसंस्तुतिः।।14.24।।
verses.translation: the same in pleasure and pain, self-contained, to whom a clod, stone, and gold are alike, the same to the dear and the undear, steadfast, the same in blame and praise of himself;

verses.sanskrit: मानापमानयोस्तुल्यस्तुल्यो मित्रारिपक्षयोः।सर्वारम्भपरित्यागी गुणातीतः स उच्यते।।14.25।।
verses.translation: the same in honor and dishonor, the same to friend and foe, abandoning all undertakings — he is said to have transcended the gunas.

verses.sanskrit: मां च योऽव्यभिचारेण भक्ितयोगेन सेवते।स गुणान्समतीत्यैतान् ब्रह्मभूयाय कल्पते।।14.26।।
verses.translation: He who serves Me with unswerving yoga of devotion — crossing beyond the gunas, he becomes fit to become Brahman.

verses.sanskrit: ब्रह्मणो हि प्रतिष्ठाऽहममृतस्याव्ययस्य च।शाश्वतस्य च धर्मस्य सुखस्यैकान्तिकस्य च।।14.27।।
verses.translation: For I am the abode of Brahman, the immortal and imperishable, of eternal dharma, and of absolute bliss.

chapters.chapter_title_english: The Yoga of the Supreme Person (Purushottama Yoga)

verses.sanskrit: श्री भगवानुवाचऊर्ध्वमूलमधःशाखमश्वत्थं प्राहुरव्ययम्।छन्दांसि यस्य पर्णानि यस्तं वेद स वेदवित्।।15.1।।
verses.translation:
The Blessed Lord said:
They speak of the imperishable ashvattha (peepul) tree, with its roots above and branches below; its leaves are the Vedic hymns. He who knows it, knows the Vedas.

verses.sanskrit: अधश्चोर्ध्वं प्रसृतास्तस्य शाखा     गुणप्रवृद्धा विषयप्रवालाः।अधश्च मूलान्यनुसन्ततानि     कर्मानुबन्धीनि मनुष्यलोके।।15.2।।
verses.translation: Below and above its branches spread, nourished by the gunas; the objects of sense are its twigs. Below, also, its roots stretch forth, giving rise to action in the world of men.

verses.sanskrit: न रूपमस्येह तथोपलभ्यते    नान्तो न चादिर्न च संप्रतिष्ठा।अश्वत्थमेनं सुविरूढमूल    मसङ्गशस्त्रेण दृढेन छित्त्वा।।15.3।।
verses.translation: Its form is not perceived here as such — neither its end, nor its beginning, nor its foundation. Cutting this firmly-rooted ashvattha with the strong axe of non-attachment,

verses.sanskrit: ततः पदं तत्परिमार्गितव्य    यस्मिन्गता न निवर्तन्ति भूयः।तमेव चाद्यं पुरुषं प्रपद्ये    यतः प्रवृत्तिः प्रसृता पुराणी।।15.4।।
verses.translation: one should seek that place, reaching which they do not return: 'I take refuge in that primal Person from whom the ancient stream of activity has flowed.'

verses.sanskrit: निर्मानमोहा जितसङ्गदोषा    अध्यात्मनित्या विनिवृत्तकामाः।द्वन्द्वैर्विमुक्ताः सुखदुःखसंज्ञै    र्गच्छन्त्यमूढाः पदमव्ययं तत्।।15.5।।
verses.translation: Free from pride and delusion, having conquered the evil of attachment, ever dwelling in the Self, desires withdrawn, freed from the pairs of opposites known as pleasure and pain — the undeluded reach that imperishable abode.

verses.sanskrit: न तद्भासयते सूर्यो न शशाङ्को न पावकः।यद्गत्वा न निवर्तन्ते तद्धाम परमं मम।।15.6।।
verses.translation: Neither the sun, nor the moon, nor fire illumines that place; having reached which they do not return — that is My supreme abode.

verses.sanskrit: ममैवांशो जीवलोके जीवभूतः सनातनः।मनःषष्ठानीन्द्रियाणि प्रकृतिस्थानि कर्षति।।15.7।।
verses.translation: An eternal part of Me, becoming a jiva in the world of living beings, draws to itself the mind and the five senses resting in Prakriti.

verses.sanskrit: शरीरं यदवाप्नोति यच्चाप्युत्क्रामतीश्वरः।गृहीत्वैतानि संयाति वायुर्गन्धानिवाशयात्।।15.8।।
verses.translation: When the Lord (of the body) takes up a body and when He departs from it, He takes these and goes, as the wind carries scents from their places.

verses.sanskrit: श्रोत्रं चक्षुः स्पर्शनं च रसनं घ्राणमेव च।अधिष्ठाय मनश्चायं विषयानुपसेवते।।15.9।।
verses.translation: Presiding over the ear, eye, touch, taste, smell, and also the mind, he experiences the objects of the senses.

verses.sanskrit: उत्क्रामन्तं स्थितं वापि भुञ्जानं वा गुणान्वितम्।विमूढा नानुपश्यन्ति पश्यन्ति ज्ञानचक्षुषः।।15.10।।
verses.translation: Whether departing or remaining, or experiencing, united with the gunas — the deluded do not see; but those who possess the eye of knowledge see.

verses.sanskrit: यतन्तो योगिनश्चैनं पश्यन्त्यात्मन्यवस्थितम्।यतन्तोऽप्यकृतात्मानो नैनं पश्यन्त्यचेतसः।।15.11।।
verses.translation: The striving yogis also see Him seated in themselves; but the unintelligent, with uncultivated selves, though striving, do not see Him.

verses.sanskrit: यदादित्यगतं तेजो जगद्भासयतेऽखिलम्।यच्चन्द्रमसि यच्चाग्नौ तत्तेजो विद्धि मामकम्।।15.12।।
verses.translation: That radiance of the sun that illumines the whole world, and which is in the moon and in fire — know that radiance to be Mine.

verses.sanskrit: गामाविश्य च भूतानि धारयाम्यहमोजसा।पुष्णामि चौषधीः सर्वाः सोमो भूत्वा रसात्मकः।।15.13।।
verses.translation: Entering the earth, I sustain all beings with My energy. Becoming the sap-full moon, I nourish all plants.

verses.sanskrit: अहं वैश्वानरो भूत्वा प्राणिनां देहमाश्रितः।प्राणापानसमायुक्तः पचाम्यन्नं चतुर्विधम्।।15.14।।
verses.translation: Becoming the fire Vaishvanara that dwells in the body of breathing creatures, joining the prana and apana, I digest the fourfold food.

verses.sanskrit: सर्वस्य चाहं हृदि सन्निविष्टो    मत्तः स्मृतिर्ज्ञानमपोहनं च।वेदैश्च सर्वैरहमेव वेद्यो    वेदान्तकृद्वेदविदेव चाहम्।।15.15।।
verses.translation: And I am seated in the hearts of all; from Me come memory, knowledge, and their loss. I alone am to be known through all the Vedas; I am the author of Vedanta, and the knower of the Vedas.

verses.sanskrit: द्वाविमौ पुरुषौ लोके क्षरश्चाक्षर एव च।क्षरः सर्वाणि भूतानि कूटस्थोऽक्षर उच्यते।।15.16।।
verses.translation: There are these two persons (purushas) in the world — the perishable and the imperishable. The perishable is all creatures; the imperishable is called the unchanging (kutastha).

verses.sanskrit: उत्तमः पुरुषस्त्वन्यः परमात्मेत्युदाहृतः।यो लोकत्रयमाविश्य बिभर्त्यव्यय ईश्वरः।।15.17।।
verses.translation: But the highest Person is another, called the supreme Self, the imperishable Lord who, entering the three worlds, sustains them.

verses.sanskrit: यस्मात्क्षरमतीतोऽहमक्षरादपि चोत्तमः।अतोऽस्मि लोके वेदे च प्रथितः पुरुषोत्तमः।।15.18।।
verses.translation: Since I transcend the perishable, and am higher even than the imperishable, I am celebrated in the world and in the Veda as Purushottama (the Supreme Person).

verses.sanskrit: यो मामेवमसम्मूढो जानाति पुरुषोत्तमम्।स सर्वविद्भजति मां सर्वभावेन भारत।।15.19।।
verses.translation: He who, undeluded, thus knows Me as Purushottama, knows all; he worships Me with all his being, O Bharata.

verses.sanskrit: इति गुह्यतमं शास्त्रमिदमुक्तं मयाऽनघ।एतद्बुद्ध्वा बुद्धिमान्स्यात्कृतकृत्यश्च भारत।।15.20।।
verses.translation: Thus, O sinless one, this most secret teaching has been declared by Me. Having understood this, one becomes wise, and has fulfilled all his duties, O Bharata.

chapters.chapter_title_english: The Yoga of the Division between the Divine and Demoniac

verses.sanskrit:
श्री भगवानुवाच

अभयं सत्त्वसंशुद्धिः ज्ञानयोगव्यवस्थितिः।

दानं दमश्च यज्ञश्च स्वाध्यायस्तप आर्जवम्।।16.1।।
verses.translation:
The Blessed Lord said:
Fearlessness, purity of heart, steadfastness in the yoga of knowledge, charity, self-restraint, sacrifice, study of scripture, austerity, uprightness;

verses.sanskrit: अहिंसा सत्यमक्रोधस्त्यागः शान्तिरपैशुनम्।दया भूतेष्वलोलुप्त्वं मार्दवं ह्रीरचापलम्।।16.2।।
verses.translation: non-injury, truthfulness, absence of anger, renunciation, tranquility, absence of calumny, compassion for beings, freedom from covetousness, gentleness, modesty, freedom from fickleness;

verses.sanskrit:
तेजः क्षमा धृतिः शौचमद्रोहो नातिमानिता।

भवन्ति सम्पदं दैवीमभिजातस्य भारत।।16.3।।
verses.translation: vigor, forgiveness, fortitude, purity, freedom from malice, absence of pride — these belong to one born with the divine endowment, O Bharata.

verses.sanskrit: दम्भो दर्पोऽभिमानश्च क्रोधः पारुष्यमेव च।अज्ञानं चाभिजातस्य पार्थ सम्पदमासुरीम्।।16.4।।
verses.translation: Ostentation, arrogance, self-conceit, anger, harshness, and ignorance — these belong, O Partha, to one born with the demoniac endowment.

verses.sanskrit: दैवी सम्पद्विमोक्षाय निबन्धायासुरी मता।मा शुचः सम्पदं दैवीमभिजातोऽसि पाण्डव।।16.5।।
verses.translation: The divine endowment leads to liberation; the demoniac to bondage. Grieve not, O Pandava; you were born with the divine endowment.

verses.sanskrit: द्वौ भूतसर्गौ लोकेऽस्मिन् दैव आसुर एव च।दैवो विस्तरशः प्रोक्त आसुरं पार्थ मे श्रृणु।।16.6।।
verses.translation: There are two kinds of beings created in this world: the divine and the demoniac. The divine has been described at length; hear now from Me of the demoniac, O Partha.

verses.sanskrit: प्रवृत्तिं च निवृत्तिं च जना न विदुरासुराः।न शौचं नापि चाचारो न सत्यं तेषु विद्यते।।16.7।।
verses.translation: Demoniac men know neither action nor abstention from action. Purity, good conduct, truth — none of these is in them.

verses.sanskrit: असत्यमप्रतिष्ठं ते जगदाहुरनीश्वरम्।अपरस्परसम्भूतं किमन्यत्कामहैतुकम्।।16.8।।
verses.translation: 'The world is without truth, without moral basis, without a Lord,' they say; 'produced by mutual union, what else? — driven by lust.'

verses.sanskrit: एतां दृष्टिमवष्टभ्य नष्टात्मानोऽल्पबुद्धयः।प्रभवन्त्युग्रकर्माणः क्षयाय जगतोऽहिताः।।16.9।।
verses.translation: Holding this view, these lost souls of little understanding and cruel deeds arise as enemies of the world for its destruction.

verses.sanskrit: काममाश्रित्य दुष्पूरं दम्भमानमदान्विताः।मोहाद्गृहीत्वासद्ग्राहान्प्रवर्तन्तेऽशुचिव्रताः।।16.10।।
verses.translation: Clinging to insatiable lust, full of hypocrisy, pride, and arrogance, holding evil ideas through delusion, they engage in action with impure resolve.

verses.sanskrit: चिन्तामपरिमेयां च प्रलयान्तामुपाश्रिताः।कामोपभोगपरमा एतावदिति निश्िचताः।।16.11।।
verses.translation: Given over to immeasurable cares that end only with death, making the gratification of desires their highest aim, convinced that this is all —

verses.sanskrit: आशापाशशतैर्बद्धाः कामक्रोधपरायणाः।ईहन्ते कामभोगार्थमन्यायेनार्थसञ्चयान्।।16.12।।
verses.translation: bound by hundreds of fetters of expectation, given over to lust and anger, they strive to amass wealth by unjust means, to gratify their desires.
