verses.sanskrit: इदमद्य मया लब्धमिमं प्राप्स्ये मनोरथम्।इदमस्तीदमपि मे भविष्यति पुनर्धनम्।।16.13।।
verses.translation: 'This has been gained by me today; this desire I shall fulfil. This is mine; this wealth shall also be mine hereafter.'

verses.sanskrit: असौ मया हतः शत्रुर्हनिष्ये चापरानपि।ईश्वरोऽहमहं भोगी सिद्धोऽहं बलवान्सुखी।।16.14।।
verses.translation: 'That enemy has been slain by me; and others also I shall slay. I am the lord; I am the enjoyer; I am successful, mighty, and happy.'

verses.sanskrit: आढ्योऽभिजनवानस्मि कोऽन्योऽस्ति सदृशो मया।यक्ष्ये दास्यामि मोदिष्य इत्यज्ञानविमोहिताः।।16.15।।
verses.translation: 'I am wealthy and well-born. Who else is equal to me? I shall sacrifice, I shall give, I shall rejoice' — thus deluded by ignorance,

verses.sanskrit: अनेकचित्तविभ्रान्ता मोहजालसमावृताः।प्रसक्ताः कामभोगेषु पतन्ति नरकेऽशुचौ।।16.16।।
verses.translation: bewildered by many thoughts, caught in the net of delusion, attached to the gratification of desires, they fall into a foul hell.

verses.sanskrit: आत्मसम्भाविताः स्तब्धा धनमानमदान्विताः।यजन्ते नामयज्ञैस्ते दम्भेनाविधिपूर्वकम्।।16.17।।
verses.translation: Self-conceited, stubborn, full of the intoxication of wealth and pride, they perform sacrifices in name only, with hypocrisy, not according to the rule.

verses.sanskrit: अहङ्कारं बलं दर्पं कामं क्रोधं च संश्रिताः।मामात्मपरदेहेषु प्रद्विषन्तोऽभ्यसूयकाः।।16.18।।
verses.translation: Resorting to egotism, power, insolence, lust, and anger, these malignant ones hate Me in their own bodies and in those of others.

verses.sanskrit: तानहं द्विषतः क्रूरान्संसारेषु नराधमान्।क्षिपाम्यजस्रमशुभानासुरीष्वेव योनिषु।।16.19।।
verses.translation: These hateful and cruel evildoers, the lowest of men, I repeatedly hurl into demoniac wombs in the cycle of rebirths.

verses.sanskrit: असुरीं योनिमापन्ना मूढा जन्मनि जन्मनि।मामप्राप्यैव कौन्तेय ततो यान्त्यधमां गतिम्।।16.20।।
verses.translation: Entering demoniac wombs, the deluded, not attaining Me from birth to birth, thereafter go to the lowest state, O son of Kunti.

verses.sanskrit: त्रिविधं नरकस्येदं द्वारं नाशनमात्मनः।कामः क्रोधस्तथा लोभस्तस्मादेतत्त्रयं त्यजेत्।।16.21।।
verses.translation: Threefold is the gate of hell, destructive of the self: lust, anger, and greed. Therefore one should abandon these three.

verses.sanskrit: एतैर्विमुक्तः कौन्तेय तमोद्वारैस्त्रिभिर्नरः।आचरत्यात्मनः श्रेयस्ततो याति परां गतिम्।।16.22।।
verses.translation: The man who is freed from these three gates of darkness, O son of Kunti, practices what is good for himself and thereby attains the supreme goal.

verses.sanskrit: यः शास्त्रविधिमुत्सृज्य वर्तते कामकारतः।न स सिद्धिमवाप्नोति न सुखं न परां गतिम्।।16.23।।
verses.translation: He who, casting aside the ordinance of the scriptures, acts under the impulse of desire, attains neither perfection, nor happiness, nor the supreme goal.

verses.sanskrit: तस्माच्छास्त्रं प्रमाणं ते कार्याकार्यव्यवस्थितौ।ज्ञात्वा शास्त्रविधानोक्तं कर्म कर्तुमिहार्हसि।।16.24।।
verses.translation: Therefore let the scripture be your authority in determining what should be done and what should not be done. Knowing what is enjoined by the rule of the scripture, you should perform action here.

chapters.chapter_title_english: The Yoga of the Threefold Division of Faith

verses.sanskrit: अर्जुन उवाचये शास्त्रविधिमुत्सृज्य यजन्ते श्रद्धयाऽन्विताः।तेषां निष्ठा तु का कृष्ण सत्त्वमाहो रजस्तमः।।17.1।।
verses.translation:
Arjuna said:
Those who, setting aside the ordinance of scripture, yet worship with faith — what is their state, O Krishna? Is it sattva, rajas, or tamas?

verses.sanskrit: श्री भगवानुवाचत्रिविधा भवति श्रद्धा देहिनां सा स्वभावजा।सात्त्विकी राजसी चैव तामसी चेति तां श्रृणु।।17.2।।
verses.translation:
The Blessed Lord said:
The faith of the embodied is of three kinds, born of their own nature — sattvic, rajasic, and tamasic. Hear of it.

verses.sanskrit: सत्त्वानुरूपा सर्वस्य श्रद्धा भवति भारत।श्रद्धामयोऽयं पुरुषो यो यच्छ्रद्धः स एव सः।।17.3।।
verses.translation: The faith of each is according to his own nature, O Bharata. A person is formed by his faith; as his faith is, so is he.

verses.sanskrit: यजन्ते सात्त्विका देवान्यक्षरक्षांसि राजसाः।प्रेतान्भूतगणांश्चान्ये यजन्ते तामसा जनाः।।17.4।।
verses.translation: Sattvic men worship the gods; rajasic, the yakshas and rakshasas; tamasic folk worship ghosts and the hosts of bhutas.

verses.sanskrit: अशास्त्रविहितं घोरं तप्यन्ते ये तपो जनाः।दम्भाहङ्कारसंयुक्ताः कामरागबलान्विताः।।17.5।।
verses.translation: Those who undertake fearsome austerities, not enjoined by scripture, bound by hypocrisy and egoism, impelled by the force of desire and passion,

verses.sanskrit: कर्षयन्तः शरीरस्थं भूतग्राममचेतसः।मां चैवान्तःशरीरस्थं तान्विद्ध्यासुरनिश्चयान्।।17.6।।
verses.translation: senseless, tormenting the group of elements in the body — and Me who dwell within the body — know them to be of demoniac resolve.

verses.sanskrit: आहारस्त्वपि सर्वस्य त्रिविधो भवति प्रियः।यज्ञस्तपस्तथा दानं तेषां भेदमिमं श्रृणु।।17.7।।
verses.translation: The food dear to each is also of three kinds, and so too sacrifice, austerity, and charity. Hear their distinction.

verses.sanskrit: आयुःसत्त्वबलारोग्यसुखप्रीतिविवर्धनाः।रस्याः स्निग्धाः स्थिरा हृद्या आहाराः सात्त्विकप्रियाः।।17.8।।
verses.translation: Foods that promote life, vitality, strength, health, happiness, and cheerfulness; that are savory, smooth, firm, and pleasing to the heart — these are dear to the sattvic.

verses.sanskrit: कट्वम्ललवणात्युष्णतीक्ष्णरूक्षविदाहिनः।आहारा राजसस्येष्टा दुःखशोकामयप्रदाः।।17.9।।
verses.translation: Foods that are bitter, sour, salty, excessively hot, pungent, dry, and burning, causing pain, grief, and sickness — are dear to the rajasic.

verses.sanskrit: यातयामं गतरसं पूति पर्युषितं च यत्।उच्छिष्टमपि चामेध्यं भोजनं तामसप्रियम्।।17.10।।
verses.translation: Food that is stale, insipid, putrid, decayed, refuse, and impure is dear to the tamasic.

verses.sanskrit: अफलाकाङ्क्षिभिर्यज्ञो विधिदृष्टो य इज्यते।यष्टव्यमेवेति मनः समाधाय स सात्त्विकः।।17.11।।
verses.translation: The sacrifice which is offered according to rule by those who do not desire its fruit, convinced that 'this is a duty to be performed' — that is sattvic.

verses.sanskrit: अभिसंधाय तु फलं दम्भार्थमपि चैव यत्।इज्यते भरतश्रेष्ठ तं यज्ञं विद्धि राजसम्।।17.12।।
verses.translation: But the sacrifice that is offered seeking its fruit, or for ostentation, O best of the Bharatas — know that to be rajasic.

verses.sanskrit: विधिहीनमसृष्टान्नं मन्त्रहीनमदक्षिणम्।श्रद्धाविरहितं यज्ञं तामसं परिचक्षते।।17.13।।
verses.translation: A sacrifice which is contrary to the ordinance, in which no food is distributed, without sacred verses, without gifts, devoid of faith — they call tamasic.

verses.sanskrit: देवद्विजगुरुप्राज्ञपूजनं शौचमार्जवम्।ब्रह्मचर्यमहिंसा च शारीरं तप उच्यते।।17.14।।
verses.translation: Worship of the gods, the twice-born, teachers, and the wise; purity, uprightness, celibacy, and non-injury — these are called the austerity of the body.

verses.sanskrit: अनुद्वेगकरं वाक्यं सत्यं प्रियहितं च यत्।स्वाध्यायाभ्यसनं चैव वाङ्मयं तप उच्यते।।17.15।।
verses.translation: Speech that causes no pain, truthful, pleasing and beneficial, and the regular recitation of the Vedas — these are called the austerity of speech.

verses.sanskrit: मनःप्रसादः सौम्यत्वं मौनमात्मविनिग्रहः।भावसंशुद्धिरित्येतत्तपो मानसमुच्यते।।17.16।।
verses.translation: Serenity of mind, gentleness, silence, self-control, and purity of disposition — this is called the austerity of the mind.

verses.sanskrit: श्रद्धया परया तप्तं तपस्तत्ित्रविधं नरैः।अफलाकाङ्क्षिभिर्युक्तैः सात्त्विकं परिचक्षते।।17.17।।
verses.translation: This threefold austerity, practiced with supreme faith by men who are yoked and do not desire fruit — they call sattvic.

verses.sanskrit: सत्कारमानपूजार्थं तपो दम्भेन चैव यत्।क्रियते तदिह प्रोक्तं राजसं चलमध्रुवम्।।17.18।।
verses.translation: The austerity that is practiced with hypocrisy for the sake of respect, honor, and reverence — is said to be rajasic, unstable and fleeting.

verses.sanskrit: मूढग्राहेणात्मनो यत्पीडया क्रियते तपः।परस्योत्सादनार्थं वा तत्तामसमुदाहृतम्।।17.19।।
verses.translation: The austerity that is performed out of foolish notion, with torture of oneself, or for the ruin of another — is called tamasic.

verses.sanskrit: दातव्यमिति यद्दानं दीयतेऽनुपकारिणे।देशे काले च पात्रे च तद्दानं सात्त्विकं स्मृतम्।।17.20।।
verses.translation: That gift given with the thought 'it must be given,' to one who cannot repay, at the proper place, time, and to a worthy person — that gift is held to be sattvic.

verses.sanskrit: यत्तु प्रत्युपकारार्थं फलमुद्दिश्य वा पुनः।दीयते च परिक्लिष्टं तद्दानं राजसं स्मृतम्।।17.21।।
verses.translation: That gift given grudgingly, or with the desire for a return, or for the sake of its fruit — is held to be rajasic.

verses.sanskrit: अदेशकाले यद्दानमपात्रेभ्यश्च दीयते।असत्कृतमवज्ञातं तत्तामसमुदाहृतम्।।17.22।।
verses.translation: The gift given at a wrong place and time, to unworthy persons, without respect, with contempt — is declared to be tamasic.

verses.sanskrit: तत्सदिति निर्देशो ब्रह्मणस्त्रिविधः स्मृतः।ब्राह्मणास्तेन वेदाश्च यज्ञाश्च विहिताः पुरा।।17.23।।
verses.translation: Om Tat Sat — this is declared to be the threefold designation of Brahman. By this, of old, were the brahmanas, the Vedas, and the sacrifices ordained.

verses.sanskrit: तस्मादोमित्युदाहृत्य यज्ञदानतपःक्रियाः।प्रवर्तन्ते विधानोक्ताः सततं ब्रह्मवादिनाम्।।17.24।।
verses.translation: Therefore, with 'Om' as the opening, acts of sacrifice, gift, and austerity as enjoined by the scriptures are always begun by the knowers of Brahman.

verses.sanskrit: तदित्यनभिसन्धाय फलं यज्ञतपःक्रियाः।दानक्रियाश्च विविधाः क्रियन्ते मोक्षकाङ्क्षि।।17.25।।
verses.translation: With 'Tat' uttered, without aim at fruit, the various acts of sacrifice and austerity and the acts of gift are performed by the seekers of liberation.

verses.sanskrit: सद्भावे साधुभावे च सदित्येतत्प्रयुज्यते।प्रशस्ते कर्मणि तथा सच्छब्दः पार्थ युज्यते।।17.26।।
verses.translation: The word 'Sat' is used in the sense of reality and in the sense of goodness; and in an auspicious act also, O Partha, the word 'Sat' is used.

verses.sanskrit: यज्ञे तपसि दाने च स्थितिः सदिति चोच्यते।कर्म चैव तदर्थीयं सदित्येवाभिधीयते।।17.27।।
verses.translation: Steadiness in sacrifice, in austerity, and in gift is also called 'Sat'; and action for the sake of That is indeed called 'Sat.'

verses.sanskrit: अश्रद्धया हुतं दत्तं तपस्तप्तं कृतं च यत्।असदित्युच्यते पार्थ न च तत्प्रेत्य नो इह।।17.28।।
verses.translation: Whatever is sacrificed, given, or performed, and whatever austerity is practiced without faith — it is called 'asat,' O Partha; it is nothing here, and nothing in the hereafter.

chapters.chapter_title_english: The Yoga of Liberation through Renunciation

verses.sanskrit:
अर्जुन उवाच

संन्यासस्य महाबाहो तत्त्वमिच्छामि वेदितुम्।

त्यागस्य च हृषीकेश पृथक्केशिनिषूदन।।18.1।।
verses.translation:
Arjuna said:
O mighty-armed one, I wish to know the truth of sannyasa (renunciation), O Hrishikesha, and of tyaga (relinquishment), O Keshinisudana, each separately.

verses.sanskrit:
श्री भगवानुवाच

काम्यानां कर्मणां न्यासं संन्यासं कवयो विदुः।

सर्वकर्मफलत्यागं प्राहुस्त्यागं विचक्षणाः।।18.2।।
verses.translation:
The Blessed Lord said:
The sages know sannyasa to be the giving up of desire-prompted actions; the wise call tyaga the relinquishment of the fruit of all actions.

verses.sanskrit:
त्याज्यं दोषवदित्येके कर्म प्राहुर्मनीषिणः।

यज्ञदानतपःकर्म न त्याज्यमिति चापरे।।18.3।।
verses.translation: Some thinkers say action should be abandoned as evil; others, that acts of sacrifice, gift, and austerity should not be abandoned.

verses.sanskrit: निश्चयं श्रृणु मे तत्र त्यागे भरतसत्तम।त्यागो हि पुरुषव्याघ्र त्रिविधः संप्रकीर्तितः।।18.4।।
verses.translation: Hear My decision on this matter of tyaga, O best of the Bharatas. Tyaga has been declared to be of three kinds, O tiger among men.

verses.sanskrit: यज्ञदानतपःकर्म न त्याज्यं कार्यमेव तत्।यज्ञो दानं तपश्चैव पावनानि मनीषिणाम्।।18.5।।
verses.translation: Acts of sacrifice, gift, and austerity are not to be abandoned, but are to be performed; for sacrifice, gift, and austerity are purifiers of the wise.

verses.sanskrit: एतान्यपि तु कर्माणि सङ्गं त्यक्त्वा फलानि च।कर्तव्यानीति मे पार्थ निश्िचतं मतमुत्तमम्।।18.6।।
verses.translation: But even these actions should be performed abandoning attachment and fruits — this, O Partha, is My final and decisive opinion.

verses.sanskrit: नियतस्य तु संन्यासः कर्मणो नोपपद्यते।मोहात्तस्य परित्यागस्तामसः परिकीर्तितः।।18.7।।
verses.translation: Verily the renunciation of obligatory action is not proper. Its relinquishment through delusion is declared to be tamasic.

verses.sanskrit: दुःखमित्येव यत्कर्म कायक्लेशभयात्त्यजेत्।स कृत्वा राजसं त्यागं नैव त्यागफलं लभेत्।।18.8।।
verses.translation: He who abandons action merely because it is painful, from fear of bodily trouble — performs rajasic relinquishment; he does not obtain its fruit.

verses.sanskrit: कार्यमित्येव यत्कर्म नियतं क्रियतेऽर्जुन।सङ्गं त्यक्त्वा फलं चैव स त्यागः सात्त्विको मतः।।18.9।।
verses.translation: But when obligatory action is performed, O Arjuna, abandoning attachment and fruit, because it ought to be done — that relinquishment is considered sattvic.

verses.sanskrit: न द्वेष्ट्यकुशलं कर्म कुशले नानुषज्जते।त्यागी सत्त्वसमाविष्टो मेधावी छिन्नसंशयः।।18.10।।
verses.translation: The relinquisher, endowed with sattva, of steady intellect, whose doubts are rent, neither hates disagreeable action nor is attached to agreeable action.

verses.sanskrit: न हि देहभृता शक्यं त्यक्तुं कर्माण्यशेषतः।यस्तु कर्मफलत्यागी स त्यागीत्यभिधीयते।।18.11।।
verses.translation: It is not possible for the embodied to renounce actions without residue. But he who relinquishes the fruit of action — he is called the relinquisher.

verses.sanskrit: अनिष्टमिष्टं मिश्रं च त्रिविधं कर्मणः फलम्।भवत्यत्यागिनां प्रेत्य न तु संन्यासिनां क्वचित्।।18.12।।
verses.translation: Undesired, desired, and mixed — threefold is the fruit of action that accrues after death to those who have not relinquished; but there is no such fruit for the sannyasins.

verses.sanskrit: पञ्चैतानि महाबाहो कारणानि निबोध मे।सांख्ये कृतान्ते प्रोक्तानि सिद्धये सर्वकर्मणाम्।।18.13।।
verses.translation: Learn from Me, O mighty-armed one, these five causes declared in the Sankhya-siddhanta for the accomplishment of all actions:

verses.sanskrit: अधिष्ठानं तथा कर्ता करणं च पृथग्विधम्।विविधाश्च पृथक्चेष्टा दैवं चैवात्र पञ्चमम्।।18.14।।
verses.translation: the body, the agent, the various instruments, the various and distinct activities, and divine providence as the fifth.

verses.sanskrit: शरीरवाङ्मनोभिर्यत्कर्म प्रारभते नरः।न्याय्यं वा विपरीतं वा पञ्चैते तस्य हेतवः।।18.15।।
verses.translation: Whatever action a man undertakes with his body, speech, or mind, whether right or wrong — these five are its causes.

verses.sanskrit: तत्रैवं सति कर्तारमात्मानं केवलं तु यः।पश्यत्यकृतबुद्धित्वान्न स पश्यति दुर्मतिः।।18.16।।
verses.translation: Therefore, he who, with an unformed intellect, looks upon the pure Self alone as the agent — that dull-witted one sees not.

verses.sanskrit: यस्य नाहंकृतो भावो बुद्धिर्यस्य न लिप्यते।हत्वापि स इमाँल्लोकान्न हन्ति न निबध्यते।।18.17।।
verses.translation: He whose state is free from egoism, whose intellect is not tainted — though he slay these people, he slays not, nor is he bound.

verses.sanskrit: ज्ञानं ज्ञेयं परिज्ञाता त्रिविधा कर्मचोदना।करणं कर्म कर्तेति त्रिविधः कर्मसंग्रहः।।18.18।।
verses.translation: The knower, the object of knowledge, and knowledge — this is the threefold impulse to action. The instrument, the action, and the agent — this is the threefold constituent of action.

verses.sanskrit: ज्ञानं कर्म च कर्ता च त्रिधैव गुणभेदतः।प्रोच्यते गुणसंख्याने यथावच्छृणु तान्यपि।।18.19।।
verses.translation: Knowledge, action, and agent are declared in the Sankhya doctrine to be of three kinds only, according to the distinction of the gunas. Hear them duly also.

verses.sanskrit: सर्वभूतेषु येनैकं भावमव्ययमीक्षते।अविभक्तं विभक्तेषु तज्ज्ञानं विद्धि सात्त्विकम्।।18.20।।
verses.translation: That knowledge by which one sees in all beings the one, imperishable Being, undivided in the divided — know that knowledge to be sattvic.

verses.sanskrit: पृथक्त्वेन तु यज्ज्ञानं नानाभावान्पृथग्विधान्।वेत्ति सर्वेषु भूतेषु तज्ज्ञानं विद्धि राजसम्।।18.21।।
verses.translation: The knowledge which sees in all beings, by reason of differentiation, various entities of distinct kinds — know that knowledge to be rajasic.

verses.sanskrit: यत्तु कृत्स्नवदेकस्मिन्कार्ये सक्तमहैतुकम्।अतत्त्वार्थवदल्पं च तत्तामसमुदाहृतम्।।18.22।।
verses.translation: But that which clings to one single effect as if it were the whole, without reason, not based on truth, and trivial — that is declared to be tamasic.

verses.sanskrit: नियतं सङ्गरहितमरागद्वेषतः कृतम्।अफलप्रेप्सुना कर्म यत्तत्सात्त्विकमुच्यते।।18.23।।
verses.translation: The obligatory action which is performed without attachment, without desire or hatred, by one who seeks no fruit — is declared to be sattvic.

verses.sanskrit: यत्तु कामेप्सुना कर्म साहङ्कारेण वा पुनः।क्रियते बहुलायासं तद्राजसमुदाहृतम्।।18.24।।
verses.translation: But the action which is performed with great effort by one who seeks his desires or impelled by egoism — is declared to be rajasic.

verses.sanskrit: अनुबन्धं क्षयं हिंसामनपेक्ष्य च पौरुषम्।मोहादारभ्यते कर्म यत्तत्तामसमुच्यते।।18.25।।
verses.translation: The action undertaken through delusion, without regard to consequence, loss, injury, or ability — is declared to be tamasic.

verses.sanskrit: मुक्तसङ्गोऽनहंवादी धृत्युत्साहसमन्वितः।सिद्ध्यसिद्ध्योर्निर्विकारः कर्ता सात्त्विक उच्यते।।18.26।।
verses.translation: Free from attachment, without speaking of 'I,' endowed with fortitude and vigor, unmoved in success and failure — the agent is called sattvic.

verses.sanskrit: रागी कर्मफलप्रेप्सुर्लुब्धो हिंसात्मकोऽशुचिः।हर्षशोकान्वितः कर्ता राजसः परिकीर्तितः।।18.27।।
verses.translation: Passionate, desiring the fruit of action, greedy, harmful by nature, impure, full of joy and sorrow — the agent is declared rajasic.

verses.sanskrit: अयुक्तः प्राकृतः स्तब्धः शठो नैष्कृतिकोऽलसः।विषादी दीर्घसूत्री च कर्ता तामस उच्यते।।18.28।।
verses.translation: Unyoked, vulgar, stubborn, deceitful, malicious, slothful, despondent, and procrastinating — the agent is called tamasic.

verses.sanskrit: बुद्धेर्भेदं धृतेश्चैव गुणतस्त्रिविधं श्रृणु।प्रोच्यमानमशेषेण पृथक्त्वेन धनञ्जय।।18.29।।
verses.translation: Now hear, O Dhananjaya, the threefold division of the intellect and of steadiness according to the gunas — being declared separately and fully.

verses.sanskrit: प्रवृत्तिं च निवृत्तिं च कार्याकार्ये भयाभये।बन्धं मोक्षं च या वेत्ति बुद्धिः सा पार्थ सात्त्विकी।।18.30।।
verses.translation: The intellect which knows action and abstention from action, what ought to be done and what ought not, fear and fearlessness, bondage and liberation, O Partha — that is sattvic.

verses.sanskrit: यया धर्ममधर्मं च कार्यं चाकार्यमेव च।अयथावत्प्रजानाति बुद्धिः सा पार्थ राजसी।।18.31।।
verses.translation: The intellect by which one incorrectly understands dharma and adharma, what ought to be done and what ought not — that, O Partha, is rajasic.

verses.sanskrit: अधर्मं धर्ममिति या मन्यते तमसाऽऽवृता।सर्वार्थान्विपरीतांश्च बुद्धिः सा पार्थ तामसी।।18.32।।
verses.translation: The intellect which, enveloped in darkness, conceives adharma as dharma, and sees all things inverted — that, O Partha, is tamasic.

verses.sanskrit: धृत्या यया धारयते मनःप्राणेन्द्रियक्रियाः।योगेनाव्यभिचारिण्या धृतिः सा पार्थ सात्त्विकी।।18.33।।
verses.translation: The unswerving steadiness by which, through yoga, one restrains the functions of the mind, life-breaths, and senses — that steadiness, O Partha, is sattvic.

verses.sanskrit: यया तु धर्मकामार्थान् धृत्या धारयतेऽर्जुन।प्रसङ्गेन फलाकाङ्क्षी धृतिः सा पार्थ राजसी।।18.34।।
verses.translation: But the steadiness, O Arjuna, by which one holds fast to dharma, pleasure, and wealth, with attachment, craving the fruit — that steadiness is rajasic.

verses.sanskrit: यया स्वप्नं भयं शोकं विषादं मदमेव च।न विमुञ्चति दुर्मेधा धृतिः सा पार्थ तामसी।।18.35।।
verses.translation: That by which a fool does not give up sleep, fear, grief, depression, and conceit — that steadiness, O Partha, is tamasic.

verses.sanskrit: सुखं त्विदानीं त्रिविधं श्रृणु मे भरतर्षभ।अभ्यासाद्रमते यत्र दुःखान्तं च निगच्छति।।18.36।।
verses.translation: Now hear from Me, O bull of the Bharatas, the three kinds of happiness in which one rejoices through long practice, and comes to the end of pain.

verses.sanskrit: यत्तदग्रे विषमिव परिणामेऽमृतोपमम्।तत्सुखं सात्त्विकं प्रोक्तमात्मबुद्धिप्रसादजम्।।18.37।।
verses.translation: That which is like poison at first but like nectar at the end, born of the clear insight of the Self — that happiness is declared sattvic.

verses.sanskrit: विषयेन्द्रियसंयोगाद्यत्तदग्रेऽमृतोपमम्।परिणामे विषमिव तत्सुखं राजसं स्मृतम्।।18.38।।
verses.translation: That which at first, through the contact of the senses with their objects, is like nectar, but in the end like poison — that happiness is held to be rajasic.

verses.sanskrit: यदग्रे चानुबन्धे च सुखं मोहनमात्मनः।निद्रालस्यप्रमादोत्थं तत्तामसमुदाहृतम्।।18.39।।
verses.translation: That happiness which, both at the beginning and afterwards, deludes the self, arising from sleep, indolence, and heedlessness — is declared tamasic.

verses.sanskrit: न तदस्ति पृथिव्यां वा दिवि देवेषु वा पुनः।सत्त्वं प्रकृतिजैर्मुक्तं यदेभिः स्यात्ित्रभिर्गुणैः।।18.40।।
verses.translation: There is no being on earth, nor again in heaven among the gods, that is free from these three gunas born of Prakriti.

verses.sanskrit: ब्राह्मणक्षत्रियविशां शूद्राणां च परंतप।कर्माणि प्रविभक्तानि स्वभावप्रभवैर्गुणैः।।18.41।।
verses.translation: The duties of brahmanas, kshatriyas, vaishyas, and shudras, O scorcher of foes, are distributed according to the gunas born of their own nature.

verses.sanskrit: शमो दमस्तपः शौचं क्षान्तिरार्जवमेव च।ज्ञानं विज्ञानमास्तिक्यं ब्रह्मकर्म स्वभावजम्।।18.42।।
verses.translation: Calmness, self-restraint, austerity, purity, forbearance, uprightness, knowledge, realization, faith in the next world — these are the duties of the brahmana, born of his nature.

verses.sanskrit: शौर्यं तेजो धृतिर्दाक्ष्यं युद्धे चाप्यपलायनम्।दानमीश्वरभावश्च क्षात्रं कर्म स्वभावजम्।।18.43।।
verses.translation: Valor, splendor, fortitude, skill, not fleeing in battle, generosity, and lordliness — these are the duties of the kshatriya, born of his nature.

verses.sanskrit: कृषिगौरक्ष्यवाणिज्यं वैश्यकर्म स्वभावजम्।परिचर्यात्मकं कर्म शूद्रस्यापि स्वभावजम्।।18.44।।
verses.translation: Agriculture, cattle-rearing, and trade are the duties of the vaishya, born of his nature. Action consisting of service is the duty of the shudra, born of his nature.

verses.sanskrit: स्वे स्वे कर्मण्यभिरतः संसिद्धिं लभते नरः।स्वकर्मनिरतः सिद्धिं यथा विन्दति तच्छृणु।।18.45।।
verses.translation: Engaged each in his own duty, man attains perfection. How one engaged in his own duty attains perfection — hear that.

verses.sanskrit: यतः प्रवृत्तिर्भूतानां येन सर्वमिदं ततम्।स्वकर्मणा तमभ्यर्च्य सिद्धिं विन्दति मानवः।।18.46।।
verses.translation: From whom the activity of all beings proceeds, by whom all this is pervaded — worshipping Him by his own duty, man attains perfection.

verses.sanskrit: श्रेयान्स्वधर्मो विगुणः परधर्मात्स्वनुष्ठितात्।स्वभावनियतं कर्म कुर्वन्नाप्नोति किल्बिषम्।।18.47।।
verses.translation: Better is one's own dharma, though imperfect, than another's dharma well performed. Doing the action ordained by his own nature, one incurs no sin.

verses.sanskrit: सहजं कर्म कौन्तेय सदोषमपि न त्यजेत्।सर्वारम्भा हि दोषेण धूमेनाग्निरिवावृताः।।18.48।।
verses.translation: One should not abandon the duty to which one is born, O son of Kunti, though it be faulty; for all undertakings are enveloped by fault, as fire by smoke.

verses.sanskrit: असक्तबुद्धिः सर्वत्र जितात्मा विगतस्पृहः।नैष्कर्म्यसिद्धिं परमां संन्यासेनाधिगच्छति।।18.49।।
verses.translation: With intellect unattached everywhere, the self subdued, desire gone, one attains through renunciation the supreme perfection of freedom from action.

verses.sanskrit: सिद्धिं प्राप्तो यथा ब्रह्म तथाप्नोति निबोध मे।समासेनैव कौन्तेय निष्ठा ज्ञानस्य या परा।।18.50।।
verses.translation: How one who has attained perfection reaches Brahman — that supreme consummation of knowledge — learn from Me, O son of Kunti, only in brief.

verses.sanskrit: बुद्ध्या विशुद्धया युक्तो धृत्याऽऽत्मानं नियम्य च।शब्दादीन् विषयांस्त्यक्त्वा रागद्वेषौ व्युदस्य च।।18.51।।
verses.translation: Endowed with a pure intellect, restraining the self by firmness, giving up sound and other objects, and casting aside attraction and aversion;

verses.sanskrit: विविक्तसेवी लघ्वाशी यतवाक्कायमानसः।ध्यानयोगपरो नित्यं वैराग्यं समुपाश्रितः।।18.52।।
verses.translation: dwelling in solitude, eating lightly, controlling body, speech, and mind, ever intent on the yoga of meditation, taking refuge in dispassion;

verses.sanskrit: अहङ्कारं बलं दर्पं कामं क्रोधं परिग्रहम्।विमुच्य निर्ममः शान्तो ब्रह्मभूयाय कल्पते।।18.53।।
verses.translation: abandoning egoism, force, arrogance, desire, anger, and possession; free from the sense of 'mine,' at peace — he becomes fit for becoming Brahman.

verses.sanskrit: ब्रह्मभूतः प्रसन्नात्मा न शोचति न काङ्क्षति।समः सर्वेषु भूतेषु मद्भक्तिं लभते पराम्।।18.54।।
verses.translation: Having become Brahman, serene in self, he neither grieves nor desires. The same to all beings, he attains supreme devotion to Me.

verses.sanskrit: भक्त्या मामभिजानाति यावान्यश्चास्मि तत्त्वतः।ततो मां तत्त्वतो ज्ञात्वा विशते तदनन्तरम्।।18.55।।
verses.translation: By devotion he comes to know Me, how great I am and who I am in truth; then, having known Me in truth, he enters into Me at once.

verses.sanskrit: सर्वकर्माण्यपि सदा कुर्वाणो मद्व्यपाश्रयः।मत्प्रसादादवाप्नोति शाश्वतं पदमव्ययम्।।18.56।।
verses.translation: Performing all actions, ever taking refuge in Me, by My grace he attains the eternal, imperishable abode.

verses.sanskrit: चेतसा सर्वकर्माणि मयि संन्यस्य मत्परः।बुद्धियोगमुपाश्रित्य मच्चित्तः सततं भव।।18.57।।
verses.translation: Mentally renouncing all actions in Me, intent on Me as the highest goal, resorting to the yoga of the intellect, fix your mind ever on Me.

verses.sanskrit: मच्चित्तः सर्वदुर्गाणि मत्प्रसादात्तरिष्यसि।अथ चेत्त्वमहङ्कारान्न श्रोष्यसि विनङ्क्ष्यसि।।18.58।।
verses.translation: With your mind on Me, you shall, by My grace, cross over all obstacles. But if, through egoism, you will not listen, you shall perish.

verses.sanskrit: यदहङ्कारमाश्रित्य न योत्स्य इति मन्यसे।मिथ्यैष व्यवसायस्ते प्रकृतिस्त्वां नियोक्ष्यति।।18.59।।
verses.translation: If, clinging to egoism, you think 'I will not fight' — this resolve of yours is vain; Prakriti itself will constrain you.

verses.sanskrit: स्वभावजेन कौन्तेय निबद्धः स्वेन कर्मणा।कर्तुं नेच्छसि यन्मोहात्करिष्यस्यवशोऽपि तत्।।18.60।।
verses.translation: Bound, O son of Kunti, by your own karma born of your nature, even what through delusion you wish not to do, you shall helplessly do.

verses.sanskrit: ईश्वरः सर्वभूतानां हृद्देशेऽर्जुन तिष्ठति।भ्रामयन्सर्वभूतानि यन्त्रारूढानि मायया।।18.61।।
verses.translation: The Lord dwells in the hearts of all beings, O Arjuna, causing all beings to revolve as if mounted on a machine, by His maya.

verses.sanskrit: तमेव शरणं गच्छ सर्वभावेन भारत।तत्प्रसादात्परां शान्तिं स्थानं प्राप्स्यसि शाश्वतम्।।18.62।।
verses.translation: Take refuge in Him alone with all your being, O Bharata. By His grace you shall attain supreme peace and the eternal abode.

verses.sanskrit: इति ते ज्ञानमाख्यातं गुह्याद्गुह्यतरं मया।विमृश्यैतदशेषेण यथेच्छसि तथा कुरु।।18.63।।
verses.translation: Thus has wisdom, more secret than any secret, been declared to you by Me. Reflecting on this fully, then do as you wish.

verses.sanskrit: सर्वगुह्यतमं भूयः श्रृणु मे परमं वचः।इष्टोऽसि मे दृढमिति ततो वक्ष्यामि ते हितम्।।18.64।।
verses.translation: Hear again My supreme word, the most secret of all. You are dearly beloved of Me; therefore I shall speak for your welfare.

verses.sanskrit: मन्मना भव मद्भक्तो मद्याजी मां नमस्कुरु।मामेवैष्यसि सत्यं ते प्रतिजाने प्रियोऽसि मे।।18.65।।
verses.translation: Fix your mind on Me, be devoted to Me, sacrifice to Me, bow down to Me. You shall come to Me alone; truly do I promise you — you are dear to Me.

verses.sanskrit: सर्वधर्मान्परित्यज्य मामेकं शरणं व्रज।अहं त्वा सर्वपापेभ्यो मोक्षयिष्यामि मा शुचः।।18.66।।
verses.translation: Abandoning all dharmas, take refuge in Me alone. I shall liberate you from all sins. Do not grieve.

verses.sanskrit: इदं ते नातपस्काय नाभक्ताय कदाचन।न चाशुश्रूषवे वाच्यं न च मां योऽभ्यसूयति।।18.67।।
verses.translation: This is never to be told by you to one without austerity, nor to one without devotion, nor to one who does not wish to hear, nor to one who speaks ill of Me.

verses.sanskrit: य इमं परमं गुह्यं मद्भक्तेष्वभिधास्यति।भक्ितं मयि परां कृत्वा मामेवैष्यत्यसंशयः।।18.68।।
verses.translation: He who shall teach this supreme secret to My devotees, performing supreme devotion to Me, shall without doubt come to Me.

verses.sanskrit: न च तस्मान्मनुष्येषु कश्िचन्मे प्रियकृत्तमः।भविता न च मे तस्मादन्यः प्रियतरो भुवि।।18.69।।
verses.translation: And among men, none shall do dearer service to Me than he; nor shall any on earth be more beloved by Me.

verses.sanskrit: अध्येष्यते च य इमं धर्म्यं संवादमावयोः।ज्ञानयज्ञेन तेनाहमिष्टः स्यामिति मे मतिः।।18.70।।
verses.translation: And he who shall study this righteous dialogue between us — by him I shall be worshipped through the sacrifice of knowledge; such is My conviction.

verses.sanskrit: श्रद्धावाननसूयश्च श्रृणुयादपि यो नरः।सोऽपि मुक्तः शुभाँल्लोकान्प्राप्नुयात्पुण्यकर्मणाम्।।18.71।।
verses.translation: Even the man who, full of faith and without ill-will, should but hear it — he too, liberated, shall attain the auspicious worlds of those of meritorious deeds.

verses.sanskrit: कच्चिदेतच्छ्रुतं पार्थ त्वयैकाग्रेण चेतसा।कच्चिदज्ञानसंमोहः प्रनष्टस्ते धनञ्जय।।18.72।।
verses.translation: Has this been heard by you, O Partha, with a one-pointed mind? Has your delusion born of ignorance been destroyed, O Dhananjaya?

verses.sanskrit: अर्जुन उवाचनष्टो मोहः स्मृतिर्लब्धा त्वत्प्रसादान्मयाच्युत।स्थितोऽस्मि गतसन्देहः करिष्ये वचनं तव।।18.73।।
verses.translation:
Arjuna said:
My delusion is destroyed; memory is regained by Your grace, O Achyuta. I stand free from doubt. I shall do Your word.

verses.sanskrit: सञ्जय उवाचइत्यहं वासुदेवस्य पार्थस्य च महात्मनः।संवादमिममश्रौषमद्भुतं रोमहर्षणम्।।18.74।।
verses.translation:
Sanjaya said:
Thus I heard this dialogue between Vasudeva (Krishna) and the noble-souled Partha — wonderful and making the hair stand on end.

verses.sanskrit: व्यासप्रसादाच्छ्रुतवानेतद्गुह्यमहं परम्।योगं योगेश्वरात्कृष्णात्साक्षात्कथयतः स्वयम्।।18.75।।
verses.translation: By the grace of Vyasa, I have heard this supreme secret of yoga directly from Krishna, the Lord of yoga, who declared it in person.

verses.sanskrit: राजन्संस्मृत्य संस्मृत्य संवादमिममद्भुतम्।केशवार्जुनयोः पुण्यं हृष्यामि च मुहुर्मुहुः।।18.76।।
verses.translation: O king, as I recall again and again this wondrous and holy dialogue between Keshava and Arjuna, I rejoice again and again.

verses.sanskrit:
तच्च संस्मृत्य संस्मृत्य रूपमत्यद्भुतं हरेः।

विस्मयो मे महान् राजन् हृष्यामि च पुनः पुनः।।18.77।।
verses.translation: And as I recall again and again that most wondrous form of Hari, great is my wonder, O king, and I rejoice again and again.

verses.sanskrit:
यत्र योगेश्वरः कृष्णो यत्र पार्थो धनुर्धरः।

तत्र श्रीर्विजयो भूतिर्ध्रुवा नीतिर्मतिर्मम।।18.78।
verses.translation: Wherever is Krishna, the Lord of yoga, wherever is Partha, the wielder of the bow — there, I am convinced, are fortune, victory, prosperity, and sound policy.
